 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Worker Pool                                   ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Concurrency-capped fan-out primitive for the opt-in multi-executor        ║
     5	// ║ ("fan-out") layer above the single-executor deep-loop. Generalizes the    ║
     6	// ║ council parallel dispatcher (lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs) by      ║
     7	// ║ adding a concurrency cap so N executor lineages run with at most K in      ║
     8	// ║ flight, plus a status-ledger writer mirroring the proven packet-122       ║
     9	// ║ orchestration-status.log.                                                  ║
    10	// ║                                                                           ║
    11	// ║ Design: pure pool primitive (worker is INJECTED) + ledger helpers, fully  ║
    12	// ║ unit-tested. The real spawn worker and the CLI entry that wires it        ║
    13	// ║ (arg parse, JSON-out, exit codes) live in fanout-run.cjs.                ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    19	// 1. IMPORTS
    20	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21	
    22	const fs = require('node:fs');
    23	const path = require('node:path');
    24	
    25	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	// 2. HELPERS
    27	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	
    29	function isRecord(value) {
    30	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    31	}
    32	
    33	function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
    34	  if (value instanceof Date) {
    35	    return value.toISOString();
    36	  }
    37	  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
    38	    return value;
    39	  }
    40	  return new Date().toISOString();
    41	}
    42	
    43	function normalizeConcurrency(value) {
    44	  const n = Number(value);
    45	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 1) {
    46	    return 1;
    47	  }
    48	  return Math.floor(n);
    49	}
    50	
    51	function labelFor(item, index) {
    52	  if (isRecord(item) && typeof item.label === 'string' && item.label.trim() !== '') {
    53	    return item.label;
    54	  }
    55	  return `item-${index}`;
    56	}
    57	
    58	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59	// 3. CORE LOGIC
    60	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * Run a single pool item through the worker and capture its result or error.
    64	 *
    65	 * Never throws: a worker rejection is captured as a `rejected` result so one
    66	 * failing lineage cannot sink the whole pool (mirrors council `settleSeat`).
    67	 *
    68	 * @param {Object} params - Settlement parameters.
    69	 * @param {*} params.item - The work item (e.g. a lineage descriptor).
    70	 * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based item index (preserves order).
    71	 * @param {Function} params.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
    72	 * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO timestamp.
    73	 * @param {Function} [params.onEvent] - Optional callback for ledger events.
    74	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with label, status, timing, output|error.
    75	 */
    76	async function settleItem({ item, index, worker, now, onEvent }) {
    77	  const label = labelFor(item, index);
    78	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    79	  const startedAtMs = Date.now();
    80	
    81	  if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    82	    onEvent({ event: 'started', label, index, at: startedAtIso });
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  try {
    86	    const output = await worker(item, { index });
    87	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    88	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
    89	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    90	      onEvent({ event: 'completed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs });
    91	    }
    92	    return {
    93	      label,
    94	      status: 'fulfilled',
    95	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
    96	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
    97	      duration_ms: durationMs,
    98	      output,
    99	    };
   100	  } catch (error) {
   101	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   102	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
   103	    const normalizedError = {
   104	      name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
   105	      message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
   106	    };
   107	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   108	      onEvent({ event: 'failed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs, error: normalizedError });
   109	    }
   110	    return {
   111	      label,
   112	      status: 'rejected',
   113	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   114	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   115	      duration_ms: durationMs,
   116	      error: normalizedError,
   117	    };
   118	  }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * Run items through a worker with at most `concurrency` in flight.
   123	 *
   124	 * This is the fan-out generalization of council `dispatchCouncilSeats`: same
   125	 * never-throws per-item settlement and ordered results, plus a concurrency cap
   126	 * so a large lineage set runs K-at-a-time (the proven `xargs -P K` shape).
   127	 *
   128	 * @param {Object} options - Pool configuration.
   129	 * @param {Array} options.items - Work items (each ideally has a `label`).
   130	 * @param {number} options.concurrency - Max items in flight (clamped to >= 1).
   131	 * @param {Function} options.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
   132	 * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date/ISO timestamp.
   133	 * @param {Function} [options.onEvent] - Optional ledger-event callback.
   134	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} { results: [...ordered], summary: { total,
   135	 *   succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   136	 * @throws {TypeError} If items is not an array or worker is not a function.
   137	 */
   138	function runCappedPool(options) {
   139	  if (!isRecord(options)) {
   140	    throw new TypeError('runCappedPool options must be an object');
   141	  }
   142	  const { items, worker } = options;
   143	  if (!Array.isArray(items)) {
   144	    throw new TypeError('items must be an array');
   145	  }
   146	  if (typeof worker !== 'function') {
   147	    throw new TypeError('worker must be a function');
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  const concurrency = normalizeConcurrency(options.concurrency);
   151	  const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
   152	  const onEvent = typeof options.onEvent === 'function' ? options.onEvent : undefined;
   153	  const results = new Array(items.length);
   154	
   155	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   156	    if (items.length === 0) {
   157	      resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   158	      return;
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    let nextIndex = 0;
   162	    let active = 0;
   163	    let resolved = false;
   164	
   165	    const pump = () => {
   166	      if (resolved) {
   167	        return;
   168	      }
   169	      if (nextIndex >= items.length && active === 0) {
   170	        resolved = true;
   171	        resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   172	        return;
   173	      }
   174	      while (active < concurrency && nextIndex < items.length) {
   175	        const index = nextIndex;
   176	        nextIndex += 1;
   177	        active += 1;
   178	        settleItem({ item: items[index], index, worker, now, onEvent })
   179	          .then((result) => {
   180	            results[index] = result;
   181	          })
   182	          .catch((error) => {
   183	            // settleItem never throws, but stay defensive so the pump never wedges.
   184	            results[index] = {
   185	              label: labelFor(items[index], index),
   186	              status: 'rejected',
   187	              error: { name: 'PoolError', message: String(error) },
   188	            };
   189	          })
   190	          .finally(() => {
   191	            active -= 1;
   192	            pump();
   193	          });
   194	      }
   195	    };
   196	
   197	    pump();
   198	  });
   199	}
   200	
   201	/**
   202	 * Build the ordered-results + summary envelope for a completed pool run.
   203	 *
   204	 * @param {Array<Object>} results - Per-item settlement results (ordered).
   205	 * @returns {Object} { results, summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   206	 */
   207	function buildPoolSummary(results) {
   208	  const total = results.length;
   209	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   210	  const failed = total - succeeded;
   211	  return {
   212	    results,
   213	    summary: {
   214	      total,
   215	      succeeded,
   216	      failed,
   217	      all_failed: total > 0 && failed === total,
   218	    },
   219	  };
   220	}
   221	
   222	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   223	// 4. STATUS LEDGER
   224	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   225	
   226	/**
   227	 * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
   228	 *
   229	 * Generalizes the packet-122 `orchestration-status.log`: one line per lineage
   230	 * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).
   231	 *
   232	 * @param {string} ledgerPath - Path to the JSONL ledger file.
   233	 * @param {Object} entry - A serializable event object.
   234	 */
   235	function appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, entry) {
   236	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(ledgerPath), { recursive: true });
   237	  fs.appendFileSync(ledgerPath, `${JSON.stringify(entry)}\n`);
   238	}
   239	
   240	/**
   241	 * Write the orchestration summary JSON for a completed fan-out run.
   242	 *
   243	 * @param {string} summaryPath - Path to the summary JSON file.
   244	 * @param {Object} summary - Summary payload (e.g. the pool summary plus
   245	 *   salvage counts and per-lineage rollups).
   246	 */
   247	function writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, summary) {
   248	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(summaryPath), { recursive: true });
   249	  fs.writeFileSync(summaryPath, `${JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)}\n`);
   250	}
   251	
   252	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   253	// 5. EXPORTS
   254	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   255	
   256	module.exports = {
   257	  runCappedPool,
   258	  settleItem,
   259	  buildPoolSummary,
   260	  appendStatusLedger,
   261	  writeOrchestrationSummary,
   262	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	
     3	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗

    12	'use strict';

    14	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    36	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    39	
    40	const TSX_LOADER = path.resolve(

    44	  'system-spec-kit',

    56	    {
    57	      cwd: process.cwd(),
    58	      env: { ...process.env, DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED: '1' },

    60	      encoding: 'utf8',

    64	  if (child.stderr) process.stderr.write(child.stderr);
    65	  process.exit(child.status === null ? 1 : child.status);
    66	}
    67	
    68	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	// 3. HELPERS
    70	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    71	
    72	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    73	  const args = {};
    74	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    75	    const token = argv[i];
    76	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    77	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    78	    }
    79	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    80	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    81	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    82	      args[key] = true;
    83	    } else {
    84	      args[key] = next;
    85	      i += 1;
    86	    }
    87	  }
    88	  return args;
    89	}
    90	
    91	function inputError(message) {
    92	  const err = new Error(message);
    93	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    94	  return err;
    95	}
    96	
    97	function ensureString(args, key) {
    98	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    99	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
   100	  }
   101	  return args[key];
   102	}
   103	
   104	function jsonOut(payload) {
   105	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
   106	}
   107	
   108	// Per-kind first state-dir env var (used for lockfile isolation across same-kind replicas).
   109	const SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND = {
   110	  'cli-codex': 'SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR',
   111	  'cli-gemini': 'SPECKIT_GEMINI_STATE_DIR',
   112	  'cli-claude-code': 'SPECKIT_CLAUDE_CODE_STATE_DIR',
   113	  'cli-opencode': 'SPECKIT_OPENCODE_STATE_DIR',
   114	  'cli-devin': 'SPECKIT_DEVIN_STATE_DIR',
   115	};
   116	
   117	/**
   118	 * Build the "run the full loop" prompt text for a CLI lineage subprocess.
   119	 * The subprocess reads this, loads the skill, and runs the loop to convergence
   120	 * with config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir overriding step_resolve_artifact_root.
   121	 */
   122	function buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage) {
   123	  const skillFile =
   124	    loopType === 'review'
   125	      ? '.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md'
   126	      : '.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md';
   127	  const agentName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review' : 'deep-research';
   128	  return [
   129	    `You are a ${agentName} agent running a fan-out lineage.`,
   130	    ``,
   131	    `Read ${skillFile} and execute the ${loopType} loop with these parameters:`,
   132	    `  spec_folder: ${specFolder}`,
   133	    `  config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: ${lineageDir}`,
   134	    `  session_id: ${sessionId}`,
   135	    `  executor: ${lineage.kind} model=${lineage.model || 'default'}`,
   136	    `  loop_type: ${loopType}`,
   137	    ``,
   138	    `The step_resolve_artifact_root step will bind artifact_dir to ${lineageDir} via the`,
   139	    `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir override — do NOT run the resolveArtifactRoot node`,
   140	    `command; bind artifact_dir directly to the override value.`,
   141	    ``,
   142	    `Run phase_init, phase_main_loop (to convergence), and phase_synthesis.`,
   143	    `Write all outputs to ${lineageDir}. Do not touch any path outside ${lineageDir}.`,
   144	    ``,
   145	    `When complete, output a single line: FANOUT_LINEAGE_COMPLETE:${lineage.label}`,
   146	  ].join('\n');
   147	}
   148	
   149	/**
   150	 * Compute a generous timeout for a full lineage loop run.
   151	 * A single iteration is timeoutSeconds; a full loop is up to iterations * timeoutSeconds.
   152	 * We double it for safety and cap at 4 hours.
   153	 */
   154	function computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage) {
   155	  const iters = lineage.iterations || 12;
   156	  const perIterSecs = lineage.timeoutSeconds || 900;
   157	  return Math.min(iters * perIterSecs * 2 * 1000, 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Build the subprocess command for a CLI lineage.
   162	 * Returns { command, args, input?, env } where input is stdin content when applicable.
   163	 */
   164	function buildLineageCommand(lineage, prompt, resolvedSandbox, resolvedPermission) {
   165	  const kind = lineage.kind;
   166	
   167	  if (kind === 'cli-codex') {
   168	    return {
   169	      command: 'codex',
   170	      args: [
   171	        'exec',
   172	        '--model',
   173	        lineage.model || 'o4-mini',
   174	        '-c',
   175	        `model_reasoning_effort=${lineage.reasoningEffort || 'medium'}`,
   176	        '-c',
   177	        `service_tier=${lineage.serviceTier || 'default'}`,
   178	        '-c',
   179	        'approval_policy=never',
   180	        '--sandbox',
   181	        resolvedSandbox,
   182	        '-',
   183	      ],
   184	      input: prompt,
   185	    };
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  if (kind === 'cli-gemini') {
   189	    return {
   190	      command: 'gemini',
   191	      args: [prompt, '-m', lineage.model || 'gemini-2.5-pro', '-s', resolvedSandbox, '-y', '-o', 'text'],
   192	    };
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  if (kind === 'cli-claude-code') {
   196	    const args = [
   197	      '-p',
   198	      prompt,
   199	      '--model',
   200	      lineage.model || 'claude-opus-4-8',
   201	      '--permission-mode',
   202	      resolvedPermission,
   203	      '--output-format',
   204	      'text',
   205	    ];
   206	    if (lineage.reasoningEffort) {
   207	      args.push('--effort', lineage.reasoningEffort);
   208	    }
   209	    return { command: 'claude', args };
   210	  }
   211	
   212	  if (kind === 'cli-opencode') {
   213	    const args = [
   214	      'run',
   215	      '--model',
   216	      lineage.model || 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-8',
   217	      '--agent',
   218	      'general',
   219	      '--format',
   220	      'json',
   221	      '--dangerously-skip-permissions',
   222	      '--pure',
   223	    ];
   224	    if (lineage.reasoningEffort) {
   225	      args.push('--variant', lineage.reasoningEffort);
   226	    }
   227	    args.push(prompt);
   228	    return { command: 'opencode', args, input: '' };
   229	  }
   230	
   231	  if (kind === 'cli-devin') {
   232	    return {
   233	      command: 'devin',
   234	      args: [
   235	        '--print',
   236	        '--model',
   237	        lineage.model || 'swe-1.6',
   238	        '--permission-mode',
   239	        resolvedPermission,
   240	      ],
   241	      input: prompt,
   242	    };
   243	  }
   244	
   245	  throw inputError(`Unknown CLI executor kind: ${kind}`);
   246	}
   247	
   248	const {
   249	  runSalvageSweep,
   250	  extractTextFromOpencodeJson,
   251	} = require('./fanout-salvage.cjs');
   252	
   253	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254	// 4. CORE LOGIC
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	
   257	async function main() {
   258	  const args = parseArgs();
   259	  const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
   260	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   261	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   262	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   263	  }
   264	  const fanoutConfigJson = ensureString(args, 'fanoutConfigJson');
   265	  const baseArtifactDir = ensureString(args, 'baseArtifactDir');
   266	
   267	  const {
   268	    parseFanoutConfig,
   269	    expandLineages,
   270	    resolveCodexSandboxMode,
   271	    resolveClaudePermissionMode,
   272	    resolveGeminiSandboxMode,
   273	    resolveDevinPermissionMode,
   274	  } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts');
   275	
   276	  installSignalHandlers(() => {});
   277	  maybeThrowTestFault();
   278	
   279	  let rawConfig;
   280	  try {
   281	    rawConfig = JSON.parse(fanoutConfigJson);
   282	  } catch {
   283	    throw inputError('fanoutConfigJson is not valid JSON');
   284	  }
   285	
   286	  const fanoutConfig = parseFanoutConfig(rawConfig);
   287	  const allLineages = expandLineages(fanoutConfig);
   288	
   289	  // Fan-out pool handles CLI lineages only; native lineages are dispatched by the YAML step.
   290	  const cliLineages = allLineages.filter((l) => l.kind !== 'native');
   291	
   292	  if (cliLineages.length === 0) {
   293	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no CLI lineages to spawn', results: [], summary: { total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false } });
   294	    return;
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  const lineagesDir = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'lineages');
   298	  const ledgerPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-status.log');
   299	  const summaryPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   300	  const runId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
   301	
   302	  fs.mkdirSync(lineagesDir, { recursive: true });
   303	
   304	  const { results, summary } = await runCappedPool({
   305	    items: cliLineages,
   306	    concurrency: fanoutConfig.concurrency,
   307	    onEvent: (event) => appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, event),
   308	    worker: async (lineage) => {
   309	      const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, lineage.label);
   310	      const stateDir = path.join(lineageDir, '.executor-state');
   311	      fs.mkdirSync(lineageDir, { recursive: true });
   312	      fs.mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });
   313	
   314	      const sessionId = `fanout-${lineage.label}-${runId}`;
   315	      const prompt = buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage);
   316	
   317	      const resolvedSandbox = resolveCodexSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   318	      const resolvedPermission = resolveClaudePermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   319	      const resolvedGeminiSandbox = resolveGeminiSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   320	      const resolvedDevinPermission = resolveDevinPermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   321	
   322	      const effectiveSandbox = lineage.kind === 'cli-gemini' ? resolvedGeminiSandbox : resolvedSandbox;
   323	      const effectivePermission = lineage.kind === 'cli-devin' ? resolvedDevinPermission : resolvedPermission;
   324	
   325	      const { command, args: cmdArgs, input } = buildLineageCommand(
   326	        lineage,
   327	        prompt,
   328	        effectiveSandbox,
   329	        effectivePermission,
   330	      );
   331	
   332	      // Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles.
   333	      const stateEnvKey = SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[lineage.kind];
   334	      const extraEnv = {
   335	        SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID: lineage.label,
   336	        ...(stateEnvKey ? { [stateEnvKey]: stateDir } : {}),
   337	      };
   338	
   339	      const timeoutMs = computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage);
   340	
   341	      const result = spawnSync(command, cmdArgs, {
   342	        cwd: process.cwd(),
   343	        encoding: 'utf8',
   344	        timeout: timeoutMs,
   345	        env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv },
   346	        maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024,
   347	        ...(typeof input === 'string' ? { input } : {}),
   348	      });
   349	
   350	      // Save subprocess stdout for salvage sweep (write failures in weak CLI executors).
   351	      const logsDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'logs');
   352	      fs.mkdirSync(logsDir, { recursive: true });
   353	      const savedStdout = typeof result.stdout === 'string' ? result.stdout : '';
   354	      fs.writeFileSync(path.join(logsDir, 'fanout-lineage.out'), savedStdout, 'utf8');
   355	
   356	      // Recover missing iteration files from captured stdout when possible.
   357	      const salvage = runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout);
   358	
   359	      const exitCode = result.status ?? (result.error ? 1 : 0);
   360	      return { label: lineage.label, exitCode, timedOut: result.signal === 'SIGTERM', salvage };
   361	    },
   362	  });
   363	
   364	  writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, {
   365	    run_id: runId,
   366	    loop_type: loopType,
   367	    spec_folder: specFolder,
   368	    base_artifact_dir: baseArtifactDir,
   369	    total_cli_lineages: cliLineages.length,
   370	    ...summary,
   371	  });
   372	
   373	  const exitCode = summary.all_failed ? 3 : summary.failed > 0 ? 2 : 0;
   374	  jsonOut({ status: exitCode === 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial', run_id: runId, results, summary });
   375	  process.exit(exitCode);
   376	}
   377	
   378	main().catch((err) => {
   379	  const code = classifyExitCode(err);
   380	  jsonOut({
   381	    status: 'error',
   382	    error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
   383	    code: err && err.code ? err.code : 'SCRIPT_ERROR',
   384	  });
   385	  if (code === 1) {
   386	    process.stderr.write(
   387	      JSON.stringify({ error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err), stack: err && err.stack }) + '\n',
   388	    );
   389	  }
   390	  process.exit(code);
   391	});

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Config
     2	
     3	import { z } from '../../../system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules/zod/index.js';
     4	
     5	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
     6	
     7	export const EXECUTOR_KINDS = ['native', 'cli-codex', 'cli-gemini', 'cli-claude-code', 'cli-opencode', 'cli-devin'] as const;
     8	export type ExecutorKind = typeof EXECUTOR_KINDS[number];
     9	
    10	export const REASONING_EFFORTS = ['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh'] as const;
    11	export type ReasoningEffort = typeof REASONING_EFFORTS[number];
    12	
    13	export const SERVICE_TIERS = ['priority', 'standard', 'fast'] as const;
    14	export type ServiceTier = typeof SERVICE_TIERS[number];
    15	
    16	const SANDBOX_MODES = ['read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'] as const;
    17	export type SandboxMode = typeof SANDBOX_MODES[number];
    18	export type GeminiSandboxMode = 'docker' | 'none';
    19	export type ClaudePermissionMode = 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions';
    20	
    21	// ───── CONSTANTS ─────
    22	
    23	export const executorConfigSchema = z.object({
    24	  kind: z.enum(EXECUTOR_KINDS).default('native'),
    25	  model: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
    26	  reasoningEffort: z.enum(REASONING_EFFORTS).nullable().default(null),
    27	  serviceTier: z.enum(SERVICE_TIERS).nullable().default(null),
    28	  sandboxMode: z.enum(SANDBOX_MODES).nullable().default(null),
    29	  timeoutSeconds: z.number().int().positive().default(900),
    30	});
    31	
    32	export type ExecutorConfig = z.infer<typeof executorConfigSchema>;
    33	
    34	export const EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT: Record<ExecutorKind, readonly (keyof ExecutorConfig)[]> = {
    35	  native: [],
    36	  'cli-codex': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'serviceTier', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    37	  'cli-gemini': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    38	  'cli-claude-code': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    39	  'cli-opencode': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    40	  'cli-devin': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    41	};
    42	
    43	export const GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['gemini-3.1-pro-preview'] as const;
    44	export type GeminiSupportedModel = typeof GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    45	
    46	export const DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['swe-1.6', 'deepseek-v4', 'glm-5.1', 'kimi-k2.6'] as const;
    47	export type DevinSupportedModel = typeof DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    48	export type DevinPermissionMode = 'auto' | 'dangerous';
    49	
    50	// ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
    51	
    52	type ExecutorConfigIssue = {
    53	  path: PropertyKey[];
    54	  message: string;
    55	};
    56	
    57	export class ExecutorNotWiredError extends Error {
    58	  kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>;
    59	
    60	  constructor(kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>) {
    61	    super(`Executor kind '${kind}' is reserved in the schema but not yet wired. Awaiting future spec for ${kind} integration.`);
    62	    this.name = 'ExecutorNotWiredError';
    63	    this.kind = kind;
    64	  }
    65	}
    66	
    67	export class ExecutorConfigError extends Error {
    68	  issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[];
    69	
    70	  constructor(input: { issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[] }) {
    71	    super(input.issues.map((issue) => `${issue.path.map(String).join('.') || '<root>'}: ${issue.message}`).join('; '));
    72	    this.name = 'ExecutorConfigError';
    73	    this.issues = input.issues;
    74	  }
    75	}
    76	
    77	// ───── HELPERS ─────
    78	
    79	function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
    80	  return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
    81	}
    82	
    83	function normalizeSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
    84	  return mode ?? 'workspace-write';
    85	}
    86	
    87	function normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw: unknown): unknown {
    88	  if (!isRecord(raw) || !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'type')) {
    89	    return raw;
    90	  }
    91	
    92	  const legacyKind = raw.type;
    93	  if (typeof legacyKind !== 'string') {
    94	    return raw;
    95	  }
    96	
    97	  if (typeof raw.kind === 'string' && raw.kind !== legacyKind) {
    98	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
    99	      issues: [{
   100	        path: ['type'],
   101	        message: `deprecated executor field 'type' conflicts with canonical kind '${raw.kind}'`,
   102	      }],
   103	    });
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  console.warn("[executor-config] Deprecated executor field 'type' was provided; use 'kind' instead.");
   107	  const { type: _legacyType, ...rest } = raw;
   108	  return {
   109	    ...rest,
   110	    kind: typeof raw.kind === 'string' ? raw.kind : legacyKind,
   111	  };
   112	}
   113	
   114	function normalizeIssues(error: z.ZodError<ExecutorConfig>): ExecutorConfigIssue[] {
   115	  return error.issues.map((issue) => ({
   116	    path: issue.path,
   117	    message: issue.message,
   118	  }));
   119	}
   120	
   121	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Codex CLI sandbox mode.
   125	 *
   126	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   127	 * @returns Codex-compatible sandbox mode.
   128	 */
   129	export function resolveCodexSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
   130	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode);
   131	}
   132	
   133	/**
   134	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Gemini CLI sandbox mode.
   135	 *
   136	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   137	 * @returns 'docker' for workspace-write/read-only, 'none' for danger-full-access.
   138	 */
   139	export function resolveGeminiSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): GeminiSandboxMode {
   140	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'none' : 'docker';
   141	}
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Claude Code permission mode.
   145	 *
   146	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   147	 * @returns Claude-compatible permission mode.
   148	 */
   149	export function resolveClaudePermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): ClaudePermissionMode {
   150	  switch (normalizeSandboxMode(mode)) {
   151	    case 'read-only':
   152	      return 'plan';
   153	    case 'danger-full-access':
   154	      return 'bypassPermissions';
   155	    default:
   156	      return 'acceptEdits';
   157	  }
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Devin CLI permission mode.
   162	 *
   163	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   164	 * @returns 'auto' for workspace-write/read-only, 'dangerous' for danger-full-access.
   165	 */
   166	export function resolveDevinPermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): DevinPermissionMode {
   167	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'dangerous' : 'auto';
   168	}
   169	
   170	/**
   171	 * Parse and validate a raw executor configuration.
   172	 *
   173	 * Normalizes legacy `type` fields, validates against the Zod schema,
   174	 * and enforces executor-kind-specific field support.
   175	 *
   176	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   177	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
   178	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   179	 */
   180	export function parseExecutorConfig(raw: unknown): ExecutorConfig {
   181	  const parsed = executorConfigSchema.safeParse(normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw));
   182	  if (!parsed.success) {
   183	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   184	      issues: normalizeIssues(parsed.error),
   185	    });
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  const config = parsed.data;
   189	  if (config.kind === 'cli-codex' && config.model === null) {
   190	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   191	      issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-codex' }],
   192	    });
   193	  }
   194	  if (config.kind === 'cli-devin' && config.model === null) {
   195	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   196	      issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-devin' }],
   197	    });
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  const supportedFlags = EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT[config.kind];
   201	  const unsupportedFields: string[] = [];
   202	  const allOptionalFields: (keyof ExecutorConfig)[] = [
   203	    'model',
   204	    'reasoningEffort',
   205	    'serviceTier',
   206	    'sandboxMode',
   207	    'timeoutSeconds',
   208	  ];
   209	
   210	  for (const field of allOptionalFields) {
   211	    if (!supportedFlags.includes(field) && config[field] !== null && !(field === 'timeoutSeconds' && config[field] === 900)) {
   212	      unsupportedFields.push(field);
   213	    }
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  if (unsupportedFields.length > 0) {
   217	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   218	      issues: unsupportedFields.map((field) => ({
   219	        path: [field],
   220	        message: `field '${field}' is not supported by executor kind '${config.kind}'. Supported fields for ${config.kind}: ${
   221	          supportedFlags.length ? supportedFlags.join(', ') : 'none'
   222	        }.`,
   223	      })),
   224	    });
   225	  }
   226	
   227	  if (
   228	    config.kind === 'cli-gemini' &&
   229	    config.model !== null &&
   230	    !GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS.some((model) => model === config.model)
   231	  ) {
   232	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   233	      issues: [
   234	        {
   235	          path: ['model'],
   236	          message: `model '${config.model}' is not a supported cli-gemini model. Supported: ${GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS.join(', ')}.`,
   237	        },
   238	      ],
   239	    });
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  if (
   243	    config.kind === 'cli-devin' &&
   244	    config.model !== null &&
   245	    !DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS.some((model) => model === config.model)
   246	  ) {
   247	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   248	      issues: [
   249	        {
   250	          path: ['model'],
   251	          message: `model '${config.model}' is not a supported cli-devin model. Supported: ${DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS.join(', ')}.`,
   252	        },
   253	      ],
   254	    });
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  return config;
   258	}
   259	
   260	/**
   261	 * Resolve an executor configuration by merging CLI and file sources.
   262	 *
   263	 * @param sources - CLI and file partial configs (CLI takes precedence).
   264	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
   265	 */
   266	export function resolveExecutorConfig(sources: {
   267	  cli?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
   268	  file?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
   269	}): ExecutorConfig {
   270	  const merged = {
   271	    ...(sources.file ?? {}),
   272	    ...(sources.cli ?? {}),
   273	  };
   274	  return parseExecutorConfig(merged);
   275	}
   276	
   277	// ───── FAN-OUT (MULTI-EXECUTOR) CONFIG ─────
   278	//
   279	// Opt-in layer ABOVE the single-executor path. When a fan-out config is present,
   280	// the loop runs N executor "lineages" concurrently (capped), each running the
   281	// existing sequential loop in its own isolated sub-packet. The single-executor
   282	// path (executorConfigSchema / parseExecutorConfig) is untouched and remains the
   283	// default; callers use EITHER a single `executor` OR a `fanout` block, never both.
   284	
   285	const LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/;
   286	
   287	/**
   288	 * One fan-out lineage: a single executor config plus fan-out metadata.
   289	 *
   290	 * - `label`: directory-safe id for the lineage's isolated sub-packet.
   291	 * - `count`: number of replicas of THIS config to run (e.g. 5x the same model).
   292	 * - `iterations`: per-lineage max-iterations override; null = packet default.
   293	 */
   294	export const lineageExecutorSchema = executorConfigSchema.extend({
   295	  label: z.string().min(1).regex(LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN, {
   296	    message: "label must match /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/ (lowercase, digits, hyphens; dir-safe)",
   297	  }),
   298	  count: z.number().int().positive().default(1),
   299	  iterations: z.number().int().positive().nullable().default(null),
   300	});
   301	
   302	export type LineageExecutor = z.infer<typeof lineageExecutorSchema>;
   303	
   304	export const fanoutConfigSchema = z.object({
   305	  executors: z.array(lineageExecutorSchema).min(1),
   306	  concurrency: z.number().int().positive().default(2),
   307	});
   308	
   309	export type FanoutConfig = z.infer<typeof fanoutConfigSchema>;
   310	
   311	/**
   312	 * Parse and validate a raw fan-out configuration.
   313	 *
   314	 * Each entry's executor subset is routed through {@link parseExecutorConfig} so
   315	 * ALL existing kind/model/flag rules (cli-codex needs model, gemini/devin model
   316	 * whitelists, per-kind flag support) apply per lineage with zero duplication.
   317	 * Labels must be unique and base-label (count>1) expansion must not collide.
   318	 *
   319	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   320	 * @returns Validated FanoutConfig.
   321	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   322	 */
   323	export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {
   324	  const parsed = fanoutConfigSchema.safeParse(raw);
   325	  if (!parsed.success) {
   326	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
   327	  }
   328	
   329	  const config = parsed.data;
   330	
   331	  // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).
   332	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   333	    const { label: _label, count: _count, iterations: _iterations, ...executorSubset } = entry;
   334	    try {
   335	      parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);
   336	    } catch (err) {
   337	      if (err instanceof ExecutorConfigError) {
   338	        throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   339	          issues: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: ['executors', index, ...issue.path], message: issue.message })),
   340	        });
   341	      }
   342	      throw err;
   343	    }
   344	  });
   345	
   346	  // Labels must be unique among the declared lineages.
   347	  const seenLabels = new Set<string>();
   348	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   349	    if (seenLabels.has(entry.label)) {
   350	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   351	        issues: [{ path: ['executors', index, 'label'], message: `duplicate lineage label '${entry.label}'` }],
   352	      });
   353	    }
   354	    seenLabels.add(entry.label);
   355	  });
   356	
   357	  // Expanded labels (count>1 → label-1, label-2, …) must not collide either.
   358	  const expandedLabels = new Set<string>();
   359	  for (const lineage of expandLineages(config)) {
   360	    if (expandedLabels.has(lineage.label)) {
   361	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   362	        issues: [{ path: ['executors'], message: `expanded lineage label '${lineage.label}' collides; rename base labels` }],
   363	      });
   364	    }
   365	    expandedLabels.add(lineage.label);
   366	  }
   367	
   368	  return config;
   369	}
   370	
   371	/**
   372	 * Expand a fan-out config into concrete per-replica lineages.
   373	 *
   374	 * A `count` of 1 keeps the base label; `count > 1` yields `${label}-1` …
   375	 * `${label}-N`. Each expanded lineage carries `count: 1` so downstream
   376	 * consumers see one runnable unit per element.
   377	 *
   378	 * @param config - Validated FanoutConfig.
   379	 * @returns Flat list of single-replica lineages (one runnable unit each).
   380	 */
   381	export function expandLineages(config: FanoutConfig): LineageExecutor[] {
   382	  const lineages: LineageExecutor[] = [];
   383	  for (const entry of config.executors) {
   384	    if (entry.count === 1) {
   385	      lineages.push({ ...entry, count: 1 });
   386	      continue;
   387	    }
   388	    for (let replica = 1; replica <= entry.count; replica += 1) {
   389	      lineages.push({ ...entry, label: `${entry.label}-${replica}`, count: 1 });
   390	    }
   391	  }
   392	  return lineages;
   393	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Audit
     2	
     3	import { spawn, spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';

     9	
    10	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
    11	
    12	export const CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV = 'SPECKIT_CLI_DISPATCH_STACK' as const;
    13	
    14	export type DispatchFailureReason =
    15	  | 'timeout'
    16	  | 'crash'
    17	  | 'missing_output'
    18	  | 'invalid_output'
    19	  | 'other'
    20	  | RecursionGuardFailureReason;

    22	export type RecursionGuardLayer = 'stack' | 'ancestry' | 'env' | 'lockfile';
    23	export type RecursionGuardFailureReason =
    24	  | 'recursion-guard-stack'
    25	  | 'recursion-guard-ancestry'
    26	  | 'recursion-guard-env'
    27	  | 'recursion-guard-lockfile';
    28	
    29	export type ExecutorDispatchAllowedResult =
    30	  | { allowed: true }
    31	  | {
    32	      allowed: false;
    33	      reason: RecursionGuardFailureReason;
    34	      layer: RecursionGuardLayer;
    35	      detail: string;
    36	    };
    37	
    38	// ───── CONSTANTS ─────
    39	
    40	type ExecutorDispatchGuardContext = {
    41	  env?: Record<string, string | undefined>;
    42	  ancestryCmdlines?: string[];
    43	  statePaths?: string[];
    44	};
    45	
    46	const EXECUTOR_BINARY_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string>> = {
    47	  'cli-codex': 'codex',
    48	  'cli-gemini': 'gemini',
    49	  'cli-claude-code': 'claude',
    50	  'cli-opencode': 'opencode',
    51	  'cli-devin': 'devin',
    52	};
    53	
    54	const EXECUTOR_SESSION_ENV_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string>> = {
    55	  'cli-codex': 'CODEX_SESSION_ID',
    56	  'cli-gemini': 'GEMINI_SESSION_ID',
    57	  'cli-claude-code': 'CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID',
    58	  'cli-opencode': 'OPENCODE_SESSION_ID',
    59	  'cli-devin': 'DEVIN_SESSION_ID',
    60	};
    61	
    62	const EXECUTOR_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string[]>> = {
    63	  'cli-codex': ['SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR', 'CODEX_HOME'],
    64	  'cli-gemini': ['SPECKIT_GEMINI_STATE_DIR', 'GEMINI_HOME'],
    65	  'cli-claude-code': ['SPECKIT_CLAUDE_CODE_STATE_DIR', 'CLAUDE_CODE_HOME', 'CLAUDE_HOME'],
    66	  'cli-opencode': ['SPECKIT_OPENCODE_STATE_DIR', 'OPENCODE_HOME'],
    67	  'cli-devin': ['SPECKIT_DEVIN_STATE_DIR', 'DEVIN_HOME'],
    68	};
    69	
    70	const EXECUTOR_DEFAULT_HOME_DIR_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string>> = {
    71	  'cli-codex': '.codex',
    72	  'cli-gemini': '.gemini',
    73	  'cli-claude-code': '.claude',
    74	  'cli-opencode': '.opencode',
    75	  'cli-devin': '.devin',
    76	};
    77	
    78	const EXECUTOR_COMMON_ENV_ALLOWLIST = new Set([
    79	  'PATH',
    80	  'HOME',
    81	  'LANG',
    82	  'TMPDIR',
    83	  'NODE_PATH',
    84	  'TERM',
    85	]);
    86	
    87	const EXECUTOR_ENV_PREFIXES_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string[]>> = {

    89	  'cli-gemini': ['GEMINI_', 'GOOGLE_'],
    90	  'cli-claude-code': ['CLAUDE_', 'CLAUDE_CODE_', 'ANTHROPIC_'],
    91	  'cli-opencode': ['OPENCODE_'],
    92	  'cli-devin': ['DEVIN_'],
    93	};
    94	
    95	type RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput = {
    96	  command: string;
    97	  args: string[];
    98	  cwd: string;
    99	  timeoutSeconds: number;
   100	  stateLogPath: string;
   101	  executor: ExecutorConfig;
   102	  iteration: number;
   103	  input?: string;
   104	  guardContext?: ExecutorDispatchGuardContext;
   105	  timeoutGraceMs?: number;
   106	  lineageId?: string;
   107	};
   108	
   109	// ───── HELPERS ─────
   110	
   111	function getExecutorKind(config: ExecutorConfig): ExecutorKind {
   112	  return config.kind ?? (config as ExecutorConfig & { type?: ExecutorKind }).type ?? 'native';
   113	}
   114	
   115	function isAllowedExecutorEnvKey(key: string, kind: ExecutorKind): boolean {
   116	  if (EXECUTOR_COMMON_ENV_ALLOWLIST.has(key) || key.startsWith('LC_')) {
   117	    return true;
   118	  }
   119	  return (EXECUTOR_ENV_PREFIXES_BY_KIND[kind] ?? []).some((prefix) => key.startsWith(prefix));
   120	}
   121	
   122	function recursionReasonForLayer(layer: RecursionGuardLayer): RecursionGuardFailureReason {
   123	  return `recursion-guard-${layer}` as RecursionGuardFailureReason;
   124	}
   125	
   126	function splitDispatchStack(stack: string | undefined): string[] {
   127	  return (stack ?? '')
   128	    .split(':')
   129	    .map((entry) => entry.trim())
   130	    .filter((entry) => entry.length > 0);
   131	}
   132	
   133	function commandLineContainsBinary(commandLine: string, binary: string): boolean {
   134	  return commandLine
   135	    .split(/\0|\s+/)
   136	    .map((token) => token.trim().replace(/^['"]|['"]$/g, ''))
   137	    .filter((token) => token.length > 0)
   138	    .some((token) => basename(token) === binary);
   139	}
   140	
   141	function readLinuxAncestorCmdlines(startPid: number = process.ppid): string[] {
   142	  const cmdlines: string[] = [];
   143	  let currentPid = startPid;
   144	  const seen = new Set<number>();
   145	
   146	  while (currentPid > 1 && !seen.has(currentPid)) {
   147	    seen.add(currentPid);
   148	    const procDir = join('/proc', String(currentPid));
   149	    const cmdlinePath = join(procDir, 'cmdline');
   150	    const statPath = join(procDir, 'stat');
   151	
   152	    if (!existsSync(cmdlinePath) || !existsSync(statPath)) {
   153	      break;
   154	    }
   155	
   156	    try {
   157	      const cmdline = readFileSync(cmdlinePath, 'utf8').replace(/\0/g, ' ').trim();
   158	      if (cmdline.length > 0) {
   159	        cmdlines.push(cmdline);
   160	      }
   161	
   162	      const stat = readFileSync(statPath, 'utf8');
   163	      const closeParenIndex = stat.lastIndexOf(')');
   164	      const afterCommand = closeParenIndex === -1 ? '' : stat.slice(closeParenIndex + 2).trim();
   165	      const parentPid = Number(afterCommand.split(/\s+/)[1]);
   166	      if (!Number.isInteger(parentPid) || parentPid <= 0) {
   167	        break;
   168	      }
   169	      currentPid = parentPid;
   170	    } catch {
   171	      break;
   172	    }
   173	  }
   174	
   175	  return cmdlines;
   176	}
   177	
   178	function getDefaultStatePaths(kind: ExecutorKind, env: Record<string, string | undefined>): string[] {
   179	  const paths: string[] = [];
   180	  for (const envName of EXECUTOR_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[kind] ?? []) {
   181	    const value = env[envName];
   182	    if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
   183	      paths.push(value);
   184	    }
   185	  }
   186	
   187	  const home = env.HOME;
   188	  const homeDir = EXECUTOR_DEFAULT_HOME_DIR_BY_KIND[kind];
   189	  if (typeof home === 'string' && homeDir) {
   190	    paths.push(join(home, homeDir));
   191	  }
   192	
   193	  return [...new Set(paths)];
   194	}
   195	
   196	function candidateLockfileNames(kind: ExecutorKind): string[] {
   197	  const binary = EXECUTOR_BINARY_BY_KIND[kind] ?? kind;
   198	  return [
   199	    `${kind}.lock`,
   200	    `${binary}.lock`,
   201	    `speckit-${kind}.lock`,
   202	    `speckit-cli-dispatch-${kind}.lock`,
   203	  ];
   204	}
   205	
   206	function pathIsReadableDirectory(path: string): boolean {
   207	  try {
   208	    return statSync(path).isDirectory();
   209	  } catch {
   210	    return false;
   211	  }
   212	}
   213	
   214	function findLastNonEmptyLineIndex(lines: string[]): number {
   215	  for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
   216	    if (lines[index]?.trim() !== '') {
   217	      return index;
   218	    }
   219	  }
   220	
   221	  return -1;
   222	}
   223	
   224	function isObjectRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
   225	  return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
   226	}
   227	
   228	function readJsonlFile(stateLogPath: string): {
   229	  content: string;
   230	  hasTrailingNewline: boolean;
   231	  lines: string[];
   232	} {
   233	  const content = readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8');
   234	  return {
   235	    content,
   236	    hasTrailingNewline: content.endsWith('\n'),
   237	    lines: content.split(/\r?\n/),
   238	  };
   239	}
   240	
   241	function readJsonlFileAfterTailRepair(stateLogPath: string): ReturnType<typeof readJsonlFile> {
   242	  repairJsonlTail(stateLogPath);
   243	  return readJsonlFile(stateLogPath);
   244	}
   245	
   246	function rewriteJsonlFile(stateLogPath: string, lines: string[], hasTrailingNewline: boolean): void {
   247	  const rewritten = lines.join('\n');
   248	  writeFileSync(stateLogPath, hasTrailingNewline ? `${rewritten}\n` : rewritten, 'utf8');
   249	}
   250	
   251	function parseJsonlObjectLine(line: string): Record<string, unknown> | null {
   252	  try {
   253	    const parsedRecord = JSON.parse(line);
   254	    return isObjectRecord(parsedRecord) ? parsedRecord : null;
   255	  } catch {
   256	    return null;
   257	  }
   258	}
   259	
   260	function findLatestIterationRecordIndex(lines: string[], iteration: number): number {
   261	  for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
   262	    const line = lines[index]?.trim();
   263	    if (!line) {
   264	      continue;
   265	    }
   266	
   267	    const parsedRecord = parseJsonlObjectLine(line);
   268	    if (
   269	      parsedRecord &&
   270	      parsedRecord.iteration === iteration &&
   271	      (parsedRecord.type === 'iteration' || parsedRecord.type === 'iteration_start')
   272	    ) {
   273	      return index;
   274	    }
   275	  }
   276	
   277	  return -1;
   278	}
   279	
   280	function findLatestIterationEvent(lines: string[], iteration: number): Record<string, unknown> | null {
   281	  for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
   282	    const line = lines[index]?.trim();
   283	    if (!line) {
   284	      continue;
   285	    }
   286	
   287	    const parsedRecord = parseJsonlObjectLine(line);
   288	    if (
   289	      parsedRecord &&
   290	      parsedRecord.iteration === iteration &&
   291	      parsedRecord.type === 'event'
   292	    ) {
   293	      return parsedRecord;
   294	    }
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  return null;
   298	}
   299	
   300	function killProcessGroup(pid: number | undefined, signal: NodeJS.Signals): void {
   301	  if (typeof pid !== 'number') {
   302	    return;
   303	  }
   304	
   305	  try {
   306	    process.kill(process.platform === 'win32' ? pid : -pid, signal);
   307	  } catch {
   308	  }
   309	}
   310	
   311	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   312	
   313	/**
   314	 * Detect whether the same executor kind already appears in the dispatch stack.
   315	 *
   316	 * @param stack - The SPECKIT_CLI_DISPATCH_STACK env value.
   317	 * @param kind - The executor kind to check.
   318	 * @returns True if the kind is already present in the stack.
   319	 */
   320	export function detectSameKindFromStack(stack: string | undefined, kind: ExecutorKind): boolean {
   321	  if (kind === 'native') {
   322	    return false;
   323	  }
   324	
   325	  return splitDispatchStack(stack).includes(kind);
   326	}
   327	
   328	/**
   329	 * Detect whether an executor binary appears in the process ancestry.
   330	 *
   331	 * Reads Linux /proc ancestor cmdlines to find the executor binary.
   332	 *
   333	 * @param kind - The executor kind to check.
   334	 * @param ancestryCmdlines - Pre-computed ancestor command lines (optional).
   335	 * @returns True if the executor binary is found in the ancestry.
   336	 */
   337	export function detectFromAncestry(kind: ExecutorKind, ancestryCmdlines: string[] = readLinuxAncestorCmdlines()): boolean {
   338	  const binary = EXECUTOR_BINARY_BY_KIND[kind];
   339	  if (!binary) {
   340	    return false;
   341	  }
   342	
   343	  return ancestryCmdlines.some((commandLine) => commandLineContainsBinary(commandLine, binary));
   344	}
   345	
   346	/**
   347	 * Detect whether a runtime session environment variable is set for an executor.
   348	 *
   349	 * @param kind - The executor kind to check.
   350	 * @param env - Environment variable map (defaults to process.env).
   351	 * @returns True if the session environment variable is set.
   352	 */
   353	export function detectFromRuntimeEnv(
   354	  kind: ExecutorKind,
   355	  env: Record<string, string | undefined> = process.env,
   356	): boolean {
   357	  const sessionEnvName = EXECUTOR_SESSION_ENV_BY_KIND[kind];
   358	  if (!sessionEnvName) {
   359	    return false;
   360	  }

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:145:    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:148:      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:149:      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:150:      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:151:      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:152:      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:162:            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:796:            - "--dangerously-skip-permissions enables hands-off automation; sandboxMode='read-only' is not currently honored (no opencode equivalent)"

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:820:            elif grep -qE "(I cannot write|read-only mode|permission errors when attempting to write)" "$DEVIN_STDOUT_TMP" 2>/dev/null; then
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:827:          timeout_seconds: "{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:830:            resolved_permission_mode: |
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:831:              Resolve via resolveDevinPermissionMode(config.executor.sandboxMode) — defined in
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:832:              .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:833:              Mapping: sandboxMode='danger-full-access' → 'dangerous', everything else → 'auto'.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:834:              Default (sandboxMode unset): 'auto'. Research iterations are read-only by contract, so 'auto' is the expected resolution.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:840:              and permissions.allow/deny scope expressions (Read(/path/**), Exec(<cmd>)) at Devin's strict parser.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:847:            - "Supported models: swe-1.6 (default), deepseek-v4, glm-5.1, kimi-k2.6 (per DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS in executor-config.ts)"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:848:            - "--permission-mode auto auto-approves read-only ops and prompts on write; dangerous auto-approves all (only for sandboxed runs)"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:855:            - "v1.0.4.0: stderr captured to {state_paths.prompt_dir}/devin-stderr-iter-NNN.log and scanned for 'A tool was rejected by the user' (Devin runtime approval gate firing inside --permission-mode auto); ITER_OUTCOME=tool_rejected flagged so the post_dispatch_validate / reducer can skip the iter cleanly without inheriting a stuck-style failure"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:856:            - "v1.0.4.0: stdout captured to {state_paths.prompt_dir}/devin-stdout-iter-NNN.log and scanned for stdout-fallback boilerplate ('I cannot write', 'read-only mode', 'permission errors when attempting to write'); ITER_OUTCOME=degraded_stdout_fallback flagged so the JSONL row can be tagged dispatch_quality=degraded for the synthesis phase"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:879:            - "executor_missing — non-native executor did not record provenance"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:909:          bash: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --spec-folder \"{spec_folder}\" --loop-type \"research\" --session-id \"{config.lineage.sessionId}\" --nodes '{graph_upsert_nodes_json}' --edges '{graph_upsert_edges_json}'"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:931:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"iteration","run":{current_iteration},"mode":"research","status":"error","focus":"{next_focus}","findingsCount":0,"newInfoRatio":0.0,"keyQuestions":[],"answeredQuestions":[],"durationMs":0,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:961:      step_fanout_merge:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:962:        description: "Merge N lineage registries into one consolidated registry; skip when single-executor"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:963:        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:965:          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1024:          Preserve machine-owned markers so the reducer-owned registry, dashboard, and strategy anchors can be refreshed on re-run without mutating prior synthesis. (Note: `completed-continue` lineage branch is deferred — see `deep-research/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branch Contract`.)
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:33:  lineage_mode: "[auto|resume|restart] - Session lifecycle intent. Default: auto. The runtime only persists lineage events for `resume` (same sessionId, no archive) and `restart` (new sessionId, generation+1, prior review/ archived). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and no longer accepted — see deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:116:    description: "Resolve artifact directory — use fan-out lineage override when provided, else resolve from spec_folder"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:118:    branch_on: "config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:121:        artifact_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:122:        artifact_root_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:124:        artifact_archive_root: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}/_archive"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:133:  step_fanout_spawn:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:134:    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:135:    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:137:      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:138:      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:139:      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:140:      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:141:      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:143:      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:144:        description: "Spawn all CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:145:        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:147:          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:150:            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:151:            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:154:      step_fanout_spawn_native:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:155:        description: "Dispatch each native lineage as a sequential deep-review sub-agent"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:156:        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:157:        for_each: "lineage in expanded native entries of config.fanout"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:164:              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:165:              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:166:              executor: native
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:250:          log: "Auto-resume: state artifacts are complete and consistent. Continue the same lineage (sessionId unchanged, generation unchanged, no archive)."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:251:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"generation":{generation},"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:254:          action: "Archive the current review packet under the root-level review_archive/ tree and start a new lineage segment with a fresh sessionId and generation+1."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:257:            lineage_mode: "restart"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:260:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:262:        note_deferred_branches: "fork and completed-continue are deferred and no longer runtime-supported in this release. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are live. See deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches for the one-session contract."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:339:          lineageMode: "new"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:369:        action: "Initialize JSONL state log with config record including review fields and lineage metadata"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:373:        content: '{"type":"config","mode":"review","reviewTarget":"{review_target}","reviewTargetType":"{review_target_type}","reviewDimensions":{review_dimensions_json},"resource_map_present":{resource_map_present},"resource_map":{"emit":{resource_map_emit}},"sessionId":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","parentSessionId":null,"lineageMode":"new","generation":1,"continuedFromRun":null,"maxIterations":{max_iterations},"convergenceThreshold":{convergence_threshold},"createdAt":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","specFolder":"{spec_folder}"}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:378:        content: '{"sessionId":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","openFindings":[],"resolvedFindings":[],"repeatedFindings":[],"dimensionCoverage":{"correctness":false,"security":false,"traceability":false,"maintainability":false},"findingsBySeverity":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"openFindingsCount":0,"resolvedFindingsCount":0,"convergenceScore":0.0}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:448:          - security_sensitive_fix_scope: "Boolean true when the current review is a rerun after fixes involving security, path disclosure, auth/authz, sandboxing, env precedence, public schemas, persistence, or user-visible error payloads; derive from the latest review report, remediation notes, or planning packet when present, else false."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:470:          1. Call graph convergence on the current review lineage before the inline 3-signal vote.
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:529:               h) candidateCoverageGate: skip entirely when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 on the latest iteration. Otherwise pass when the current iteration's `reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass` is `trivial` with `enforcement: "skip"` AND `reviewDepthApplicability.evidenceRefs` is non-empty, OR when the reducer registry's `searchDebt` is empty AND `candidateCoverage.covered` is a superset of `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` for the lineage. Otherwise pass = false; record `searchDebt` items as blocked-stop evidence; blocker name = "candidateCoverageGate"; recovery_strategy = "Resolve deferred/blocked obligations in next iteration or downgrade scope explicitly to trivial with cited evidence."

.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:3:argument-hint: "<profile_path> [:auto|:confirm] [--spec-folder=PATH] [--scorer=pattern|5dim] [--grader=noop|mock|llm] [--iterations=N] [--executor=NAME --model=NAME] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:76:1. **Tier 1 - Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist a config record under `{spec_folder}/improvement/model-benchmark-config.json` (shape: `lane: "model-benchmark"`, `profilePath`, `specFolder`, `outputsDir`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `scoringMethod`, `grader`, optional `executor`, optional `model`, and when `grader = llm` also thread `executor`/`model` into `modelBenchmarkConfig.target_model` so `dispatch-model.cjs` reads them), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-model-benchmark-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:78:2. **Tier 2 - Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: none needed. `profile_path` absence is not ambiguity because it has a default, and `executor`/`model` are only required when `grader = llm`.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:96:  executor: cli-codex  # required only when grader = llm; one of cli-opencode | cli-claude-code | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-devin
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:97:  model: gpt-5.5  # required only when grader = llm; model id for the chosen executor
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:100:Rules: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2 (unspecified fields fall back to default, marker fields take precedence over $ARGUMENTS flags, unknown fields warn, malformed lines parse-error). When `grader != llm`, any `executor`/`model` marker fields are ignored with a warning.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:114:| `executor` | Conditional | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, required only when `grader = llm` | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:184:   │    Which executor and model? e.g. "cli-codex, gpt-5.5"         │
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:203:   - executor = [from Q5, only when grader = llm]
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:214:⛔ NEVER request executor/model when grader is not llm
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:229:- `executor = ________________` (only when grader = llm)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:249:| executor               | Conditional   | ______     | Q5 (only when grader=llm) |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:255:│   ├─ grader == llm AND (executor missing OR model missing)?
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:268:- **DO NOT** request executor/model unless `grader = llm`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:297:**Inputs:** `$ARGUMENTS` - optional profile path, optional mode suffix, optional spec folder, optional scorer/grader flags, optional executor+model (only with `--grader=llm`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:317:- **Threading executor/model when `grader = llm`:** the gathered `executor` and `model` values are NOT loop-host flags. Setup MUST persist them into `modelBenchmarkConfig.target_model` (executor + model) inside the runtime config that `dispatch-model.cjs` reads, so the dispatcher resolves the requested executor and model. Setup persists `executor` and `model` into the Lane B config record (see Step 3) and the config record threads `modelBenchmarkConfig.target_model`. Without that thread, `dispatch-model.cjs` falls back to its built-in defaults and the gathered values are silently dropped.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:348:| llm | `--grader llm` | Real model dispatch via `dispatch-model.cjs`, requires an executor + model |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:352:`dispatch-model.cjs` is the executor router. It is loaded only on the model-benchmark path and only when `grader = llm`. Supported executors: `cli-opencode`, `cli-claude-code`, `cli-codex`, `cli-gemini`, `cli-devin`.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:385:Persist the config record to `{spec_folder}/improvement/model-benchmark-config.json` (`lane`, `profilePath`, `specFolder`, `outputsDir`, `executionMode`, `scoringMethod`, `grader`, optional `executor`, optional `model`). When `grader = llm`, also thread the gathered `executor` and `model` into `modelBenchmarkConfig.target_model` (shape: `{ "modelBenchmarkConfig": { "target_model": { "executor": "<executor>", "model": "<model>" } } }`) so `dispatch-model.cjs` resolves the requested executor and model instead of its built-in defaults.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:446:/deep:start-model-benchmark-loop :auto --spec-folder=specs/121/008 --scorer=5dim --grader=llm --executor=cli-codex --model=gpt-5.5
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:448:Routes graded scoring through `dispatch-model.cjs` to the named executor and model.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-model-benchmark-loop.md:522:- Requested executor/model when `grader` is not `llm`

.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:3:argument-hint: "<topic> [:auto|:confirm] [--max-iterations=N] [--convergence=N] [--executor=<type> [--model=X] [--count=N] [--label=X] ...] [--executors=<json>] [--concurrency=N] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:60:1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{artifact_dir}/deep-research-config.json` (shape: `researchTopic`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:79:  executor: native  # native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin

.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:82:  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional service tier
