.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:98:| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:99:| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:100:| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:101:| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:102:| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:104:| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each `--executor` group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:105:| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:107:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-research` agent dispatches with isolated dirs. CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses concurrently in the capped pool.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:128:   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:129:   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:130:   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:131:   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:132:   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:134:   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:135:   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:136:   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:137:   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:140:   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:141:   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:143:   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:147:   - The generated `deep-research-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:150:   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:151:   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:152:   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:153:   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:154:   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:157:   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:158:   - Invalid combinations fail fast with clear errors, including `cli-codex` without `--model` and reserved-but-unwired executor kinds
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:168:   - Include Q-Exec only when `--executor` is NOT present and the topic text does NOT already mention executor hints such as `cli-codex`, `codex`, or `gpt-5.4`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:169:   - If Q-Exec is omitted and no executor is otherwise resolved, default to `native`
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:190:     D) cli-claude-code — `claude -p "PROMPT" --model X --permission-mode acceptEdits` with optional --effort. No service-tier.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:191:     E) cli-opencode — `opencode run --model X --agent general --format json --dangerously-skip-permissions --pure --dir {repo_root} [--variant Y] "PROMPT" </dev/null`. `reasoningEffort` maps to `--variant`. No service-tier.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:192:     F) cli-devin — `devin --print --prompt-file ... --model X --permission-mode auto`. Default model swe-1.6. No reasoning-effort or service-tier.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:209:   - executor config = [CLI flags, compact reply, config file, or default `native`; map compact reply fields to `config.executor.type/model/reasoningEffort/serviceTier`, and accept an optional volunteered convergence value before executor fields]
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:357:# Single-executor (default — unchanged)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:362:# Fan-out: two CLI lineages in parallel (cli-codex + cli-claude-code)

.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:380:> **Note on native fan-out:** `--executor=native --count=N` dispatches N sequential `@deep-research` sub-agents with isolated artifact dirs. Not pooled. Best for count 1–3. For higher parallelism, use CLI executor kinds.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:4:description: "Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:20:- executor configuration and audit metadata
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:24:- loop locking and permissions gates
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:35:- A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:56:  *"deep-loop runtime"*|*"deep-loop-runtime"*|*"executor config"*|*"prompt pack"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:77:lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:126:- `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:127:- `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:133:- `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:135:- `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:147:- `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:165:| `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:166:| `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:167:| `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:168:| `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:170:**Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:3:argument-hint: "<target> [:auto|:confirm] [--max-iterations=N] [--convergence=N] [--spec-folder=PATH] [--executor=<type> --count=N --label=X ...] [--executors=<json>] [--concurrency=N] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:58:1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-config.json` (shape: `reviewTarget`, `reviewTargetType`, `reviewDimensions`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:114:| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:116:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:216:     D) cli-claude-code — `claude -p "PROMPT" --model X --permission-mode acceptEdits` with optional --effort. No service-tier.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:217:     E) cli-opencode — `opencode run --model X --agent general --format json --dangerously-skip-permissions --pure --dir {repo_root} [--variant Y] "PROMPT" </dev/null`. `reasoningEffort` maps to `--variant`. No service-tier.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:218:     F) cli-devin — `devin --print --prompt-file ... --model X --permission-mode auto`. Default model swe-1.6. No reasoning-effort or service-tier.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:373:Runs seat executors in parallel for one council round; preserves seat result order; returns fulfilled or rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:418:This category covers 7 scenarios validating the opt-in multi-executor fan-out layer added in packet 124: config schema, pool primitive, CLI lineage driver, write-failure salvage, research merge, review strongest-restriction, and artifact-dir-override parity.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:423:Validates `parseFanoutConfig` + `expandLineages`: unique-label enforcement, collision detection, count expansion, per-entry kind validation reuse, and `lineageId` byte-identity when absent.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/references/state_format.md:175:When audited executor dispatch fails, the runtime can append a typed event to JSONL state. The event records iteration, executor kind, failure reason, exit status or signal when available, and provenance needed for later validation.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/references/script_interface_contract.md:131:`permissions-gate` is not called directly by the scripts. It is part of the pre-dispatch runtime safety layer for loop orchestration. Script execution is an `execute` operation that should be allowed only for runtime-owned script paths in any active permission matrix.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/references/script_interface_contract.md:135:- `lib/deep-loop/permissions-gate.ts` maps tools and bash commands to operation classes.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/references/script_interface_contract.md:162:| `lib/deep-loop/permissions-gate.ts` | Pre-dispatch execute/write boundary. |

Each carries a `MODULE:` header per the OpenCode TypeScript style guide.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:2:title: "Fan-out cross-lineage merge"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:3:description: "Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by findingId + cross-model attribution) or review (strongest-restriction: any active P0 → merged FAIL). Writes consolidated registry and fanout-attribution.md."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:6:# Fan-out cross-lineage merge
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:12:`fanout-merge.cjs` reads every `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/` sub-packet registry and
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:16:lineages are added to a `_lineages` attribution array), aggregates total
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:20:**Review merge (strongest-restriction)**: iterates active findings across all lineages,
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:26:Both modes write `{artifact_dir}/fanout-attribution.md`: a per-lineage Markdown table with
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:31:The merge step is what makes fan-out useful — without it, N lineages produce N separate
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:32:reports with no cross-lineage synthesis. The strongest-restriction policy ensures a P0
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:33:finding in ANY lineage surfaces to the final review verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:52:| `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | `mergeResearchRegistries`, `mergeReviewRegistries`, `buildAttributionMd`, `main()` (guarded behind `require.main === module`) |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/05-fanout-merge.md:58:| `tests/unit/fanout-merge.vitest.ts` | 10 tests: research dedup/attribution (3: dedup by id + cross-lineage tracking, iteration count aggregation, null registry handled); review strongest-restriction (5: clean+P0→FAIL, all clean→PASS, P1-only→CONDITIONAL, duplicate escalates severity, non-active excluded); script e2e (2: no lineages dir → ok, 2-lineage review fixture → merged FAIL + attribution.md) |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:2:title: "Fan-out CLI lineage driver"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:3:description: "CLI lineage pool driver: TSX-bootstrapped entry point that spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in an isolated lineages/{label}/ sub-packet, with per-kind state-dir isolation and a post-subprocess salvage sweep."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:6:# Fan-out CLI lineage driver
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:12:`fanout-run.cjs` bridges the pool primitive and the actual CLI executor subprocesses. It
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:13:parses the fanout config JSON, filters to CLI lineages (native lineages are handled by the
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:14:YAML `step_fanout_spawn_native` agent dispatch), creates `{base}/lineages/{label}/` and
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:15:`{label}/.executor-state/` directories per lineage, builds a "run the full loop" prompt,
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:19:variables to prevent same-kind replica lockfile collisions, runs via `spawnSync` with a
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:20:generous timeout, saves subprocess stdout to `{lineageDir}/logs/fanout-lineage.out`, then
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:21:calls `runSalvageSweep` from `fanout-salvage.cjs`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:25:Dispatches every CLI executor fan-out lineage. If this drifts, parallel research/review
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:34:`cli-gemini`, `cli-devin`. Per-lineage subprocess timeout = `min(iterations *
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:47:| `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | Main entry: CLI arg parsing, per-kind command construction, pool orchestration, stdout capture, salvage call |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/03-fanout-run.md:49:| `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | `runCappedPool` for concurrency cap + ledger |

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:3:description: "Shared peer-runtime infrastructure for deep-review and deep-research loops. Houses executor config, prompt-pack rendering, post-dispatch validation, atomic state, JSONL repair, loop locking, permissions gating, Bayesian scoring, fallback routing, coverage-graph schema and queries, four direct-invocation script entry points, runtime-owned SQLite storage and 27 vitest files."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:7:  - "executor config"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:33:Two skills, `deep-review` and `deep-research`, both run autonomous iteration loops. Both need atomic state-log writes. Both need single-writer locking. Both need a coverage graph that survives session crashes. Both need executor config parsing and Bayesian convergence scoring. Before this skill existed, all of that lived inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/` and got reached through four `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools. Two consumer skills depended on the internals of a third, and every workflow YAML call paid the marshalling cost of MCP dispatch plus JSON parse.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:52:| 🛡️ State safety | Atomic state writes, JSONL repair, single-writer locking, permissions gating | `lib/deep-loop/` (atomic-state, jsonl-repair, loop-lock, permissions-gate) |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:125:| `lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` | Schema and parsing for per-iteration executor settings | Both consumer skills share one config shape, so a CLI executor name or sandbox mode means the same thing in deep-review and deep-research |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:126:| `lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts` | Appends an `executor` provenance block to each iteration JSONL | Post-hoc reviewers can tell which model and CLI produced each iteration without scraping the prompt |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:141:| `lib/deep-loop/permissions-gate.ts` | Permission scope checks before mutating sensitive paths |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:147:Convergence detection plus fallback executor selection.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:152:| `lib/deep-loop/fallback-router.ts` | Executor fallback decision matrix when a primary executor times out or returns malformed output |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:174:| `lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` | Runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat order, returns fulfilled or rejected outcomes plus round summary counts |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:219:│   ├── deep-loop/                        # 10 TS modules (executor, prompt, state, scoring, routing)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:244:    └── helpers/spawn-cjs.ts              # Shared spawn helper
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:263:Defined by `lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`. Workflow YAMLs reference the schema through TS imports. The schema covers executor name, sandbox mode, permission scope, model selection and per-iteration timeout.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:268:import { parseExecutorConfig } from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.js';
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:271:  executor: 'cli-devin',
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:273:  sandboxMode: 'workspace-write',
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:274:  permissionScope: 'spec-folder-only',
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/README.md:285:Atomic-state semantics, loop-lock behavior, permissions-gate checks and Bayesian-scorer weights are not user-configurable through env vars. They are runtime invariants. Changing them requires a new packet with an ADR.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/04-fanout-salvage.md:12:After a CLI lineage subprocess exits, some executors (weak CLIs, sandboxed write
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/04-fanout-salvage.md:14:lineage state log to discover which iterations completed (`type == 'iteration'` records),
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/04-fanout-salvage.md:19:content is recoverable, writes a `fanout_salvage_failed` HTML comment placeholder.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/04-fanout-salvage.md:23:Proven mandatory from the packet-122 prototype: weak CLI executors intermittently fail to
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/04-fanout-salvage.md:24:write output files. Without salvage, those lineages contribute nothing to the final merge.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/04-fanout-salvage.md:30:Fully shipped in `fanout-salvage.cjs`. Pure CJS (no TSX bootstrap), exports only —

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:14:Validates the review-specific strongest-restriction merge logic in `fanout-merge.cjs`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:18:The strongest-restriction policy is what makes fan-out useful for review: if ANY lineage
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:19:found a P0, it must surface to the final verdict regardless of what other lineages found.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:20:Without this, a clean lineage could dilute a blocking lineage's P0 finding.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:26:- Objective: Confirm `mergeReviewRegistries` applies strongest-restriction: any active P0 across any lineage → `mergedVerdict=FAIL`; duplicate findingId escalates to highest severity; non-active findings excluded; all clean → PASS; P1-only → CONDITIONAL.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:39:- `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` present.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:43:1. Inspect `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` `mergeReviewRegistries` — confirm `SEVERITY_RANK` map (`{P0:3, P1:2, P2:1}`), `status !== 'active'` guard, `mergedVerdict` derivation formula.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:44:2. `bash: cd .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server && npx vitest run ../../deep-loop-runtime/tests/unit/fanout-merge.vitest.ts --reporter=verbose`

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:2:title: "DLR-024 -- Fan-out worker pool concurrency cap"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:3:description: "Validate runCappedPool respects the concurrency cap, isolates per-item failures from the pool, returns ordered results, emits ledger events, and reports all_failed when every item fails."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:6:# DLR-024 -- Fan-out worker pool concurrency cap
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:14:Validates the concurrency-capped pool primitive in `fanout-pool.cjs`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:18:Reusable primitive used by `fanout-run.cjs`. If the cap is not enforced, all CLI lineages
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:19:spawn simultaneously and exhaust system resources. If a single failure sinks the pool, one
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:20:weak executor cancels all other lineages.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:28:- Real user request: `Validate the fan-out worker pool and confirm the 10 unit tests pass and match the documented concurrency and isolation contracts.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:30:- Pass/fail: PASS if all 10 tests pass and source inspection confirms the concurrency pump logic; FAIL otherwise.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:39:- `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` present.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md:46:4. Note: gated-worker test verifies `maxActive()` ≤ concurrency; failure-isolation test verifies pool continues when one item throws.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:2:title: "DLR-029 -- Artifact-dir override and single-executor parity"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:3:description: "Validate the YAML artifact-dir override branch is byte-identical to the original resolver in single-executor mode, and that step_fanout_spawn + step_fanout_merge are fully skipped when config.fanout is absent."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:6:# DLR-029 -- Artifact-dir override and single-executor parity
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:14:Validates that the fan-out YAML changes preserve byte-identical single-executor behavior and
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:15:that the `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override correctly bypasses `resolveArtifactRoot`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:16:in lineage sub-processes.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:20:Any regression in single-executor mode would break all existing deep-research and deep-review
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:27:- Objective: Confirm `step_resolve_artifact_root` `if_absent` branch is byte-identical to the pre-change command, and `step_fanout_spawn` + `step_fanout_merge` have correct `skip_when` guards.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:29:- Real user request: `Validate the fan-out YAML parity: confirm single-executor behavior is unchanged by inspecting the if_absent branch and skip_when guards, and run the full vitest suite to confirm 197/197.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:30:- Expected signals: `if_absent.command` = original `node -e "...resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', '...')..."` byte-for-byte; `step_fanout_spawn` has `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`; `step_fanout_merge` has `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`; full vitest suite passes.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:44:1. `bash: grep -n "if_absent\|skip_when\|fanout_lineage_artifact_dir" .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml | head -20`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:46:3. Confirm `step_fanout_spawn:` has `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:47:4. Confirm `step_fanout_merge:` has `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:54:Source inspection confirms `if_absent` branch is unchanged. Both fan-out steps skipped in single-executor mode. Full vitest suite green.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:58:- `if_absent` branch modified: `resolveArtifactRoot` receives wrong arguments, breaking artifact dir resolution for all single-executor runs.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:59:- `skip_when` guard removed from `step_fanout_spawn`: the spawn step runs even in single-executor mode, attempting to call `fanout-run.cjs` with an absent fanout config.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/artifact-dir-override-parity.md:60:- `skip_when` guard removed from `step_fanout_merge`: merge step runs with no lineage sub-packets, producing an empty consolidated registry.

codex
Two places are now load-bearing for this dimension: `fanout-run.cjs` builds argv arrays rather than shell strings, which is good for injection, while artifact directory handling and same-kind state isolation need closer line-level checks.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   360	  }
   361	
   362	  return typeof env[sessionEnvName] === 'string' && env[sessionEnvName]?.trim() !== '';
   363	}
   364	
   365	/**
   366	 * Detect whether a dispatch lockfile exists in executor state directories.
   367	 *
   368	 * @param kind - The executor kind to check.
   369	 * @param statePaths - State directory paths to scan (defaults to auto-detected).
   370	 * @returns True if a lockfile is found.
   371	 */
   372	export function detectFromLockfile(kind: ExecutorKind, statePaths: string[] = getDefaultStatePaths(kind, process.env)): boolean {
   373	  if (kind === 'native') {
   374	    return false;
   375	  }
   376	
   377	  const names = candidateLockfileNames(kind);
   378	  for (const statePath of statePaths) {
   379	    const directories = [statePath, join(statePath, 'locks')];
   380	    for (const directory of directories) {
   381	      if (!pathIsReadableDirectory(directory)) {
   382	        continue;
   383	      }
   384	      const entries = new Set(readdirSync(directory));
   385	      if (names.some((name) => entries.has(name))) {
   386	        return true;
   387	      }
   388	    }
   389	  }
   390	
   391	  return false;
   392	}
   393	
   394	/**
   395	 * Validate that an executor dispatch is allowed.
   396	 *
   397	 * Checks stack, ancestry, runtime env, and lockfile layers for recursion
   398	 * guard violations per the deep-loop dispatch contract.
   399	 *
   400	 * @param config - Executor configuration.
   401	 * @param context - Optional environment, ancestry, and state path context.
   402	 * @returns Dispatch result indicating whether dispatch is allowed.
   403	 */
   404	export function validateExecutorDispatchAllowed(
   405	  config: ExecutorConfig,
   406	  context: ExecutorDispatchGuardContext = {},
   407	): ExecutorDispatchAllowedResult {
   408	  const kind = getExecutorKind(config);
   409	  if (kind === 'native') {
   410	    return { allowed: true };
   411	  }
   412	
   413	  const env = context.env ?? process.env;
   414	  const stack = env[CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV];
   415	  if (detectSameKindFromStack(stack, kind)) {
   416	    return {
   417	      allowed: false,
   418	      layer: 'stack',
   419	      reason: recursionReasonForLayer('stack'),
   420	      detail: `${kind} already appears in ${CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV}`,
   421	    };
   422	  }
   423	
   424	  if (detectFromAncestry(kind, context.ancestryCmdlines)) {
   425	    return {
   426	      allowed: false,
   427	      layer: 'ancestry',
   428	      reason: recursionReasonForLayer('ancestry'),
   429	      detail: `${kind} executor binary appears in process ancestry`,
   430	    };
   431	  }
   432	
   433	  if (detectFromRuntimeEnv(kind, env)) {
   434	    const envName = EXECUTOR_SESSION_ENV_BY_KIND[kind] ?? 'runtime session env';
   435	    return {
   436	      allowed: false,
   437	      layer: 'env',
   438	      reason: recursionReasonForLayer('env'),
   439	      detail: `${envName} is set for ${kind}`,
   440	    };
   441	  }
   442	
   443	  const statePaths = context.statePaths ?? getDefaultStatePaths(kind, env);
   444	  if (detectFromLockfile(kind, statePaths)) {
   445	    return {
   446	      allowed: false,
   447	      layer: 'lockfile',
   448	      reason: recursionReasonForLayer('lockfile'),
   449	      detail: `${kind} dispatch lockfile exists in runtime state path`,
   450	    };
   451	  }
   452	
   453	  return { allowed: true };
   454	}
   455	
   456	/**
   457	 * Build the environment for a dispatched executor.
   458	 *
   459	 * Filters the parent environment to only include allowed keys per executor kind
   460	 * and appends the current kind to the dispatch stack.
   461	 *
   462	 * @param config - Executor configuration.
   463	 * @param parentEnv - Parent environment (defaults to process.env).
   464	 * @returns Filtered environment with dispatch stack updated.
   465	 */
   466	export function buildExecutorDispatchEnv(
   467	  config: ExecutorConfig,
   468	  parentEnv: Record<string, string | undefined> = process.env,
   469	): Record<string, string | undefined> {
   470	  const kind = getExecutorKind(config);
   471	  if (kind === 'native') {
   472	    return { ...parentEnv };
   473	  }
   474	
   475	  const nextEnv: Record<string, string | undefined> = {};
   476	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(parentEnv)) {
   477	    if (value !== undefined && isAllowedExecutorEnvKey(key, kind)) {
   478	      nextEnv[key] = value;
   479	    }
   480	  }
   481	  nextEnv[CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV] = [...splitDispatchStack(parentEnv[CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV]), kind].join(':');
   482	  return nextEnv;
   483	}
   484	
   485	/**
   486	 * Build an executor audit record for provenance logging.
   487	 *
   488	 * @param executor - Executor configuration.
   489	 * @returns Audit record with kind, model, reasoning effort, and service tier.
   490	 */
   491	export function buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor: ExecutorConfig, lineageId?: string): Record<string, unknown> {
   492	  return {
   493	    kind: getExecutorKind(executor),
   494	    model: executor.model,
   495	    reasoningEffort: executor.reasoningEffort,
   496	    serviceTier: executor.serviceTier,
   497	    ...(lineageId !== undefined ? { lineageId } : {}),
   498	  };
   499	}
   500	
   501	/**
   502	 * Write the first executor provenance record for an iteration.
   503	 *
   504	 * Updates the iteration start record in the JSONL state log with executor
   505	 * audit data, or creates a new iteration_start record if none exists.
   506	 *
   507	 * @param stateLogPath - Path to the JSONL state log.
   508	 * @param executor - Executor configuration.
   509	 * @param iteration - Current iteration number.
   510	 */
   511	export function writeFirstRecordExecutor(stateLogPath: string, executor: ExecutorConfig, iteration: number): void {
   512	  if (getExecutorKind(executor) === 'native') {
   513	    return;
   514	  }
   515	
   516	  const { lines, hasTrailingNewline } = readJsonlFileAfterTailRepair(stateLogPath);
   517	  const recordIndex = findLatestIterationRecordIndex(lines, iteration);
   518	
   519	  if (recordIndex !== -1) {
   520	    const parsedRecord = JSON.parse(lines[recordIndex]);
   521	    if (!isObjectRecord(parsedRecord)) {
   522	      throw new Error(`Iteration ${iteration} JSONL record is not an object`);
   523	    }
   524	
   525	    if (isObjectRecord(parsedRecord.executor)) {
   526	      return;
   527	    }
   528	
   529	    lines[recordIndex] = JSON.stringify({
   530	      ...parsedRecord,
   531	      executor: buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor),
   532	    });
   533	    rewriteJsonlFile(stateLogPath, lines, hasTrailingNewline);
   534	    return;
   535	  }
   536	
   537	  appendJsonlRecordSafe(stateLogPath, {
   538	    type: 'iteration_start',
   539	    iteration,
   540	    executor: buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor),
   541	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   542	  });
   543	}
   544	
   545	/**
   546	 * Emit a dispatch failure event to the JSONL state log.
   547	 *
   548	 * @param stateLogPath - Path to the JSONL state log.
   549	 * @param executor - Executor configuration.
   550	 * @param reason - Dispatch failure reason.
   551	 * @param iteration - Current iteration number.
   552	 * @param detail - Optional failure detail string.
   553	 */
   554	export function emitDispatchFailure(
   555	  stateLogPath: string,
   556	  executor: ExecutorConfig,
   557	  reason: DispatchFailureReason,
   558	  iteration: number,
   559	  detail?: string,
   560	): void {
   561	  const lastIterationEvent = findLatestIterationEvent(readJsonlFileAfterTailRepair(stateLogPath).lines, iteration);
   562	  if (lastIterationEvent?.event === 'dispatch_failure') {
   563	    return;
   564	  }
   565	
   566	  appendJsonlRecordSafe(stateLogPath, {
   567	    type: 'event',
   568	    event: 'dispatch_failure',
   569	    ...(getExecutorKind(executor) === 'native' ? {} : { executor: buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor) }),
   570	    reason,
   571	    iteration,
   572	    ...(detail ? { detail } : {}),
   573	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   574	  });
   575	}
   576	
   577	/**
   578	 * Run a non-native executor command synchronously and log dispatch failures.
   579	 *
   580	 * Detects timeout, crash, and non-zero exit conditions and translates
   581	 * them into typed dispatch_failure JSONL events.
   582	 *
   583	 * @param input - Command input with executor config and state log path.
   584	 * @returns Always returns 0 (failures are logged, not thrown).
   585	 */
   586	export function runAuditedExecutorCommand(input: RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput): number {
   587	  const dispatchAllowed = validateExecutorDispatchAllowed(input.executor, input.guardContext);
   588	  if (!dispatchAllowed.allowed) {
   589	    emitDispatchFailure(input.stateLogPath, input.executor, dispatchAllowed.reason, input.iteration, dispatchAllowed.detail);
   590	    return 0;
   591	  }
   592	
   593	  const timeoutMs = Number.isFinite(input.timeoutSeconds)
   594	    ? Math.max(1000, Math.trunc(input.timeoutSeconds * 1000) - 1000)
   595	    : 1000;
   596	  const result = spawnSync(input.command, input.args, {
   597	    cwd: input.cwd,
   598	    encoding: 'utf8',
   599	    timeout: timeoutMs,
   600	    env: buildExecutorDispatchEnv(input.executor, input.guardContext?.env ?? process.env),
   601	    maxBuffer: 10 * 1024 * 1024,
   602	    ...(typeof input.input === 'string' ? { input: input.input } : {}),
   603	  });
   604	
   605	  if (typeof result.stdout === 'string' && result.stdout.length > 0) {
   606	    process.stdout.write(result.stdout);
   607	  }
   608	  if (typeof result.stderr === 'string' && result.stderr.length > 0) {
   609	    process.stderr.write(result.stderr);
   610	  }
   611	
   612	  if (result.error) {
   613	    const isTimeoutError =
   614	      result.error.name === 'TimeoutError' ||
   615	      (typeof result.error === 'object' &&
   616	        result.error !== null &&
   617	        'code' in result.error &&
   618	        result.error.code === 'ETIMEDOUT');
   619	    emitDispatchFailure(
   620	      input.stateLogPath,
   621	      input.executor,
   622	      isTimeoutError ? 'timeout' : 'crash',
   623	      input.iteration,
   624	      result.error.message,
   625	    );
   626	    return 0;
   627	  }
   628	
   629	  if (typeof result.status === 'number' && result.status !== 0) {
   630	    emitDispatchFailure(
   631	      input.stateLogPath,
   632	      input.executor,
   633	      'crash',
   634	      input.iteration,
   635	      `executor exited with status ${result.status}`,
   636	    );
   637	    return 0;
   638	  }
   639	
   640	  if (typeof result.signal === 'string' && result.signal.length > 0) {
   641	    emitDispatchFailure(
   642	      input.stateLogPath,
   643	      input.executor,
   644	      'crash',
   645	      input.iteration,
   646	      `executor terminated by signal ${result.signal}`,
   647	    );
   648	    return 0;
   649	  }
   650	
   651	  return 0;
   652	}
   653	
   654	/**
   655	 * Run a non-native executor command asynchronously with signal escalation.
   656	 *
   657	 * Uses spawn + detached process group for graceful timeout handling with
   658	 * SIGTERM escalation to SIGKILL after the grace period.
   659	 *
   660	 * @param input - Command input with executor config and state log path.
   661	 * @returns Promise resolving to 0 after process completes.
   662	 */
   663	export async function runAuditedExecutorCommandAsync(input: RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput): Promise<number> {
   664	  const dispatchAllowed = validateExecutorDispatchAllowed(input.executor, input.guardContext);
   665	  if (!dispatchAllowed.allowed) {
   666	    emitDispatchFailure(input.stateLogPath, input.executor, dispatchAllowed.reason, input.iteration, dispatchAllowed.detail);
   667	    return 0;
   668	  }
   669	
   670	  const timeoutMs = Number.isFinite(input.timeoutSeconds)
   671	    ? Math.max(1, Math.trunc(input.timeoutSeconds * 1000))
   672	    : 1000;
   673	  const graceMs = Number.isFinite(input.timeoutGraceMs)
   674	    ? Math.max(1, Math.trunc(input.timeoutGraceMs ?? 250))
   675	    : 250;
   676	
   677	  await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
   678	    let timedOut = false;
   679	    let settled = false;
   680	    let graceTimer: NodeJS.Timeout | undefined;
   681	    const child = spawn(input.command, input.args, {
   682	      cwd: input.cwd,
   683	      detached: process.platform !== 'win32',
   684	      env: buildExecutorDispatchEnv(input.executor, input.guardContext?.env ?? process.env),
   685	      stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
   686	    });
   687	
   688	    const timeoutTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   689	      timedOut = true;
   690	      killProcessGroup(child.pid, 'SIGTERM');
   691	      graceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   692	        killProcessGroup(child.pid, 'SIGKILL');
   693	      }, graceMs);
   694	    }, timeoutMs);
   695	
   696	    if (typeof input.input === 'string') {
   697	      child.stdin.write(input.input);
   698	    }
   699	    child.stdin.end();
   700	
   701	    child.stdout.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
   702	      process.stdout.write(chunk);
   703	    });
   704	    child.stderr.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
   705	      process.stderr.write(chunk);
   706	    });
   707	
   708	    child.on('error', (error: Error) => {
   709	      if (settled) {
   710	        return;
   711	      }
   712	      settled = true;
   713	      clearTimeout(timeoutTimer);
   714	      if (graceTimer) clearTimeout(graceTimer);
   715	      emitDispatchFailure(input.stateLogPath, input.executor, 'crash', input.iteration, error.message);
   716	      resolve();
   717	    });
   718	
   719	    child.on('close', (status: number | null, signal: NodeJS.Signals | null) => {
   720	      if (settled) {
   721	        return;
   722	      }
   723	      settled = true;
   724	      clearTimeout(timeoutTimer);
   725	      if (graceTimer) clearTimeout(graceTimer);
   726	
   727	      if (timedOut) {
   728	        emitDispatchFailure(
   729	          input.stateLogPath,
   730	          input.executor,
   731	          'timeout',
   732	          input.iteration,
   733	          `executor timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`,
   734	        );
   735	        resolve();
   736	        return;
   737	      }
   738	
   739	      if (typeof status === 'number' && status !== 0) {
   740	        emitDispatchFailure(
   741	          input.stateLogPath,
   742	          input.executor,
   743	          'crash',
   744	          input.iteration,
   745	          `executor exited with status ${status}`,
   746	        );
   747	        resolve();
   748	        return;
   749	      }
   750	
   751	      if (typeof signal === 'string' && signal.length > 0) {
   752	        emitDispatchFailure(
   753	          input.stateLogPath,
   754	          input.executor,
   755	          'crash',
   756	          input.iteration,
   757	          `executor terminated by signal ${signal}`,
   758	        );
   759	      }
   760

     9	const tempDirs: string[] = [];
    10	
    11	function makeTempDir(prefix: string): string {
    12	  const dir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), prefix));
    13	  tempDirs.push(dir);
    14	  return dir;
    15	}
    16	
    17	/**
    18	 * Write a stub binary at `binDir/{name}` that exits 0.
    19	 * Used to simulate headless CLI executors without real API keys.
    20	 */
    21	function writeStubBinary(binDir: string, name: string): string {
    22	  const stubPath = join(binDir, name);
    23	  writeFileSync(stubPath, '#!/bin/sh\necho "stub-done"\nexit 0\n', { mode: 0o755 });
    24	  return stubPath;
    25	}
    26	
    27	afterEach(() => {
    28	  while (tempDirs.length > 0) {
    29	    const dir = tempDirs.pop();
    30	    if (dir) rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    31	  }
    32	});

    22	/**
    23	 * Creates a temporary state log with initial content for executor-audit tests.
    24	 */
    25	function withTempStateLog(content: string, run: (stateLogPath: string) => void): void {
    26	  const tempDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'executor-audit-'));
    27	  const stateLogPath = join(tempDir, 'state.jsonl');
    28	
    29	  try {
    30	    writeFileSync(stateLogPath, content, 'utf8');
    31	    run(stateLogPath);
    32	  } finally {
    33	    rmSync(tempDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    34	  }
    35	}

     8	import type { ExecutorConfig } from '../../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.js';
     9	
    10	const posixIt = process.platform === 'win32' ? it.skip : it;
    11	
    12	/**
    13	 * Returns a default cli-codex ExecutorConfig for use in tests.
    14	 */
    15	function cliCodexExecutor(): ExecutorConfig {
    16	  return {
    17	    kind: 'cli-codex',
    18	    model: 'gpt-5.4',
    19	    reasoningEffort: 'high',
    20	    serviceTier: 'priority',
    21	    sandboxMode: null,
    22	    timeoutSeconds: 900,
    23	  };
    24	}
    25	
    26	/**
    27	 * Checks whether a process is alive using a zero-signal kill probe.
    28	 */
    29	function processIsAlive(pid: number): boolean {
    30	  try {
    31	    process.kill(pid, 0);
    32	    return true;
    33	  } catch {
    34	    return false;
    35	  }
    36	}
    37	
    38	/**
    39	 * Polls until a process is no longer alive, with a limited number of attempts.
    40	 */
    41	async function waitUntilDead(pid: number): Promise<void> {
    42	  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 20; attempt += 1) {
    43	    if (!processIsAlive(pid)) {
    44	      return;
    45	    }
    46	    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 50));
    47	  }
    48	}
    49	
    50	describe('executor-audit process-group supervisor', () => {
    51	  posixIt('kills a SIGTERM-ignoring child and grandchild process group after timeout escalation', async () => {
    52	    const tempDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'executor-process-group-'));
    53	    const stateLogPath = join(tempDir, 'state.jsonl');
    54	    const childScriptPath = join(tempDir, 'ignore-sigterm-child.cjs');
    55	    const pidsPath = join(tempDir, 'pids.json');
    56	
    57	    try {
    58	      writeFileSync(stateLogPath, '{"type":"event","event":"start"}\n', 'utf8');
    59	      writeFileSync(
    60	        childScriptPath,
    61	        [
    62	          "const { spawn } = require('node:child_process');",
    63	          "const { writeFileSync } = require('node:fs');",
    64	          "const grandchild = spawn(process.execPath, ['-e', \"process.on('SIGTERM', () => {}); setInterval(() => {}, 1000);\"], { stdio: 'ignore' });",
    65	          "writeFileSync(process.argv[2], JSON.stringify({ child: process.pid, grandchild: grandchild.pid }));",
    66	          "process.on('SIGTERM', () => {});",
    67	          'setInterval(() => {}, 1000);',
    68	          '',
    69	        ].join('\n'),
    70	        'utf8',
    71	      );
    72	
    73	      const exitCode = await runAuditedExecutorCommandAsync({
    74	        command: process.execPath,
    75	        args: [childScriptPath, pidsPath],
    76	        cwd: tempDir,
    77	        timeoutSeconds: 0.15,
    78	        timeoutGraceMs: 75,
    79	        stateLogPath,
    80	        executor: cliCodexExecutor(),
    81	        iteration: 4,
    82	        guardContext: {
    83	          env: {},
    84	          ancestryCmdlines: [],
    85	          statePaths: [],
    86	        },
    87	      });
    88	
    89	      const pids = JSON.parse(readFileSync(pidsPath, 'utf8')) as { child: number; grandchild: number };
    90	      await waitUntilDead(pids.child);
    91	      await waitUntilDead(pids.grandchild);
    92	
    93	      const records = readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8').trimEnd().split('\n').map((line) => JSON.parse(line));
    94	      expect(exitCode).toBe(0);
    95	      expect(processIsAlive(pids.child)).toBe(false);
    96	      expect(processIsAlive(pids.grandchild)).toBe(false);
    97	      expect(records.at(-1)).toMatchObject({
    98	        type: 'event',
    99	        event: 'dispatch_failure',
   100	        iteration: 4,
   101	        reason: 'timeout',
   102	        executor: { kind: 'cli-codex' },
   103	      });
   104	    } finally {
   105	      rmSync(tempDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
   106	    }
   107	  });
   108	});

 succeeded in 0ms:
   760	
   761	      resolve();
   762	    });
   763	  });
   764	
   765	  return 0;
   766	}
   767	
   768	/**
   769	 * Append executor audit information to the last JSONL record in the state log.
   770	 *
   771	 * @param stateLogPath - Path to the JSONL state log.
   772	 * @param executor - Executor configuration.
   773	 * @throws If the state log is empty or the last record is not an object.
   774	 */
   775	export function appendExecutorAuditToLastRecord(stateLogPath: string, executor: ExecutorConfig): void {
   776	  if (getExecutorKind(executor) === 'native') {
   777	    return;
   778	  }
   779	
   780	  const { lines, hasTrailingNewline } = readJsonlFile(stateLogPath);
   781	  const lastLineIndex = findLastNonEmptyLineIndex(lines);
   782	
   783	  if (lastLineIndex === -1) {
   784	    throw new Error('State log does not contain any JSONL records');
   785	  }
   786	
   787	  const parsedRecord = JSON.parse(lines[lastLineIndex]);
   788	  if (parsedRecord === null || Array.isArray(parsedRecord) || typeof parsedRecord !== 'object') {
   789	    throw new Error('Last JSONL record is not an object');
   790	  }
   791	
   792	  const mergedRecord: Record<string, unknown> = {
   793	    ...parsedRecord,
   794	    executor: buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor),
   795	  };
   796	
   797	  lines[lastLineIndex] = JSON.stringify(mergedRecord);
   798	  rewriteJsonlFile(stateLogPath, lines, hasTrailingNewline);
   799	}

  1082	
  1083	      step_generate_context:
  1084	        action: "Route continuity content into the correct canonical spec document via generate-context.js"
  1085	        command: "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json {spec_folder}"
  1086	        critical_rule: "NEVER manually author files under retired memory/ paths (the runtime rejects them); use generate-context.js so the save routes into decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, or handover.md"
  1087	
  1088	      step_index_memory:
  1089	        action: "Index the routed canonical spec document with memory_save when immediate MCP visibility is required"
  1090	        mcp_tool: memory_save
  1091	        invocation: 'memory_save({ filePath: "[routed-canonical-spec-doc-path]" })'
  1092	        note: "Use the actual canonical spec-doc path returned by generate-context.js (decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, or handover.md); memory_index_scan remains the bulk fallback"
  1093	
  1094	      step_verify_save:
  1095	        action: "Verify canonical save routed content into the correct spec document"
  1096	        check: "Does {spec_folder}/implementation-summary.md, {spec_folder}/decision-record.md, or {spec_folder}/handover.md carry the new continuity update?"
  1097	        on_fail: "Log warning but do not block completion"
  1098	
  1099	      step_release_lock:
  1100	        action: "Release the advisory lock after save, skip-save, or cancellation cleanup has resolved"
  1101	        command: "Close or unlock {state_paths.lock_file} using advisory semantics only."
  1102	        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"lock_released","path":"{state_paths.lock_file}","releasePhase":"save","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
  1103	
  1104	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1105	  # COMPLETION
  1106	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1107	  completion:
  1108	    message: |
  1109	      Deep research complete.
  1110	      Iterations: {iteration_count} | Stop reason: {reason}
  1111	      Questions answered: {answered_count}/{total_questions}
  1112	
  1113	      Artifacts:
  1114	        - research/research.md (compiled from {iteration_count} iterations)
  1115	        - {iteration_count} iteration files in research/
  1116	        - State files: config.json, state.jsonl, strategy.md, deep-research-findings-registry.json, dashboard.md
  1117	        - Memory context saved
  1118	
  1119	      STATUS=OK PATH={spec_folder}
  1120	    next_steps:
  1121	      - "/speckit:plan [feature-description]"
  1122	      - "/deep:start-research-loop [new-topic] (for related deep research)"
  1123	      - "/memory:save {spec_folder} (refresh canonical continuity before the next session)"
  1124	
  1125	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1126	# ERROR RECOVERY
  1127	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1128	error_recovery:
  1129	  agent_dispatch_timeout: "Retry once with reduced scope (fewer research actions). If second timeout, mark as 'timeout' and continue."
  1130	  state_file_missing: "If JSONL missing during loop, reconstruct from iteration files. If strategy missing, create minimal version."
  1131	  consecutive_failures: "After 3 consecutive errors, halt loop and enter synthesis with partial findings."
  1132	  memory_save_failure: "Preserve the research/ packet as backup. Log warning."
  1133	
  1134	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1135	# RULES
  1136	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1137	rules:
  1138	  ALWAYS:
  1139	    - follow_workflow_phases_in_order
  1140	    - dispatch_deep_research_agent_per_iteration
  1141	    - check_convergence_before_each_iteration
  1142	    - write_all_state_to_files
  1143	    - save_memory_context_after_synthesis
  1144	    - respect_max_iterations_cap
  1145	  NEVER:
  1146	    - skip_convergence_check
  1147	    - dispatch_non_leaf_agents
  1148	    - hold_iteration_findings_in_memory_only
  1149	    - modify_config_after_initialization
  1150	    - overwrite_jsonl_state_log
  1151	    - proceed_without_state_file_verification
  1152	
  1153	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1154	# REFERENCE-ONLY APPENDIX
  1155	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1156	reference_only_appendix:
  1157	  checkpoint_commit:
  1158	    note: "Checkpoint commits are intentionally excluded from workflow.steps."
  1159	    commands:
  1160	      - "git add {state_paths.iteration_pattern} {state_paths.state_log} {state_paths.strategy} {state_paths.research_output}"

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml | sed -n '100,180p;1100,1160p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public

   100	  iteration_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md"
   101	  # Per-iteration structured delta stream — complement to the canonical state_log
   102	  # append. MUST contain at least one record with `type === "iteration"`; variants
   103	  # like `"iteration_delta"` are silently dropped by the reducer.
   104	  delta_dir: "{artifact_dir}/deltas"
   105	  delta_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/deltas/iter-{NNN}.jsonl"
   106	  review_output: "{artifact_dir}/review-report.md"
   107	  resource_map_output: "{artifact_dir}/resource-map.md"
   108	  archive_root: "{artifact_archive_root}"
   109	
   110	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   111	# WORKFLOW
   112	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   113	workflow:
   114	
   115	  step_resolve_artifact_root:
   116	    description: "Resolve artifact directory — use fan-out lineage override when provided, else resolve from spec_folder"
   117	    note: "Root specs keep review artifacts directly in {spec_folder}/review/. Child phases and sub-phases keep local packet directories in {spec_folder}/review/{packet}/, and archives stay local at {spec_folder}/review_archive/{packet}/."
   118	    branch_on: "config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir"
   119	    if_present:
   120	      bind:
   121	        artifact_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
   122	        artifact_root_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
   123	        artifact_subfolder: null
   124	        artifact_archive_root: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}/_archive"
   125	    if_absent:
   126	      command: "node -e \"const { resolveArtifactRoot } = require('.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs'); console.log(JSON.stringify(resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', 'review')));\""
   127	      outputs:
   128	        - artifact_root_dir: "Resolved local review owner directory"
   129	        - artifact_subfolder: "Local packet subfolder ({ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}), else null"
   130	        - artifact_dir: "Resolved review packet directory for this target"
   131	        - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local review archive directory for this target"
   132	
   133	  step_fanout_spawn:
   134	    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
   135	    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   136	    instructions: |
   137	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   138	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   139	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   140	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   141	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
   142	    sub_steps:
   143	      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
   144	        description: "Spawn all CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
   145	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
   146	        command: |
   147	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
   148	            --spec-folder {spec_folder} \
   149	            --loop-type review \
   150	            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
   151	            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   152	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   153	        wait_for_completion: true
   154	      step_fanout_spawn_native:
   155	        description: "Dispatch each native lineage as a sequential deep-review sub-agent"
   156	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"
   157	        for_each: "lineage in expanded native entries of config.fanout"
   158	        dispatch:
   159	          agent: deep-review
   160	          model: opus
   161	          context: |
   162	            Execute the deep-review loop with these parameters:
   163	              spec_folder: {spec_folder}
   164	              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
   165	              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
   166	              executor: native
   167	            Run to convergence. Write all outputs to the override directory.
   168	        wait_for_completion: true
   169	
   170	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   171	  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
   172	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   173	  phase_init:
   174	    purpose: Create all state files for the review session and resolve review scope
   175	    steps:
   176	
   177	      step_preflight_contract:
   178	        action: "Validate required setup bindings and executable workflow contract before any file writes"
   179	        verify:
   180	          - required_values_present: [review_target, review_target_type, review_dimensions, spec_folder, max_iterations, convergence_threshold]
  1100	        read:
  1101	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
  1102	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
  1103	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
  1104	        content: |
  1105	          # Deep Review Dashboard - Session Overview
  1106	
  1107	          Auto-generated from JSONL state log and strategy file. Regenerated after every iteration evaluation.
  1108	
  1109	          ## Status
  1110	          - Review Target: {review_target} ({review_target_type})
  1111	          - Status: [ITERATING | CONVERGING | STUCK_RECOVERY | COMPLETE]
  1112	          - Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}
  1113	          - Provisional Verdict: [PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL | PENDING]
  1114	          - hasAdvisories: {has_advisories}
  1115	
  1116	          ## Findings Summary
  1117	          | Severity | Count | Trend |
  1118	          |----------|------:|-------|
  1119	          | P0 (Blockers) | {p0_count} | {p0_trend} |
  1120	          | P1 (Required) | {p1_count} | {p1_trend} |
  1121	          | P2 (Suggestions) | {p2_count} | {p2_trend} |
  1122	
  1123	          ## Dimension Coverage
  1124	          | Dimension | Status | Iteration | Findings |
  1125	          |-----------|--------|-----------|----------|
  1126	          | [per dimension from strategy + JSONL] |
  1127	
  1128	          ## Traceability Coverage
  1129	          | Protocol | Level | Status | Findings |
  1130	          |----------|-------|--------|----------|
  1131	          | [per protocol from traceabilityChecks] |
  1132	
  1133	          ## Progress
  1134	          | # | Dimension | Ratio | P0/P1/P2 | Status |
  1135	          |---|-----------|-------|----------|--------|
  1136	          | [per iteration from JSONL] |
  1137	
  1138	          ## Trend
  1139	          - Last 3 ratios: [{ratios}] [{trend}]
  1140	          - Stuck count: {stuck_count}
  1141	          - Gate violations: {guard_violations}
  1142	
  1143	          ## Next Focus
  1144	          {next_dimension}
  1145	        output: "{state_paths.dashboard}"
  1146	        note: "Auto-generated from JSONL + strategy. Never manually edited."
  1147	
  1148	      step_update_tracking:
  1149	        action: "Update loop tracking variables"
  1150	        set:
  1151	          current_iteration: "{current_iteration + 1}"
  1152	          p0_count: "Accumulate from severity_counts"
  1153	          p1_count: "Accumulate from severity_counts"
  1154	          p2_count: "Accumulate from severity_counts"
  1155	          stuck_count: |
  1156	            if status == 'thought': unchanged (skip)
  1157	            if status == 'insight': 0 (reset)
  1158	            if newFindingsRatio <= 0.05: stuck_count + 1
  1159	            else: 0
  1160	        log: "Iteration {N} complete. Dimensions: {dimensions}. newFindingsRatio: {newFindingsRatio}. Findings: P0={severity_counts.P0} P1={severity_counts.P1} P2={severity_counts.P2}. Stuck count: {stuck_count}."
