 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	description: Autonomous deep-research loop: iterative investigation with convergence detection. Modes :auto, :confirm.
     3	argument-hint: "<topic> [:auto|:confirm] [--max-iterations=N] [--convergence=N] [--executor=<type> [--model=X] [--count=N] [--label=X] ...] [--executors=<json>] [--concurrency=N] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
     4	allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task, WebFetch, memory_context, memory_search, code_graph_query, code_graph_context
     5	---
     6	
     7	> **Code Graph ownership:** `code_graph_query` and `code_graph_context` stay stable MCP tool IDs; implementation and docs now live under `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/`.
     8	
     9	> **EXECUTION PROTOCOL -- READ FIRST**
    10	>
    11	> This command runs a structured YAML workflow. Do NOT dispatch agents from this document.
    12	>
    13	> **YOUR FIRST ACTION:**
    14	> 1. Run the unified setup phase in this Markdown entrypoint and resolve:
    15	>    - `research_topic`
    16	>    - `spec_folder`
    17	>    - `execution_mode`
    18	>    - `maxIterations`
    19	>    - `convergenceThreshold`
    20	> 2. Load the corresponding YAML file from `assets/` only after all setup values are resolved:
    21	>    - Auto: `deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`
    22	>    - Confirm: `deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml`
    23	> 3. Execute the YAML workflow step by step using those resolved values
    24	>
    25	> All content below is reference context for the YAML workflow. Do not treat reference sections as direct instructions to execute.
    26	
    27	## CONSTRAINTS
    28	
    29	- **DO NOT** dispatch any agent from this document
    30	- **ALL** agent dispatching is handled by the YAML workflow steps
    31	- **MARKDOWN OWNS SETUP**: resolve setup inputs here first, then hand off to YAML
    32	- **YAML START CONDITION**: do not load YAML until ALL required inputs are bound:
    33	  - `research_topic`, `spec_folder`, `execution_mode`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`
    34	
    35	> **Canonical mode syntax:** use attached command suffixes (`/deep:start-research-loop:auto`, `/deep:start-research-loop:confirm`) and keep AGENTS, skills, and quick references synchronized to this entrypoint.
    36	
    37	> **Note:** Late-INIT `spec.md` detection and bounded `spec.md` mutations follow
    38	> `.opencode/skills/deep-research/references/protocol/spec_check_protocol.md`.
    39	> Acquire `{spec_folder}/research/.deep-research.lock` before `folder_state` classification and keep `research/research.md` as the source of truth for any generated findings sync.
    40	> Targeted strict validation after deep-research spec mutations uses the Node validation orchestrator; the current strict path is designed for fast packet checks and was measured at about 108ms for a fresh Level 3 packet on the local harness.
    41	
    42	# SINGLE CONSOLIDATED SETUP PROMPT
    43	
    44	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
    45	
    46	---
    47	
    48	## 0. UNIFIED SETUP PHASE
    49	
    50	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    51	
    52	For `:auto`, do not emit the consolidated prompt by default. Resolve setup with the three-tier branch below, then load the auto YAML only after all required values are bound.
    53	
    54	### `:auto` Setup Resolution
    55	
    56	Setup contract: see `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/workflows/auto_mode_contract.md`.
    57	
    58	Under `execution_mode = AUTONOMOUS` (from the `:auto` suffix), follow the three-tier flow:
    59	
    60	1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{artifact_dir}/deep-research-config.json` (shape: `researchTopic`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
    61	
    62	2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: none needed. Missing `research_topic` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.
    63	
    64	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:start-research-loop:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
    65	
    66	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the consolidated setup prompt section below.
    67	
    68	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
    69	
    70	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults. Grammar: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2.
    71	
    72	```yaml
    73	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
    74	  research_topic: WebSocket reconnection strategies  # string
    75	  spec_folder: <spec-folder>  # existing | new | update-related | phase-folder | explicit path
    76	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
    77	  maxIterations: 10  # positive integer
    78	  convergenceThreshold: 0.05  # decimal 0..1
    79	  executor: native  # native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
    80	  executor_model: ""  # optional executor-specific model id
    81	  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional reasoning effort
    82	  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional service tier
    83	  executor_timeout: 900  # optional positive integer seconds
    84	  resource_map_emit: true  # boolean
    85	```
    86	
    87	Rules: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2 (unspecified fields fall back to default; marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags; unknown fields warn; malformed lines parse-error).
    88	
    89	### Default Resolution Table
    90	
    91	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
    92	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
    93	| `research_topic` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` positional topic, or marker `research_topic` | none | N |
    94	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or targeted choice among suggested existing/new/update-related/phase folder | none | Y, when topic is present but folder choice is ambiguous |
    95	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
    96	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `10` | N |
    97	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.05` | N |
    98	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
    99	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
   100	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
   101	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
   102	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
   103	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
   104	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each `--executor` group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
   105	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   106	
   107	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-research` agent dispatches with isolated dirs. CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses concurrently in the capped pool.
   108	
   109	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
   110	
   111	```
   112	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   113	
   114	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   115	   |-- ":auto"         -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   116	   |-- ":confirm"      -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   117	   +-- No suffix       -> execution_mode = "ASK"
   118	
   119	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for topic:
   120	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   121	   |     -> research_topic = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   122	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   123	
   124	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   125	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   126	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   127	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   128	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   129	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   130	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   131	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   132	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   133	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   134	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   135	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   136	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
   137	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   138	   |
   139	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   140	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   141	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   142	   |
   143	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   144	
   145	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   146	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults
   147	   - The generated `deep-research-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
   148	
   149	   Parsing to config mapping:
   150	   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
   151	   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
   152	   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
   153	   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
   154	   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`
   155	
   156	   Validation hook:
   157	   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time
   158	   - Invalid combinations fail fast with clear errors, including `cli-codex` without `--model` and reserved-but-unwired executor kinds
   159	
   160	4. Search for related spec folders across alias roots:
   161	   $ find specs .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -10
   162	
   163	5. Search for prior work (background):
   164	   - memory_context({ input: research_topic OR "deep-research", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
   165	   - Store: prior_work_found = [yes/no]
   166	
   167	6. ASK with SINGLE prompt (include only applicable questions):
   168	   - Include Q-Exec only when `--executor` is NOT present and the topic text does NOT already mention executor hints such as `cli-codex`, `codex`, or `gpt-5.4`
   169	   - If Q-Exec is omitted and no executor is otherwise resolved, default to `native`
   170	
   171	   Q0. Research Topic (if not in command): What topic to research deeply?
   172	
   173	   Q1. Spec Folder (required):
   174	     A) Use existing [suggest if found]
   175	     B) Create new under `specs/[track]/[###]-[slug]/` (accept `.opencode/specs/` alias roots when already in use)
   176	     C) Update related [if match found]
   177	     D) Phase folder (e.g., `specs/NN-track/NNN-name/001-phase/` or matching `.opencode/specs/` alias)
   178	
   179	   Q2. Execution Mode (if no suffix):
   180	     A) Autonomous -- all iterations without approval
   181	     B) Interactive -- pause at each iteration for review
   182	
   183	   Q3. Max Iterations (if not set via flag):
   184	     Default is 10. Change? [Enter number or press enter for default]
   185	
   186	   Q-Exec. Executor (optional, press enter for default):
   187	     A) Native (default) — dispatch via @deep-research agent with Opus.
   188	     B) cli-codex — `codex exec` with --model X -c model_reasoning_effort -c service_tier.
   189	     C) cli-gemini — `gemini "PROMPT" -m gemini-3.1-pro-preview -y -o text`. Single supported model currently. No reasoning-effort or service-tier.
   190	     D) cli-claude-code — `claude -p "PROMPT" --model X --permission-mode acceptEdits` with optional --effort. No service-tier.
   191	     E) cli-opencode — `opencode run --model X --agent general --format json --dangerously-skip-permissions --pure --dir {repo_root} [--variant Y] "PROMPT" </dev/null`. `reasoningEffort` maps to `--variant`. No service-tier.
   192	     F) cli-devin — `devin --print --prompt-file ... --model X --permission-mode auto`. Default model swe-1.6. No reasoning-effort or service-tier.
   193	
   194	   Reply format examples:
   195	   - `"A, A"`
   196	   - `"WebSocket research, A, B, 15"`
   197	   - `"WebSocket reconnection strategies, B, A, 10, 0.05, B, gpt-5.4, high, fast"`
   198	   - `"agent execution guardrails research, B, A, 15, 0.05, C, gpt-5.4, _, _"`
   199	
   200	7. WAIT for user response (DO NOT PROCEED)
   201	
   202	8. Parse response and store ALL results:
   203	   - research_topic = [from Q0 or $ARGUMENTS]
   204	   - spec_choice = [A/B/C/D from Q1]
   205	   - spec_path = [derived path]
   206	   - execution_mode = [AUTONOMOUS/INTERACTIVE]
   207	   - maxIterations = [from Q3 or flag or default 10]
   208	   - convergenceThreshold = [from flag or default 0.05]
   209	   - executor config = [CLI flags, compact reply, config file, or default `native`; map compact reply fields to `config.executor.type/model/reasoningEffort/serviceTier`, and accept an optional volunteered convergence value before executor fields]
   210	
   211	9. SET STATUS: PASSED
   212	
   213	STOP HERE - Wait for user answers before continuing.
   214	
   215	DO NOT proceed until user explicitly answers
   216	NEVER auto-create spec folders without confirmation
   217	NEVER split questions into multiple prompts
   218	```

    29	
    30	## CONSTRAINTS
    31	
    32	- **DO NOT** dispatch any agent from this document
    33	- **ALL** agent dispatching is handled by the YAML workflow steps
    34	- **MARKDOWN OWNS SETUP**: resolve setup inputs here first, then hand off to YAML
    35	- **YAML START CONDITION**: do not load YAML until ALL required inputs are bound:
    36	  - `review_target`, `review_target_type`, `review_dimensions`, `spec_folder`, `execution_mode`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`
    37	
    38	> **Canonical mode syntax:** use attached command suffixes (`/deep:start-review-loop:auto`, `/deep:start-review-loop:confirm`) and keep AGENTS, skills, and quick references synchronized to this entrypoint.
    39	
    40	# SINGLE CONSOLIDATED SETUP PROMPT
    41	
    42	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
    43	
    44	---
    45	
    46	## 0. UNIFIED SETUP PHASE
    47	
    48	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    49	
    50	For `:auto`, do not emit the consolidated prompt by default. Resolve setup with the three-tier branch below, then load the auto YAML only after all required values are bound.
    51	
    52	### `:auto` Setup Resolution
    53	
    54	Setup contract: see `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/workflows/auto_mode_contract.md`.
    55	
    56	Under `execution_mode = AUTONOMOUS` (from the `:auto` suffix), follow the three-tier flow:
    57	
    58	1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-config.json` (shape: `reviewTarget`, `reviewTargetType`, `reviewDimensions`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
    59	
    60	2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `review_target_type`, `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: if `review_target_type` is ambiguous, ask only for `review_target_type` first — the answer may make `spec_folder` self-evident on the next Tier 1 pass. Missing `review_target` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.
    61	
    62	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:start-review-loop:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
    63	
    64	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the Consolidated Setup Prompt section below.
    65	
    66	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
    67	
    68	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults.
    69	
    70	```yaml
    71	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
    72	  review_target: <spec-folder>
    73	  review_target_type: spec-folder  # one of: spec-folder | skill | agent | track | files
    74	  review_dimensions: all  # or comma-separated subset: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
    75	  spec_folder: existing  # one of: existing | new | update-related | phase-folder, or an explicit specs/.opencode/specs path
    76	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
    77	  maxIterations: 10
    78	  convergenceThreshold: 0.10
    79	  executor: native  # one of: native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
    80	  executor_model: ""  # optional, executor-specific
    81	  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional
    82	  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional
    83	  executor_timeout: 900  # optional
    84	  resource_map_emit: true  # optional
    85	```
    86	
    87	Rules:
    88	
    89	- Any unspecified field falls back to its documented default.
    90	- Marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags because the caller explicitly bound setup in the prompt body.
    91	- Unknown fields are warnings, not errors.
    92	- Malformed lines, including missing `:`, emit a parse error naming the offending line. Known fields parsed before the error may still be used, and unresolved fields continue to Tier 2 or Tier 3.
    93	- Empty strings count as unresolved for required fields.
    94	- Compact legacy answer strings are only for the consolidated `:confirm` prompt. They are not a `:auto` marker format.
    95	
    96	### Default Resolution Table
    97	
    98	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
    99	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
   100	| `review_target` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` first positional target, or marker `review_target` | none | N |
   101	| `review_target_type` | Y | marker `review_target_type`, or auto-detect from `review_target` | inferred only | Y, when target is present but ambiguous |
   102	| `review_dimensions` | Y | flag `--dims` if supported by caller, marker `review_dimensions`, or default | `"all"` | N |
   103	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or target path when target is a spec folder | none | Y, when target is not a spec folder |
   104	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
   105	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `7` | N |
   106	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.10` | N |
   107	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
   108	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
   109	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
   110	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
   111	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
   112	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
   113	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
   114	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   115	
   116	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.
   117

   142	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)

   147	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   148	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)

   153	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)

   155	   |   - Review fan-out verdict: strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL)
   156	   |
   157	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=7, convergenceThreshold=0.10, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`

   111	# WORKFLOW

   113	workflow:
   114	
   115	  step_resolve_artifact_root:
   116	    description: "Resolve artifact directory — use fan-out lineage override when provided, else resolve from spec_folder"
   117	    note: "Root specs keep review artifacts directly in {spec_folder}/review/. Child phases and sub-phases keep local packet directories in {spec_folder}/review/{packet}/, and archives stay local at {spec_folder}/review_archive/{packet}/."

   119	    if_present:

   127	      outputs:

   130	        - artifact_dir: "Resolved review packet directory for this target"
   131	        - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local review archive directory for this target"
   132	
   133	  step_fanout_spawn:
   134	    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
   135	    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   136	    instructions: |
   137	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   138	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   139	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   140	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   141	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
   142	    sub_steps:
   143	      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
   144	        description: "Spawn all CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
   145	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
   146	        command: |
   147	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \

   149	            --loop-type review \
   150	            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
   151	            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   152	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   153	        wait_for_completion: true
   154	      step_fanout_spawn_native:

   157	        for_each: "lineage in expanded native entries of config.fanout"
   158	        dispatch:

   163	              spec_folder: {spec_folder}
   164	              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
   165	              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
   166	              executor: native

    21	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: spec-core | v2.2 -->
    22	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->
    23	
    24	# Phase 003 — Per-lineage spawn + sub-packet isolation
    25	
    26	## Purpose
    27	Run each lineage as the existing command (verbatim loop) into its own isolated sub-packet.
    28	
    29	## Scope
    30	- `fanout-pool.cjs` spawn path: existing deep-loop command, single synthesized `config.executor` per lineage, `--max-iterations {lineage.iterations ?? default}`, env `SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID={label}`, capture stdout to `{sub-packet}/logs/iter-NNN.out`.
    31	- `--artifact-dir-override` branch in `step_resolve_artifact_root` for all 4 YAMLs: `deep_start-{research,review}-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml` (override → use it; else `resolveArtifactRoot`). Sub-packets at `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/`.
    32	- Recursion-guard check: distinct kinds safe; same-kind replicas verify loop-lock/ancestry (`SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR`).
    33	
    34	## Success
    35	- Integration: pool spawns a stub command twice into two `lineages/{label}/` trees; distinct trees + distinct sessionIds + no lock contention.
    36	
    37	## Out of scope
    38	Salvage (004); merge (005).

    23	
    24	# Phase 001 — Fan-out schema + config plumbing
    25	
    26	## Purpose
    27	Add the opt-in multi-executor config representation without changing any behavior. Foundation for all later phases.
    28	
    29	## Scope
    30	- `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`: add `lineageExecutorSchema` (= `executorConfigSchema` + dir-safe `label`, `count`, nullable `iterations`), `fanoutConfigSchema` (`executors[]`, `concurrency` default 2), `parseFanoutConfig` (delegates per-entry to existing `parseExecutorConfig`), `expandLineages` (count→labels). Do NOT modify `executorConfigSchema`/`parseExecutorConfig`.
    31	- Backward-compat: config carries EITHER `config.executor` OR `config.fanout`, never both; both-present fails fast.
    32	- `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts`: optional `lineageId` on `buildExecutorAuditRecord` + `RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput` (absent ⇒ unchanged records).
    33	
    34	## Success
    35	- New unit tests cover fan-out parse / both-present conflict / count-expansion / per-entry kind validation reuse.
    36	- **Parity gate:** existing single-executor `parseExecutorConfig` tests byte-identical; full vitest green.
    37	
    38	## Out of scope
    39	Pool, spawn, merge, YAML, docs (later phases).

    23	
    24	# Phase 006 — Command surface + docs + final parity
    25	
    26	## Purpose
    27	Expose fan-out via the command entrypoints (opt-in), document it for both consumers, and prove single-executor parity.
    28	
    29	## Scope
    30	- `commands/deep/start-research-loop.md` + `start-review-loop.md` §0: repeatable `--executor` (groups trailing `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count`), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`; Default Resolution Table + PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS additions; fan-out EXAMPLES.
    31	- Default policy: 0–1 executor & no `--executors` ⇒ `config.executor` (single, recommended default, unchanged); 2+ / `--executors` / `count>1` ⇒ `config.fanout`.
    32	- Docs: `deep-research/SKILL.md` (§4 NEVER #9 + EXPERIMENTAL) + `deep-review/SKILL.md` (FORBIDDEN INVOCATION PATTERNS) carve-out permitting command-driven fan-out (ad-hoc shell + intra-lineage wave stay forbidden/deferred); "Fan-Out Convergence" in both `references/convergence/convergence.md`; `deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md` script table adds `fanout-pool.cjs`/`fanout-merge.cjs`.
    33	
    34	## Success
    35	- **Final parity gate (non-negotiable):** single-executor run byte-identical to pre-change `main` (config, state.jsonl modulo timestamps, iteration md, research.md/review-report.md).
    36	- Docs accurate; full vitest green; `validate.sh --strict` green for parent + children.
    37	
    38	## Out of scope
    39	Wave-within-lineage (deferred).

     1	---
     2	title: "Phase 007: Native per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out"
     3	description: "Design (spec only) a first-class contract-compliant per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out: an operator requests a fixed-count heterogeneous schedule (e.g. nine iterations on minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed then one on claude-opus-4-8) via /deep:start-review-loop, /deep:start-research-loop, and shared deep-loop-runtime, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool like packet 122 review did. Models the schedule as ordered single-executor lineages (bands) over existing fan-out via a thin iterationPlan flag desugaring one-to-one into ordered lineages plus minimal sequencing. Produces schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, command-surface design; implementation is this phase's future work."

    21	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: spec-core | v2.2 -->
    22	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->
    23	
    24	# Phase 007 — Native per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out
    25	
    26	## Purpose
    27	Design (spec only) a first-class, contract-compliant way for an operator to request a fixed-count heterogeneous per-iteration model schedule through the canonical `/deep:start-review-loop` and `/deep:start-research-loop` commands and the shared `deep-loop-runtime`. Motivating example: nine iterations on `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` then one on `claude-opus-4-8`, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool the way packet 122 review did. This phase produces the schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, and command-surface design; implementation is this phase's own future work (no code here).
    28	
    29	**Background — the packet 122 deviation:** packet 122 review ran a heterogeneous per-iteration model mix by hand-rolling a shell worker pool (`run_one.sh` with a `case` statement assigning per-iteration dimensions and a `xargs`-style fan-out). That bespoke machinery bypassed the shared `deep-loop-runtime` dispatch, the externalized JSONL state machine, and the consumer merge; it worked once but is unreproducible through the supported command surface and violates deep-review forbidden patterns.
    30	
    31	**Problem statement — the contract gap:** there is no supported way to express "run band A on model X for N iterations, then band B on model Y for M iterations" through the canonical commands. Existing fan-out (phases 001-006) runs homogeneous lineages concurrently and merges once all complete; it has no ordered heterogeneous per-iteration schedule and no ordered/seeded band sequencing, forcing operators back toward the packet 122 hand-rolled path.
    32	
    33	## Scope
    34	**In:**
    35	- Design a fixed-count heterogeneous per-iteration model-schedule capability expressible via the canonical `/deep:start-review-loop` and `/deep:start-research-loop` commands and the shared `deep-loop-runtime`.
    36	- Schema/flag design: an `iterationPlan` of ordered bands (from-to or count; executor kind and model; optional focus/dimensions; optional dependsOn/order) plus the `iteration-plan` (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) flag desugaring one-to-one into the existing `executors` lineage array, integrated through `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts` (reuse-first; no parallel dispatcher).
    37	- Ordered/sequential bands: a minimal `dependsOn`/`order` extension so a later band runs only after an earlier completes, with optional seed-context handoff, enforced inside the existing pool driver (no ad-hoc shell).
    38	- Per-band focus/dimension assignment (generalizing the packet 122 `run_one.sh` dimensions) threaded into existing per-iteration prompt construction for both review and research.
    39	- Per-iteration/per-band executor audit and tracing (band and model attribution) in the JSONL state log, deltas, and findings registry; plus merge-precedence as an ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` preserving review severity rollup and research dedup/attribution.
    40	- Contract-compliance and backward-compat design: satisfy every deep-review forbidden pattern (no ad-hoc shell fan-out; no direct CLI loop; no `/tmp` prompt dispatch; no Task-tool agent dispatch; no skipping the JSONL state machine; state under the local review/research packet); preserve read-only, externalized-state, and per-iteration-JSONL contracts; keep single-executor and homogeneous fan-out byte-identical with opt-in absent-by-default fields.
    41	- A Level-2 lean phase spec plus ordered tasks and risks numbered `007`, reusing the packet deep-research-before-build convention if siblings use it.
    42	
    43	**Out:**
    44	- Implementing the capability — design/spec only; the build is this phase's future work.
    45	- Any code edits to fan-out scripts, `executor-config.ts`, the auto/confirm YAML, or the deep-review / deep-research / deep-loop-runtime SKILL.md files in this phase.
    46	- Inventing a new parallel orchestration system, worker-pool runtime, or state machine that competes with `step_fanout_spawn` and `fanout-pool.cjs`.
    47	- True intra-lineage per-iteration executor rotation inside one continuous loop with cross-lineage iteration interleaving or band-synchronization barriers (rejected in favor of ordered homogeneous lineages unless desugaring proves insufficient).
    48	- Convergence-algorithm changes (newInfoRatio, Bayesian scoring) beyond making per-band max-iterations and ordered bands interoperate with existing convergence.
    49	- New executor kinds/providers/models beyond those registered (minimax, codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin, native) and changes to the single-executor default path or existing homogeneous fan-out merge semantics.
    50	- Re-litigating or modifying packet 122 hand-rolled artifacts.
    51	
    52	## Success
    53	- Command surface and one-to-one desugaring into existing fan-out lineages specified; desugaring pinned to real fan-out and `executor-config` entry points.
    54	- No parallel dispatcher introduced; existing machinery reused.
    55	- Ordered bands, seed-handoff, per-band focus, and model attribution covered.
    56	- Merge precedence keeps the severity-rollup invariant (any active P0 from any band forces merged FAIL).
    57	- Every deep-review forbidden pattern mapped and opt-in byte-identical proven.
    58	- Valid Level-2 packet 123 child numbered `007`.
    59	
    60	## Risks
    61	- **Sequencing gap:** existing fan-out runs lineages concurrently and jumps to synthesis once all complete; band B after band A with optional seeding needs an ordered/`dependsOn` extension to `fanout-pool.cjs` that does not yet exist, so it may be underestimated as pure sugar. Mitigation: front-load research to confirm whether ordering is sugar over concurrency caps or a genuine minimal pool-driver delta, and scope it explicitly.
    62	- **Reuse-vs-rewrite drift:** pressure to match packet 122 per-iteration-dimension UX could tempt true intra-loop executor rotation (the hard path needing band-sync barriers and iteration interleaving). Mitigation: hold the ordered-homogeneous-lineage framing and keep intra-loop rotation out of scope unless desugaring is proven insufficient.
    63	- **Merge-precedence regression:** generalizing the any-P0-to-FAIL rule into ordered band-priority risks weakening strongest-restriction, since a lower-priority band P0 must still escalate. Mitigation: specify precedence as conflict-resolution only, with severity rollup preserved as an invariant above band priority.
    64	- **Contract-compliance illusion and two-consumer divergence:** a loosely specified band-priority or ordered layer could resemble the forbidden ad-hoc dispatcher, and research dedup/attribution versus review severity-rollup may fit one design unevenly. Mitigation: explicit per-forbidden-pattern mapping; require all dispatch inside `step_fanout_spawn` and the fan-out scripts; specify the band-priority and audit model for both consumers with differences called out.
    65	- **API-assumption and scope-creep risk:** candidate files are enumerated but not re-read in this design pass, so specifying against a stale interface or drifting into code edits would mislead implementers. Mitigation: success criteria require locking the desugaring contract against actual entry points via the front-loaded research task; scope-out forbids code edits in this phase.
    66	
    67	## Out of scope
    68	Implementation/build of the capability; any code edits; a competing parallel orchestrator; true intra-lineage executor rotation; convergence-math changes; new executor kinds; packet 122 artifact changes (all deferred or excluded per Scope/Out above).

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:39:  - `fanoutConfigSchema` = `{ executors: lineageExecutorSchema[] (min 1), concurrency: int≥1 (default 2) }`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:60:- **Backward-compat rule:** config carries EITHER `config.executor` (single) OR `config.fanout` (multi), never both. The both-present guard is deferred to Phase 6 (command surface) — no call site writes `config.fanout` yet.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:63:- **`kind` vs `type` — INCONSISTENT across the two YAMLs (grep-verified):** the dispatch step `step_dispatch_iteration` branches on **`config.executor.type`** in `deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml` (L573) but on **`config.executor.kind`** in `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` (L696). Both work because `normalizeExecutorConfigInput` (executor-config.ts) aliases `type`→`kind`, and the canonical schema field is `kind`. Phase 3/6 must tolerate BOTH spellings (and ideally normalize research to `kind` while there). Fan-out config entries use `kind`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:85:  - Override logic: if `--artifact-dir-override` / `config.fanout` sub-packet dir is present → bind `artifact_dir` to it; else run the existing inline resolver unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:96:- New `step_fanout_merge` at the TOP of each `phase_synthesis`, gated on `config.fanout` present (absent ⇒ synthesis unchanged):
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:103:- `commands/deep/start-research-loop.md` + `start-review-loop.md` §0: repeatable `--executor` (groups trailing `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count`), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`; extend the Default Resolution Table + PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS; add fan-out EXAMPLES. **Default policy: 0–1 executor & no `--executors` ⇒ write `config.executor` (single, unchanged, documented as the recommended default); 2+ / `--executors` / any `count>1` ⇒ write `config.fanout`.** Add the both-present fail-fast here.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:119:- **(Option B-spawn, recommended):** add a thin **headless single-lineage loop driver** (`scripts/fanout-run.cjs` or a mode of `fanout-pool.cjs`) that, per lineage, runs the loop control-flow in JS by CALLING the existing runtime pieces as subprocesses/functions: `renderPromptPack` → `runAuditedExecutorCommand` (CLI) — note the **native/`@deep-research` path can't be driven headlessly without the agent**, so fan-out may need to require CLI executors per lineage (or treat `native` as "this agent runs it inline, others spawn"). It calls `convergence.cjs` each iteration so convergence is NOT re-implemented. This is "Option B" honestly realized; it re-implements only the loop's *control flow*, not its math.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/001-schema-config-plumbing/implementation-summary.md:50:Config carries EITHER `config.executor` (single, default, unchanged) OR `config.fanout` (multi). End §0.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:104:| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each `--executor` group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:107:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-research` agent dispatches with isolated dirs. CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses concurrently in the capped pool.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:128:   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:129:   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:130:   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:131:   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:132:   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:135:   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:136:   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:137:   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:140:   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:141:   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:143:   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:113:| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:116:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:36:- **`scripts/fanout-run.cjs`** — CLI lineage pool driver. TSX-bootstrapped; uses `parseFanoutConfig` / `expandLineages` from `executor-config.ts` and `runCappedPool` from `fanout-pool.cjs`. Filters to CLI lineages only (`kind !== 'native'`); native lineages are dispatched by the YAML. For each CLI lineage: creates `{base}/lineages/{label}/` + `{label}/.executor-state/` dirs; builds a "run the full loop" prompt; constructs the per-kind CLI command (`codex exec`, `claude -p`, `opencode run`, `gemini`, `devin --print`); sets `SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID={label}` + `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` per-lineage to prevent same-kind replica lockfile collisions; runs via `spawnSync` with a generous timeout derived from `lineage.iterations * lineage.timeoutSeconds * 2` (capped at 4 hours); saves stdout to `{lineageDir}/logs/fanout-lineage.out`; calls `runSalvageSweep` after each subprocess exits. Exit codes: 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:40:- **`step_resolve_artifact_root` updated in all 4 YAMLs** — Added `branch_on: "config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir"`. When the override is present (a lineage subprocess received it), `artifact_dir` is bound directly — no `resolveArtifactRoot` node call. When absent (single-executor mode), the `if_absent` branch runs the byte-identical original command. Affects `deep_start-{research,review}-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:42:- **`step_fanout_spawn` added to all 4 YAMLs** — New top-level workflow step with `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. When config.fanout is present: spawns CLI lineages via `fanout-run.cjs` (`step_fanout_spawn_cli`); dispatches native lineages as sequential `agent: deep-research`/`deep-review` runs (`step_fanout_spawn_native`); after all lineages converge, jumps to `phase_synthesis` skipping `phase_init` and `phase_main_loop`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:44:- **`tests/unit/fanout-run.vitest.ts`** — 5 integration tests: native-only config returns early with ok (no pool spawn); bad JSON config exits 3; missing required args exits 3; 2 cli-codex lineages create distinct `lineages/{label}/` dirs + `orchestration-summary.json` + `orchestration-status.log`; same-kind replicas get distinct `.executor-state` dirs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:58:- **`step_fanout_merge` added to top of `phase_synthesis` in all 4 YAMLs** — `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. Calls `fanout-merge.cjs` with the base `artifact_dir`. Review variant adds `bind_from_output: {p0_count: "active_p0", p1_count: "active_p1", p2_count: "active_p2"}` so `step_derive_verdict` uses merged counts rather than an empty single-executor state log. Research variant feeds the merged `deep-research-findings-registry.json` into the existing `step_compile_research` unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:64:- **`commands/deep/start-research-loop.md`** — argument-hint updated with fan-out flags; Default Resolution Table gains `fanout_executors` and `fanout_concurrency` rows; PARSE step 3 gains `--executor` (repeatable, with `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count` per group), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`, and the default policy (0–1 executor → `config.executor`; 2+ / `--executors` / `count>1` → `config.fanout`); EXAMPLES updated with 3 fan-out scenarios (2-CLI parallel, native+CLI mixed, JSON escape hatch) and a native fan-out note.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:68:- **`deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md`** — Script table extended with 4 new rows (`fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`). Fan-out isolation invariant documented: per-lineage `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override, per-lineage `session_id` for coverage-graph DB writes (no schema change), per-kind `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` for lockfile isolation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:101:All fan-out steps are gated on `config.fanout` being present:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:102:- `step_resolve_artifact_root`: `if_absent` branch runs the byte-identical original `resolveArtifactRoot` node command when `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` is not set.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:103:- `step_fanout_spawn`: `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"` — completely skipped in single mode.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:104:- `step_fanout_merge`: `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"` — completely skipped in single mode.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:187:  - `fanoutConfigSchema` = `{ executors: lineageExecutorSchema[] (min 1), concurrency: int≥1 (default 2) }`

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md:170:**Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:36:- Schema/flag design: an `iterationPlan` of ordered bands (from-to or count; executor kind and model; optional focus/dimensions; optional dependsOn/order) plus the `iteration-plan` (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) flag desugaring one-to-one into the existing `executors` lineage array, integrated through `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts` (reuse-first; no parallel dispatcher).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:39:- Per-iteration/per-band executor audit and tracing (band and model attribution) in the JSONL state log, deltas, and findings registry; plus merge-precedence as an ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` preserving review severity rollup and research dedup/attribution.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:7:Model an iteration schedule as an ordered sequence of single-executor lineages (bands) inside the existing fan-out machinery, not as new intra-loop executor rotation. Reframe "nine times M2.7 then one time Opus" as two homogeneous bands the system already supports: band A executor `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` iterations 9; band B executor `claude-opus-4-8` iterations 1, `dependsOn` A. Add a thin `iterationPlan` flag (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) over the existing `executors` schema that desugars one-to-one into ordered lineages plus a minimal sequencing hint, reusing `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts`. Per-band focus/dimensions become optional fields threaded into existing per-iteration prompt construction; merge precedence (Opus overrides MiniMax on conflict) is an ordered band-priority list consumed by `fanout-merge.cjs`, keeping severity rollup as an invariant above priority. Single-executor and homogeneous fan-out stay byte-identical; the mode is opt-in, absent-by-default. Reuse the deep-research-before-build convention if siblings use it, front-loading research to lock the desugaring contract against the real fan-out APIs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:11:2. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:102:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:4909:P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:341, `spawnSync` blocks the Node event loop inside the pool worker, so the capped pool cannot actually keep multiple lineage subprocesses in flight. This makes CLI fan-out effectively serial despite `config.fanout.concurrency`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:4913:Fix: launch the existing deep-loop command path per lineage with explicit `config.executor`, artifact-dir override, `session_id`, and max-iteration bindings.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:4923:[{"id":"f-p123-i2-01","severity":"P0","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs","line":341,"issue":"spawnSync blocks the Node event loop inside the pool worker, so the capped pool cannot actually keep multiple lineage subprocesses in flight. This makes CLI fan-out effectively serial despite config.fanout.concurrency.","fix":"Replace spawnSync with async spawn/promise settlement, preserving cwd/env/input/timeout/log capture."},{"id":"f-p123-i2-02","severity":"P0","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs","line":131,"issue":"The spawned lineage receives a custom prompt to read the skill and run phases, rather than invoking the existing deep-loop command/YAML with a synthesized per-lineage config. That diverges from the packet requirement that each lineage runs the existing command verbatim, and leaves command parsing, validation, session binding, and max-iteration behavior to model interpretation.","fix":"Launch the existing deep-loop command path per lineage with explicit config.executor, artifact-dir override, session_id, and max-iteration bindings."},{"id":"f-p123-i2-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs","line":155,"issue":"lineage.iterations is used only to size the subprocess timeout, not forwarded into the loop as max_iterations. A configured per-lineage iteration cap can therefore change timeout behavior without changing the actual loop cap.","fix":"Pass lineage.iterations ?? default into the per-lineage command/config as max_iterations."}]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:4932:P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:341, `spawnSync` blocks the Node event loop inside the pool worker, so the capped pool cannot actually keep multiple lineage subprocesses in flight. This makes CLI fan-out effectively serial despite `config.fanout.concurrency`.

That diverges from the packet requirement that each lineage runs the existing command verbatim, and leaves command parsing, validation, session binding, and max-iteration behavior to model interpretation.","fix":"Launch the existing deep-loop command path per lineage with explicit config.executor, artifact-dir override, session_id, and max-iteration bindings."},{"id":"f-p123-i2-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs","line":155,"issue":"lineage.iterations is used only to size the subprocess timeout, not forwarded into the loop as max_iterations. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:2:You are a senior code reviewer performing ONE iteration (iteration 3) of an autonomous deep-review loop over the work in packet 123 (deep-loop native parallel fan-out): the deep-loop-runtime fanout scripts, executor-config/audit libs, command flag surface, the four deep YAMLs, tests, and the 001-007 spec children.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:286:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:746:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/006-command-surface-docs-parity/spec.md:30:- `commands/deep/start-research-loop.md` + `start-review-loop.md` §0: repeatable `--executor` (groups trailing `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count`), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`; Default Resolution Table + PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS additions; fan-out EXAMPLES.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:748:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/006-command-surface-docs-parity/spec.md:35:- **Final parity gate (non-negotiable):** single-executor run byte-identical to pre-change `main` (config, state.jsonl modulo timestamps, iteration md, research.md/review-report.md).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:907:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:63:- **`kind` vs `type` — INCONSISTENT across the two YAMLs (grep-verified):** the dispatch step `step_dispatch_iteration` branches on **`config.executor.type`** in `deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml` (L573) but on **`config.executor.kind`** in `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` (L696). Both work because `normalizeExecutorConfigInput` (executor-config.ts) aliases `type`→`kind`, and the canonical schema field is `kind`. Phase 3/6 must tolerate BOTH spellings (and ideally normalize research to `kind` while there). Fan-out config entries use `kind`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:911:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:96:- New `step_fanout_merge` at the TOP of each `phase_synthesis`, gated on `config.fanout` present (absent ⇒ synthesis unchanged):
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:915:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:103:- `commands/deep/start-research-loop.md` + `start-review-loop.md` §0: repeatable `--executor` (groups trailing `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count`), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`; extend the Default Resolution Table + PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS; add fan-out EXAMPLES. **Default policy: 0–1 executor & no `--executors` ⇒ write `config.executor` (single, unchanged, documented as the recommended default); 2+ / `--executors` / any `count>1` ⇒ write `config.fanout`.** Add the both-present fail-fast here.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:917:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:105:- **Final parity gate (non-negotiable):** a single-executor run is byte-identical to pre-change `main` (config, state.jsonl modulo timestamps, iteration md, research.md/review-report.md). Snapshot-diff a real single-executor run.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:1864:.opencode/skills/deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-single-executor-parity-review.md
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:3393:.opencode/skills/deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/08--fanout/fanout-single-executor-parity-research.md
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7766:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:39:- Per-iteration/per-band executor audit and tracing (band and model attribution) in the JSONL state log, deltas, and findings registry; plus merge-precedence as an ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` preserving review severity rollup and research dedup/attribution.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7775:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:7:Model an iteration schedule as an ordered sequence of single-executor lineages (bands) inside the existing fan-out machinery, not as new intra-loop executor rotation. Reframe "nine times M2.7 then one time Opus" as two homogeneous bands the system already supports: band A executor `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` iterations 9; band B executor `claude-opus-4-8` iterations 1, `dependsOn` A. Add a thin `iterationPlan` flag (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) over the existing `executors` schema that desugars one-to-one into ordered lineages plus a minimal sequencing hint, reusing `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts`. Per-band focus/dimensions become optional fields threaded into existing per-iteration prompt construction; merge precedence (Opus overrides MiniMax on conflict) is an ordered band-priority list consumed by `fanout-merge.cjs`, keeping severity rollup as an invariant above priority. Single-executor and homogeneous fan-out stay byte-identical; the mode is opt-in, absent-by-default. Reuse the deep-research-before-build convention if siblings use it, front-loading research to lock the desugaring contract against the real fan-out APIs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7776:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/plan.md:11:2. T2: front-loaded research — document real interfaces of `executor-config.ts`, `step_fanout_spawn`/`step_fanout_spawn_cli` (both auto YAMLs), `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`; capture exact entry points and the desugaring target fields.

The first repair aligned the OVERVIEW anchor identity so the table of contents, the HTML anchor, and the internal comment marker all point to the same destination. The second repair extracted a shared truncation helper so long session summaries stop at a clean word boundary and carry the canonical Unicode ellipsis instead of drifting between punctuation styles. The fixture describes a realistic verification pass where the helper was reused by the observation summary path, the OVERVIEW owner adopted the same contract, the compiled CLI replay was exercised with explicit JSON input, and the temporary replay artifacts were removed immediately after the check so the packet memory store stayed clean for the real documentation .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:9580:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-run-cli-lineage-spawn.md:30:- Expected signals: `lineages/lineage-a/` and `lineages/lineage-b/` dirs created; `.executor-state/` paths distinct for same-kind replicas; `orchestration-summary.json` present with `total_cli_lineages=2`; `orchestration-status.log` has JSONL events; native-only config returns `{status:"ok", results:[]}`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:10108:    36	- Schema/flag design: an `iterationPlan` of ordered bands (from-to or count; executor kind and model; optional focus/dimensions; optional dependsOn/order) plus the `iteration-plan` (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) flag desugaring one-to-one into the existing `executors` lineage array, integrated through `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts` (reuse-first; no parallel dispatcher).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:10111:    39	- Per-iteration/per-band executor audit and tracing (band and model attribution) in the JSONL state log, deltas, and findings registry; plus merge-precedence as an ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` preserving review severity rollup and research dedup/attribution.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:10381:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2776:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/gpt55/iterations/iteration-001.md:107:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
