Track A live acceptance\"\n    blockers:\n      - \"Track A live MCP rescan on 026/009 still pending\"\n    completion_pct: 95\n    status: \"code-complete-pending-track-a-live-rescan\"\n    open_questions: []\n    answered_questions: []\ntemplate_source_marker: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: tasks-core + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n---\n# Task Breakdown: Memory Indexer Invariants\n\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: tasks-core + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:notation -->\n## Task Notation\n\n| Prefix | Meaning |\n|--------|---------|\n| `[ ]` | Pending |\n| `[x]` | Completed with evidence in `checklist.md` or `implementation-summary.md` |\n| `[P]` | Parallelizable |\n| `[B]` | Blocked |\n\n**Task Format**: `T### [P?] Description (file path)`\n\n### AI Execution Protocol\n\n**Pre-Task Checklist**\n- Re-read each runtime file immediately before patching it.\n- Keep edits inside this packet, `mcp_server/`, `scripts/memory/`, and the 026 parent metadata.\n- Run packet validation, focused tests, and cleanup verify before marking a task complete.\n\n**Execution Rules**\n\n| Rule ID | Rule | Why |\n|---------|------|-----|\n| TASK-SEQ-A | Track A Fix A lands before Fix B (canonical path plumbing → A2 guard → fromScan wiring). | A2 depends on `canonical_file_path` being available in `SimilarMemory`. |\n| TASK-SEQ-B | Track B shared helper lands before scanner and save-path callers. | Prevents policy drift from reappearing during the patch sequence. |\n| TASK-SCOPE | Track A and Track B edits share `memory-save.ts`; re-read before each patch. | Avoid accidentally reverting either track while editing the shared file. |\n| TASK-DB | Every cleanup mutation runs inside one SQLite transaction; `--apply` rebuilds its plan inside the transaction. | Prevents partial cleanup states and TOCTOU gaps. |\n| TASK-VERIFY | Record exact exit codes and before/after counts in `implementation-summary.md` and `checklist.md`. | Keeps verification auditable. |\n\n**Status Reporting Format**\n- Start state: which track and runtime surface is being changed.\n- Work state: which invariant, remediation wave, or cleanup pass is active.\n- End state: validation / tests / cleanup outcome — passing, pending, or blocked.\n\n**Blocked Task Protocol**\n- Mark a task `[B]` if a schema relationship or DB reference cannot be updated safely.\n- Stop the cleanup apply path on the first SQL error and preserve rollback.\n- Record blockers in `implementation-summary.md` before continuing elsewhere.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:notation -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:phase-1 -->\n## Phase 1: Setup\n\n### Investigation and Baseline\n\n- [x] **T-01** Read `/tmp/codex-lineage-investigation-output.txt` and confirm Track A root causes. [EVIDENCE: investigation summary matches the PE lineage path and the scan-recheck race.]\n- [x] **T-02** Read the live code paths before editing: PE gating, prediction-error decision logic, scan batching, reconsolidation bridge, save-time transaction flow, memory discovery, spec-doc classification, code-graph walkers. [EVIDENCE: `pe-gating.ts`, `prediction-error-gate.ts`, `memory-index.ts`, `reconsolidation-bridge.ts`, `memory-save.ts`, `memory-index-discovery.ts`, `spec-doc-paths.ts`, `structural-indexer.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-03** Live-inspect the Voyage-4 DB for Track B baseline counts. [EVIDENCE: 5700 constitutional / 2 in-folder / 5947 z_future / 0 external / duplicate gate-enforcement rule file at ids 1 + 9868.]\n- [x] **T-04** Create shared index-scope helper for memory and code graph. [EVIDENCE: `mcp_server/lib/utils/index-scope.ts` with `shouldIndexForMemory`, `shouldIndexForCodeGraph`, `EXCLUDED_FOR_MEMORY`.]\n- [x] **T-05** Create realpath canonicalization helper. [EVIDENCE: `mcp_server/lib/utils/canonical-path.ts` wraps `fs.realpathSync` with fail-open fallback.]\n- [x] **T-06** Centralize governance-audit action strings and helpers. [EVIDENCE: `mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts` exports `GOVERNANCE_AUDIT_ACTIONS`, `recordTierDowngradeAudit()`, `buildGovernanceLogicalKey()`.]\n<!-- /ANCHOR:phase-1 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:phase-2 -->\n## Phase 2: Implementation\n\n### Track A — Lineage and Concurrency\n\n- [x] **T-10** Add canonical path plumbing to `SimilarMemory` so PE orchestration can reason about same-file identity. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/save/types.ts`, `handlers/pe-gating.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-11** Implement Fix A with A2: downgrade cross-file `UPDATE` and `REINFORCE` decisions to `CREATE` in PE orchestration. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/save/pe-orchestration.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-12** Roll back Fix B1 by removing the forced `scanBatchSize = 1` override. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-index.ts` uses `BATCH_SIZE`.]\n- [x] **T-13** Implement Fix B2: mark scan-originated saves with `fromScan: true` and skip the transactional reconsolidation recheck only for those saves. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-index.ts`, `handlers/memory-save.ts`.]\n\n### Track B — Baseline Invariants\n\n- [x] **T-20** Wire memory discovery and spec-doc classification to the shared helper. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-index-discovery.ts`, `lib/config/spec-doc-paths.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-21** Align `isMemoryFile()` with the rule-file-only constitutional policy. [EVIDENCE: `lib/parsing/memory-parser.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-22** Add save-time path rejection and constitutional-tier downgrade. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-save.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-23** [P] Wire code-graph recursive and specific-file scanning to the shared helper while preserving existing excludes. [EVIDENCE: `code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts`, `code-graph/lib/indexer-types.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-24** [P] Add cleanup CLI with dry-run, apply, and verify modes. [EVIDENCE: `scripts/memory/cleanup-index-scope-violations.ts`.]\n\n### Track B — Wave-1 Storage-Layer Remediation\n\n- [x] **T-30** Hoist the constitutional-tier guard into SQL-layer update writes. [EVIDENCE: `lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-31** Protect post-insert metadata tier writes. [EVIDENCE: `lib/storage/post-insert-metadata.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-32** Re-assert index-scope and constitutional-tier invariants during checkpoint restore inside the barrier-held transaction. [EVIDENCE: `lib/storage/checkpoints.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-33** Emit durable `governance_audit` rows for non-constitutional tier downgrade attempts without failing the write path. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-save.ts`, `lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts`, `lib/storage/checkpoints.ts` emit `action='tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path'`.]\n\n### Track B — Wave-2 Hardening\n\n- [x] **T-40** Close the cleanup-script audit gap: retain historical `governance_audit` rows for deleted memories; emit `tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path_cleanup` per downgraded row. [EVIDENCE: `scripts/memory/cleanup-index-scope-violations.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-41** Collapse spec-doc exclusion drift onto `index-scope.ts` as SSOT; prove the helper drives classification + discovery. [EVIDENCE: `lib/config/spec-doc-paths.ts`, `handlers/memory-index-discovery.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-42** Harden save-time and code-graph `specificFiles` checks with `fs.realpathSync` canonicalization. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-save.ts`, `code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-43** Move cleanup apply planning onto the transaction snapshot; add walker DoS caps for `.gitignore` size, depth, and node count. [EVIDENCE: `scripts/memory/cleanup-index-scope-violations.ts`, `handlers/memory-index-discovery.ts`, `code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-44** Extend `constitutional → critical` update auditing and refresh operator + spec docs. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-crud-update.ts`, `api/index.ts`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md`.]\n<!-- /ANCHOR:phase-2 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:phase-3 -->\n## Phase 3: Verification\n\n### Focused Test Coverage\n\n- [x] **T-50** Add Fix A regression for `tasks.md` vs sibling checklist. [EVIDENCE: `tests/pe-orchestration.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-51** Replace the old B1 scan serialization test with B2 coverage for `fromScan` propagation and the non-scan control path. [EVIDENCE: `tests/handler-memory-index.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-52** [P] Add focused exclusion and constitutional-tier Vitest coverage. [EVIDENCE: `tests/index-scope.vitest.ts`, `tests/memory-save-index-scope.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-53** [P] Add SQL-layer update + checkpoint-restore invariant coverage. [EVIDENCE: `tests/memory-crud-update-constitutional-guard.vitest.ts`, `tests/checkpoint-restore-invariant-enforcement.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-54** [P] Add Wave-2 coverage for cleanup audits, SSOT unification, realpath, walker DoS caps, and governance helpers. [EVIDENCE: `tests/cleanup-script-audit-emission.vitest.ts`, `tests/exclusion-ssot-unification.vitest.ts`, `tests/symlink-realpath-hardening.vitest.ts`, `stress_test/code-graph/walker-dos-caps.vitest.ts`, `tests/memory-governance.vitest.ts`.]\n\n### Build and Suite\n\n- [x] **T-60** Run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` for `mcp_server` and `scripts`. [EVIDENCE: all four exit `0`.]\n- [x] **T-61** Run the Track A focused regression pair. [EVIDENCE: `26/26` passed.]\n- [x] **T-62** Run the Wave-2 focused Vitest set. [EVIDENCE: `20/20` passed across 8 files.]\n- [x] **T-63** Run the README-regression set. [EVIDENCE: `218/218` passed across 4 files.]\n- [x] **T-64** Run `timeout 300 npm run test:core` and record carryover failures honestly. [EVIDENCE: exit `124`; unrelated failures in `tests/copilot-hook-wiring.vitest.ts` and `tests/stage3-rerank-regression.vitest.ts` reproduced in isolation.]\n- [x] **T-65** Run packet strict validation. [EVIDENCE: `validate.sh --strict --no-recursive` exit `0`.]\n\n### Live Cleanup\n\n- [x] **T-70** Run cleanup dry-run / apply / verify against the Voyage-4 DB. [EVIDENCE: `--apply` deleted 5945 vector rows, 254 memory rows, 252 history rows, 251 lineage rows, 9 feedback rows, 10811 other reference rows; rewrote 2 feedback + 3 lineage refs; `--verify` exit `0` with final counts `constitutional_total=2`, `constitutional_in_folder=2`, `z_future_rows=0`, `external_rows=0`, `invalid_constitutional_rows=0`.]\n- [x] **T-71** Update operator README with invariants, helper location, and stable governance-audit action strings. [EVIDENCE: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md` \"Index Scope Invariants\" section.]\n\n### Root Merge Restructure\n\n- [x] **T-80** Merge Track A and Track B phase docs into root `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, `decision-record.md`, `implementation-summary.md`. [EVIDENCE: root docs rewritten with consolidated context; no copy/paste.]\n- [x] **T-81** Update root `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` to reflect merged state (no children). [EVIDENCE: `children_ids=[]`, merged `key_files`, `trigger_phrases`, and `entities`.]\n- [x] **T-82** Remove `001-memory-indexer-lineage-and-concurrency-fix/` and `002-index-scope-and-constitutional-tier-invariants/` phase folders. [EVIDENCE: `git rm -r`.]\n- [x] **T-83** Add legacy-path aliases to `graph-metadata.json.aliases` so external cross-references keep resolving. [EVIDENCE: aliases array contains the two flat pre-move paths and the two phase-path forms.]\n\n### Pending Live Acceptance\n\n- [ ] **T-90** Restart MCP, rerun `memory_index_scan` on `026/007-hook-parity`, and confirm zero `E_LINEAGE` plus zero `candidate_changed`. (BLOCKED: requires user restart + live embedding-capable runtime.)\n<!-- /ANCHOR:phase-3 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:completion -->\n## Completion Criteria\n\n- [x] All Track A code changes landed with regression coverage\n- [x] All Track B invariants enforced at every mutation surface (discovery, save, SQL update, post-insert, checkpoint restore, code-graph scan)\n- [x] Wave-1 + Wave-2 remediations merged with focused tests\n- [x] Cleanup CLI dry-run / apply / verify exited `0` against the live DB\n- [x] Operator README + spec docs reflect the final invariant set\n- [x] Phase folders merged into root docs\n- [ ] Track A live MCP rescan on `026/007-hook-parity` — pending user restart + embedding-capable runtime\n<!-- /ANCHOR:completion -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:cross-refs -->\n## Cross-References\n\n- **Specification**: `spec.md`\n- **Plan**: `plan.md`\n- **Checklist**: `checklist.md`\n- **Decisions**: `decision-record.md`\n- **Implementation Summary**: `implementation-summary.md`\n- **Investigation Source**: `/tmp/codex-lineage-investigation-output.txt`\n<!-- /ANCHOR:cross-refs -->",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:10868:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9359:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3286:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/016-llama-cpp-retrieval-quality-probe/scratch/probe-corpus.json:1095:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"...kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/005-opencode-plugin-loader-bridge-fixes/decision-record]\"\ndescription: \"ADRs for scoping decisions. ADR-001 captures the OpenCode 1.3.17 plugin discovery contract, ADR-002/003 record loader remediation, and ADR-004 records the skill-advisor hook remap from ignored Claude-style hooks to OpenCode event + experimental.chat.system.transform.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"026/009/007 adr\"\n  - \"opencode plugin loader decisions\"\nimportance_tier: \"important\"\ncontextType: \"planning\"\ntemplate_source_hint: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/005-opencode-plugin-loader-bridge-fixes\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-28T19:30:00Z\"\n    last_updated_by: \"codex-gpt-5-hygiene-pass\"\n    recent_action: \"Hygiene pass - validator structure\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Keep validators green\"\n    completion_pct: 100\ntemplate_source_marker: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n---\n# Decision Record: OpenCode Plugin Loader Remediation\n\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001 -->\n## ADR-001: OpenCode 1.3.17 plugin discovery contract — empirical baseline\n\n**Status**: Accepted (Phase 1 probe complete, 2026-04-22T15:12:00+02:00).\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-context -->\n### Context\n\nOpenCode 1.3.17 ships as a single bun-bundled binary. The TUI worker auto-discovers plugins from `.opencode/plugins/` at startup. Empirical observation (drafting session, 2026-04-22T13:30Z): the worker crashes with `TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'plugin2.auth')` when a discovered file resolves to `undefined` because it lacks a default export. The crash occurs at `/$bunfs/root/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.js:301126:25` — a bundled, non-source-readable location.\n\nThe contract details (glob shape, extension filter, opt-out mechanism, undefined-handling, recommended helper-module location) were confirmed from official docs, the installed package type contract, the installed 1.3.17 binary strings, and empirical TUI probes.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-context -->\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-decision -->\n### Decision\n\nTreat `.opencode/plugins/` as a flat plugin-entrypoint directory. In OpenCode 1.3.17, the server-side local plugin scanner loads JavaScript/TypeScript files from `{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}` under each config directory. The official documentation says files placed in `.opencode/plugins/` and `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` are automatically loaded at startup and that a plugin module exports plugin functions. The installed `@opencode-ai/plugin` types define a `Plugin` as a function returning `Hooks`, while the 1.3.17 binary still supports a legacy path that treats every exported function in a loaded `.js` module as a plugin function. That legacy behavior is the immediate null-hook source for `spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`: its named `parseTransportPlan` export is invoked as a second plugin, returns `null` for a plugin input object, and later the auth loop crashes while reading `plugin2.auth`.\n\n**Discovery contract matrix**:\n\n| Aspect                            | Observed in OpenCode 1.3.17 | Evidence |\n|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------|----------|\n| Discovery glob                    | `{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}` from config directories | `strings /Users/michelkerkmeester/.opencode/bin/opencode | rg \"{plugin,plugins}\"`; newer local source snapshot has the same `Glob.scan(\"{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}\", { dot: true, symlink: true })` shape |\n| Recursive vs flat                 | Flat only                   | The glob has a single `*` segment, not `**/*`; helper subdirectories are outside the discovered entrypoint set |\n| Extension filter                  | `.ts` and `.js` only in the installed 1.3.17 binary | Binary string evidence; official docs say JavaScript/TypeScript files, and do not mention `.mjs` |\n| Default-export shape required     | V1 modules default-export an object with `server()` or `tui()`; legacy modules may export plugin functions directly | `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts`; binary strings for `must default export an object with server()` and `Plugin export is not a function` |\n| Behavior on missing default export | Missing default is not enough by itself if no exported functions exist, but any loaded exported function returning `null`/`undefined` leaves a null hook and crashes at auth iteration | Probe + log: TUI emitted `TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'plugin2.auth')`; log recorded project `.js` plugins loaded and `plugin config hook failed` with `hook.config` null |\n| Behavior on `export default null` | Equivalent risk if the loaded plugin resolves to `null` | Inferred from auth loop string `plugin2.auth` and the observed null-hook crash |\n| Behavior on `export default undefined` | Equivalent risk if the loaded plugin resolves to `undefined`/missing hook object | Inferred from legacy loader contract and observed null-hook crash |\n| Opt-out: filename prefix          | No documented opt-out found | Official docs list plugin directories and npm config; no filename-prefix exclusion documented |\n| Opt-out: opencode.json `plugins.exclude` | No documented opt-out found | Official config docs expose `plugin` package list, not local-folder exclude rules |\n| Opt-out: manifest file            | No documented manifest opt-out for local plugin folders | Installed package type contract and official docs do not define a local manifest exclusion |\n| Recommended helper-module location | Any sibling folder outside `{plugin,plugins}/` is safe; use `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` locally | Flat glob evidence plus the packet's Outcome A target layout |\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-decision -->\n### Methodology\n\n1. Checked official OpenCode plugin docs: local files in `.opencode/plugins/` and `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` are startup-loaded, and plugin modules export plugin functions.\n2. Inspected the installed dependency at `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/`; the local install is `@opencode-ai/plugin` package metadata `1.3.15` under a `1.3.17` OpenCode binary, but the exposed `Plugin`/`Hooks` contract is sufficient for the hook-object shape.\n3. Inspected the installed 1.3.17 binary with `strings` to recover the local glob and auth-loop crash site.\n4. Ran empirical probes with `XDG_STATE_HOME=/tmp/opencode-state XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/opencode-data XDG_CACHE_HOME=/tmp/opencode-cache` so the sandbox did not block OpenCode state writes. `opencode --version` returned `1.3.17`; `timeout 4 opencode` reproduced the `plugin2.auth` JSON envelope.\n5. Checked structured logs under `/tmp/opencode-data/opencode/log/`; they showed the two project `.js` plugins loaded, no `.mjs` helpers loaded, and a null `hook.config` failure before the TUI bootstrap error.\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-consequences -->\n### Consequences\n\n- **Positive**: ADR-002 can choose the remediation outcome with full confidence in the contract semantics.\n- **Positive**: The contract documentation becomes a reusable reference for any future OpenCode plugin authoring or upgrade probe.\n- **Negative**: Phase 1 must complete before Phase 2 can begin; partial-knowledge implementation risks shipping against assumption.\n\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001 -->\n\n---\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-consequences -->\n### ADR-002: Remediation outcome selection (A / B / C)\n\n**Status**: Accepted.\n\n### Context\n\nThree feasible remediation paths emerged during drafting. The choice depends on ADR-001's confirmation of the OpenCode plugin discovery contract.\n\n### Decision\n\nChoose **Outcome A: move helpers out of `.opencode/plugins/`**, with one compatibility correction inside the already in-scope compact plugin: make the parser helper safe when OpenCode 1.3.17's legacy loader invokes named function exports as plugins. This preserves the packet's primary file-organization outcome and addresses the empirically observed null-hook crash source.\n\n**Decision matrix**:\n\n| # | Outcome                                                                                | Mechanism                                                                                                                | Feasibility (depends on ADR-001) | Eng. cost | Runtime cost | Coverage / Risk |\n|---|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|-----------|--------------|-----------------|\n| A | **Move helpers out of `.opencode/plugins/`**                                           | `git mv` 3 helper files to `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/`; update `BRIDGE_PATH` constants and schema-helper import; guard the compact plugin's named parser export against legacy-loader invocation | HIGH — discovery is flat and outside sibling folders | Low (~30-60 min) | Negligible | Chosen. Eliminates helper drift, documents entrypoints-only convention, and fixes the observed null hook. |\n| B | **Add no-op default exports to the 3 helper files**                                    | Append `export default async function() { return {}; }` to each helper                                                   | LOW benefit — installed 1.3.17 does not load `.mjs`; would not address the named parser export | Lowest (~10 min) | Marginal — zombie plugins if extensions change later | Rejected. Masks folder impurity and does not match the empirical crash source. |\n| C | **Use OpenCode opt-out mechanism (filename prefix or opencode.json exclude)**          | Rename helpers to `_<name>.mjs` or add `plugins.exclude: [\"*-bridge.mjs\", \"*-schema.mjs\"]` to opencode.json              | INFEASIBLE — no documented local-folder opt-out surfaced | Low (~30 min) | Negligible | Rejected. Depends on unsupported behavior. |\n\n**Selected primary path**: A (full isolation plus legacy export hardening). It does not depend on undocumented OpenCode behavior, has a clear file-system convention, and handles the observed 1.3.17 legacy loader behavior.\n\n**Fallback if A is infeasible** (e.g., discovery is recursive into `.opencode/`): B is the safest universal fallback.\n\n**Use C only if** ADR-001 surfaces a stable, documented opt-out — otherwise its dependency on undocumented behavior makes it brittle across OpenCode upgrades.\n\n### Consequences (Outcome A)\n\n- **Positive**: TUI starts cleanly. Plugin folder becomes self-documenting (every file is an entrypoint).\n- **Positive**: Future contributors cannot accidentally re-introduce the crash by adding a helper next to a plugin (regression test catches it).\n- **Positive**: Bridge subprocess architecture preserved — bridges still run as spawned `node` processes, just from a different parent path.\n- **Positive**: The compact plugin's test-only parser helper no longer creates a null hook if a legacy loader treats it as a plugin function.\n- **Negative**: One extra folder (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/`) to know about. Mitigated by README convention doc.\n- **Negative**: Move breaks existing bridge path references — T-10 inventory governs which references are updated.\n\n### References\n\n- Reproduction evidence: drafting session 2026-04-22T13:30Z reproduced the crash in both Public root and Barter.\n- Official docs: https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/ and https://opencode.ai/docs/config/\n- Installed package: `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/` (inspected during Phase 1).\n- Installed binary: `/Users/michelkerkmeester/.opencode/bin/opencode` and wrapper `/Users/michelkerkmeester/.superset/bin/opencode`.\n- Bundled worker: `/$bunfs/root/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.js` (non-source-readable; behavior inferred from crash signature, binary strings, logs, and empirical probe).\n- Sibling phases: `../002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/`, `../003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/` (different runtime, different gap).\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-impl -->\n### ADR-003: Implementation outcome — Outcome A shipped\n\n**Status**: Accepted (implemented 2026-04-22T15:21Z).\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-impl -->\n### Context\n\nPhase 2 implemented the ADR-002 selection after the empirical contract investigation. The helper files were removed from `.opencode/plugins/`, which is now documented as an entrypoint-only folder.\n\nOne additional code-level compatibility correction was necessary inside the in-scope compact plugin. The installed 1.3.17 loader has a legacy path that can treat named function exports as plugin factories. `parseTransportPlan()` is exported for tests; when invoked with a plugin context object instead of a response string, it previously returned `null`, which could reproduce the same null-hook failure class. The parser now returns an empty hook object for non-string input, preserving its test utility while avoiding a null hook under legacy loader invocation.\n\n### Decision\n\nShip Outcome A with these implementation details:\n\n| Area | Outcome |\n|------|---------|\n| Helper location | `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` |\n| Plugin folder convention | `.opencode/plugins/` contains only `spec-kit-skill-advisor.js`, `spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`, and `README.md` |\n| Advisor bridge | `BRIDGE_PATH` now targets `../plugin-helpers/spec-kit-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs` |\n| Compact bridge | `BRIDGE_PATH` now targets `../plugin-helpers/spec-kit-compact-code-graph-bridge.mjs` |\n| Schema helper | compact plugin imports `../plugin-helpers/spec-kit-opencode-message-schema.mjs` |\n| Legacy loader hardening | `parseTransportPlan(nonString)` returns `{}` instead of `null` |\n| Regression guard | `opencode-plugins-folder-purity.vitest.ts` dynamically imports every plugin-folder `*.js`, `*.mjs`, and `*.ts` file and requires a default export |\n\n### Evidence\n\n- `ls .opencode/plugins/` shows only `README.md`, `spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`, and `spec-kit-skill-advisor.js`.\n- `ls .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` shows the three relocated helper files.\n- `node --check` passed for both plugin entrypoint files.\n- Direct advisor bridge smoke returned a live advisor brief.\n- Direct compact bridge smoke returned transport JSON.\n- Direct legacy parser guard smoke returned `{}` for a plugin-like object.\n- Regression guard failed with a temporary no-default-export stub and passed after removal.\n- Focused plugin-loader vitest set passed 15/15.\n- `npm run build` passed.\n\n### Limitations\n\n- `git mv` could not take `.git/index.lock` in this sandbox, so the move was performed at the filesystem level. The worktree shows delete/add pairs until a later staging operation records them as renames.\n- Exact home-state `opencode` smokes no longer emitted `plugin2.auth` but hit sandbox state/DB permission errors. XDG-writable smokes reached OpenCode TUI/server bootstrap logs without `plugin2.auth`.\n- Full `npx vitest run` is blocked by an out-of-scope Copilot hook wiring mismatch: `copilot-hook-wiring.vitest.ts` expects repo-local hook commands while current `.github/hooks/superset-notify.json` points to Superset hook commands.\n\n---\n\n### ADR-004: Skill-advisor plugin hook-name remap to OpenCode API\n\n**Status**: Accepted (2026-04-23, follow-up to ADR-003 after live TUI smoke).\n\n### Context\n\nAfter Outcome A shipped and the loader crash was fixed, a real OpenCode TUI session was used to test plugin behavior using natural prompts. The skill-advisor plugin's `spec_kit_skill_advisor_status` tool was registered correctly (`tool:` key is in OpenCode's `Hooks` API), but the per-prompt advisor brief never appeared in any conversation turn. Investigation of `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts` (Hooks interface, lines 142-260) showed the cause:\n\nThe skill-advisor plugin returns Claude-Code-style hook names that OpenCode 1.3.17 silently ignores:\n\n| Plugin returns                | OpenCode `Hooks` recognizes |\n|-------------------------------|-----------------------------|\n| `onSessionStart()`            | NOT in API — silently ignored |\n| `onUserPromptSubmitted(input)`| NOT in API — silently ignored |\n| `onSessionEnd(input)`         | NOT in API — silently ignored |\n| `tool: { ... }`               | RECOGNIZED — tool registered |\n\nOpenCode's actual API for the equivalent surfaces:\n- Per-prompt brief injection → `'experimental.chat.system.transform'(input, { system })` push the brief into `output.system[]`, OR `'chat.message'(input, output)` (read-only — cannot inject context)\n- Session lifecycle observation → `event:` listener filtering for session-start/end events\n- Tool registration → already correct (`tool:` key)\n\nComparison with the sibling compact plugin in the same folder confirms this: that plugin uses `'experimental.session.compacting'`, `'experimental.chat.system.transform'`, `'experimental.chat.messages.transform'`, and `event:` correctly and its hooks fire as expected. The skill-advisor plugin is the only OpenCode plugin in the repo with this Claude-Code-API drift.\n\nThis is a pre-existing latent bug independent of packet 007's loader crash. The plugin appeared installed and the status tool worked, but the headline feature (advisor brief on every prompt) was always a no-op in OpenCode. It worked in Claude Code because `onUserPromptSubmitted` is the Claude Code hook name.\n\n### Decision\n\nRemap the skill-advisor plugin's three lifecycle hooks to OpenCode's `Hooks` API:\n\n| Current (Claude Code style) | New (OpenCode API)                                | Behavior                              |\n|-----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------|\n| `onSessionStart()`          | `event:` listener filtered to session-start event | Set `runtimeReady = true`             |\n| `onUserPromptSubmitted()`   | `'experimental.chat.system.transform'`            | Push advisor brief into `system[]`    |\n| `onSessionEnd()`            | `event:` listener filtered to session-end event   | Cache cleanup                         |\n\nKeep the `tool:` registration unchanged — already correct.\n\nKeep all existing helper functions (`getAdvisorContext`, `extractPrompt`, `sessionIdFrom`, etc.) unchanged — only the hook surface changes.\n\nMirror the compact plugin's event-listener pattern for the lifecycle observers.\n\nAdd a focused vitest covering: the plugin returns the OpenCode-shaped hook keys; `experimental.chat.system.transform` populates `system[]` with the brief; the `event:` handler filters correctly; the status tool still works.\n\n### Evidence\n\n| Check | Result |\n|-------|--------|\n| Hook-shape vitest | PASS — `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/spec-kit-skill-advisor-plugin.vitest.ts` passed 18/18, including returned hook keys `event`, `experimental.chat.system.transform`, and `tool`, and absence of `onSessionStart`, `onUserPromptSubmitted`, and `onSessionEnd`. |\n| Direct `experimental.chat.system.transform` smoke | PASS — minimal Node smoke imported the plugin, invoked `experimental.chat.system.transform`, and asserted `output.system[0]=Advisor: smoke brief landed.` |\n| Real OpenCode session shows advisor brief in conversation | NOT RUN — sandbox-friendly direct hook smoke plus focused vitest is accepted Phase 4 evidence; a real TUI smoke remains optional because the hook surface itself was invoked directly. |\n| Compact plugin behavior unchanged | PASS BY SCOPE — `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` was read as the reference pattern only and not modified in Phase 4. |\n| Strict spec validation | PASS — `bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/005-opencode-plugin-loader-bridge-fixes --strict` passed with 0 errors / 0 warnings. |\n| `npm run build` in `mcp_server` | PASS — `tsc --build` completed successfully. |\n\n### Consequences\n\n- **Positive**: The advisor brief actually fires on every prompt in OpenCode (matches Claude Code parity).\n- **Positive**: Single-file change, scoped, low blast radius.\n- **Positive**: Documents OpenCode vs Claude Code hook API drift for future plugin authors.\n- **Negative**: The plugin diverges from its Claude Code variant; if a shared codebase is ever desired, both sets of hook names would need to coexist.\n- **Negative**: Hook semantics differ subtly — `experimental.chat.system.transform` runs before each LLM call; legacy `onUserPromptSubmitted` ran on user submission. The brief content is recomputed on each LLM turn, which is what we want, but cache hit-rate metrics may rise.\n\n### References\n\n- OpenCode `Hooks` interface: `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts:142-260`\n- Compact plugin reference implementation: `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:329` (event listener) and `:364` (`experimental.chat.system.transform`)\n- Skill-advisor current hooks: `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-skill-advisor.js:369-390`\n- Live finding: 2026-04-23 OpenCode session in Public root, where natural prompts surfaced no `Advisor:` line in any turn\n\n---\n\n### ADR-005: Phase 5 follow-ups from real OpenCode TUI smoke\n\n**Status**: Accepted (2026-04-23, post-Phase-4 live verification).\n\n### Context\n\nAfter Phase 4 shipped, a real OpenCode TUI session in Public root verified the hook remap by running natural prompts (code review, improve prompt, architecture walkthrough, status tool). Headline result: the remap works — `bridge_invocations=8`, model exhibits skill-routing behavior (sk-code-review structure, sk-improve-prompt clarifying-question pattern). Briefs reach the model context.\n\nThe status tool surfaced three quantified anomalies and Codex independently raised five code-quality findings during the test 1 review.\n\n### Quantified anomalies (status tool snapshot)\n\n```\nruntime_ready=false       <- should be true after session.created\nlast_bridge_status=skipped  <- last call returned no brief (env, not bug)\ncache_hits=8 AND cache_misses=8 with bridge_invocations=8 and cache_entries=4\n```\n\n### Decision\n\nAddress four scoped fixes in Phase 5 and explicitly defer the larger architectural changes:\n\n| Finding | Action | Rationale |\n|---------|--------|-----------|\n| `runtime_ready=false` despite session events firing | Fix: investigate the actual `@opencode-ai/sdk` Event type discriminant; retain the real `type` discriminant; stop bridge status from overwriting lifecycle readiness; mirror compact plugin pattern more precisely | Cosmetic flag is misleading; correct readiness semantics prove event hook works as designed |\n| `cache_hits == cache_misses == bridge_invocations` accounting bug | Fix: audit increment paths; ensure exactly one counter increments per lookup; expose total lookups separately when `bridge_invocations` is confirmed to mean subprocess spawns | Clear logic bug; metrics are operator-facing |\n| `output.system.push()` would throw on `output={}` or `{ system: null }` | Fix: defensive guard — initialize `output.system = output.system ?? []` at top of handler | Cheap, idiomatic, matches OpenCode contract reality |\n| Session IDs not normalized to strings before cache use | Fix: coerce `String(sessionID)` in `sessionIdFrom()` and at cache key construction sites | Defensive, cheap |\n| Module-global state can race across plugin instances | DEFER | Bigger refactor (per-instance state via WeakMap or closure capture); single-instance use is the dominant case; flag as P2 follow-up |\n| No in-flight promise dedup for concurrent identical bridge calls | DEFER | Real concern but multi-process bridge already fail-opens; flag as P2 follow-up |\n| Unbounded prompt/brief sizes | DEFER | Add later as part of broader plugin hardening if observed in production |\n\n### Evidence (to be filled post-implementation)\n\n| Check | Result |\n|-------|--------|\n| `runtime_ready` reaches `true` after first session event | PASS — SDK `Event` uses `type`; direct Node smoke with `{ type: 'session.created' }` returned `runtime_ready=true` and `last_bridge_status=ready`. Vitest also proves skipped bridge responses no longer reset readiness to false. |\n| `cache_hits + cache_misses == bridge_invocations` invariant in vitest | REFRAMED + PASS — code audit showed `bridge_invocations` counts subprocess spawns/cache misses. Status now emits `advisor_lookups`; vitest asserts `cache_misses === bridge_invocations` and `cache_hits + cache_misses === advisor_lookups`. Direct smoke returned `cache_hits=1`, `cache_misses=1`, `bridge_invocations=1`, `advisor_lookups=2`. |\n| `output.system` defensive guard handles `{}` input | PASS — `appendAdvisorBrief()` initializes `output.system = Array.isArray(output?.system) ? output.system : []`; vitest covers both `{}` and `{ system: null }`. |\n| `sessionIdFrom()` returns a string for object inputs | PASS — session IDs normalize through stable stringification; vitest passes reordered object session IDs and observes deterministic cache hits. |\n| Strict spec validation | PASS — Phase 5 `validate.sh --strict` passed 0 errors / 0 warnings. |\n\n### Consequences\n\n- **Positive**: Status tool numbers are trustworthy (operator-visible).\n- **Positive**: Plugin handler is robust to host-contract drift (defensive `output.system` guard).\n- **Positive**: P2 deferrals are explicitly tracked as follow-ups, not silent.\n- **Negative**: Module-global state and in-flight dedup remain as latent concerns; if a future change loads the plugin twice or hits high-concurrency request patterns, those issues will surface.\n\n### References\n\n- Real-session evidence: 2026-04-23 OpenCode TUI in Public root, status tool output captured by user.\n- Codex independent review (during Phase 5 test 1): 5 findings; this ADR addresses 4 directly + defers 1.\n\n---\n\n### Cross-References\n\n- **Spec**: `spec.md` §2 (problem), §4 (requirements REQ-001..REQ-013)\n- **Plan**: `plan.md` §2 quality gates, §4 phases (now 5 phases)\n- **Tasks**: `tasks.md` T-01..T-09 (Phase 1), T-10..T-19 (Phase 2), T-20..T-29 (Phase 3), T-30..T-35 (Phase 4 — hook remap), T-36..T-41 (Phase 5 — status accuracy + defensive guards)\n- **Sibling phases**: `../002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/`, `../003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/`\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-alternatives -->\n## Alternatives Considered\n\n<!-- TODO: backfill with real content; stub added by Tier 4 alignment -->\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-alternatives -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-five-checks -->\n## Five Checks Evaluation\n\n<!-- TODO: backfill with real content; stub added by Tier 4 alignment -->\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-five-checks -->",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:10869:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-004.out:9360:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3302:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/017-llama-cpp-default-flip/scratch/probe-1k-corpus.json:47:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"...ization/007-hook-parity/005-codex-hook-parity-remediation/research/007-deep-review-remediation-pt-02/research]\"\ndescription: \"Synthesis of 10 iterations investigating the exact Codex CLI 0.122.0 hook contract (stdin payload, stdout-to-model-context injection, exit codes, timeouts, concurrency, event taxonomy, env propagation). Outcome: A (full parity achievable) — Codex hooks DO inject stdout as developer-role messages, exactly the behavior needed to mirror Claude's SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit payloads.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"ization\"\n  - \"009\"\n  - \"hook\"\n  - \"daemon\"\n  - \"parity\"\n  - \"research\"\n  - \"007\"\n  - \"deep\"\nimportance_tier: \"important\"\ncontextType: \"research\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/research/007-deep-review-remediation-pt-02\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-24T15:25:01Z\"\n    last_updated_by: \"backfill-memory-block\"\n    recent_action: \"Backfilled _memory block (repo-wide frontmatter sweep)\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Revalidate packet docs and update continuity on next save\"\n    key_files: [\"research.md\"]\n---\n# Deep Research Synthesis — Codex CLI Hook Parity Contract\n\n**Spec phase**: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/`\n**Research packet**: `007-deep-review-remediation-pt-02/`\n**Iterations**: 10 (all completed; newInfoRatio trajectory 0.90 → 0.10 over final 7 iterations)\n**Executor**: `cli-codex` with gpt-5.4, reasoning `high`, service_tier `fast`, sandbox `workspace-write`\n**Outcome**: **A (full hook parity achievable)**\n\n---\n\n## 1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY\n\n**The research question**: what is the exact Codex CLI 0.122.0 hook contract — stdin payload format, stdout-to-model-context semantics, exit-code behavior, timeout budget, multi-hook concurrency, event taxonomy, env propagation — so we can port the Claude-side `hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.ts` to `hooks/codex/` without shipping against assumptions?\n\n**Short answer**: **Codex hooks DO inject stdout into the model context as developer-role messages.** This is the key capability copilot CLI lacks (per sibling phase 007). The injection surface is exactly what Claude's `SessionStart` + `UserPromptSubmit` hooks do. Full parity is achievable with a straightforward port.\n\nThe contract, confirmed across official docs + generated schemas + upstream Rust source + a working empirical probe on 0.122.0:\n\n- **Transport**: `tokio::process::Command` with piped stdin, writes a serialized JSON object containing event-specific fields.\n- **stdin schema (SessionStart)**: `{cwd, hook_event_name:\"SessionStart\", model, permission_mode, session_id, source: \"startup\"|\"resume\"|\"clear\", transcript_path}` — `additionalProperties: false`.\n- **stdin schema (UserPromptSubmit)**: `{cwd, hook_event_name:\"UserPromptSubmit\", model, permission_mode, prompt, session_id, transcript_path, turn_id}` — `additionalProperties: false`.\n- **stdout contract**: plain text stdout OR JSON `{hookSpecificOutput: {additionalContext: \"...\"}}` — both become a `role: \"developer\"` message in the transcript. SessionStart context is recorded BEFORE the user prompt; UserPromptSubmit context AFTER.\n- **Exit codes**: `0` = success + inject stdout. `2` + stderr text = block/decline (UserPromptSubmit only — aborts the turn with the stderr as reason). Other non-zero = hook failed, but turn continues (fail-open).\n- **Timeout**: per-hook `timeout` or `timeoutSec` field (seconds); default 600s. On timeout, hook result is empty; turn continues.\n- **Concurrency**: multiple matching hooks for one event run concurrently via `join_all`, NOT serial by registration order.\n- **Events (6 total)**: `SessionStart`, `PreToolUse`, `PermissionRequest`, `PostToolUse`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop`.\n- **Environment**: inherits parent process env (no `env_clear` call in the command runner). Codex prepends arg0 directories to `PATH`.\n- **Feature flag required**: `[features] codex_hooks = true` in `~/.codex/config.toml`. **Hooks do not fire without this flag** — this was confirmed empirically: on 0.122.0, the user's existing `hooks.json` registrations are currently inert because the flag is absent from their `config.toml`.\n\n**Classification**: **Outcome A (full parity)** per `plan.md` §2 Phase 1 exit criteria. Phase 2 (port + register) can proceed immediately.\n\n---\n\n## 2. KEY FINDINGS (PRIMARY-SOURCE-CITED)\n\n### F1. Command hooks receive one JSON object on stdin (not argv, not plain text)\n\n`tokio::process::Command` is spawned with piped stdin; the serialized `SessionStartCommandInput` / `UserPromptSubmitCommandInput` Rust structs are written to the child's stdin via `write_all(input_json.as_bytes())`. The user's prompt is passed in the JSON `prompt` field, NOT as raw stdin text. `additionalProperties: false` in the generated schemas — unknown fields rejected.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks (developers.openai.com)](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Schema: [session-start.command.input.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/session-start.command.input.schema.json)\n- Schema: [user-prompt-submit.command.input.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/user-prompt-submit.command.input.schema.json)\n- Source: [command_runner.rs](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/command_runner.rs)\n- Empirical: `codex-cli 0.122.0` isolated `CODEX_HOME` probe; observed stdin JSON matched schema exactly.\n\n### F2. stdout IS injected into model-visible context as developer-role messages\n\n**THIS IS THE CAPABILITY COPILOT LACKS.** For both `SessionStart` and `UserPromptSubmit`:\n\n- Plain text stdout → added as extra developer context.\n- JSON stdout with `{hookSpecificOutput: {additionalContext: \"...\"}}` → additionalContext value added as extra developer context.\n- Stdout that LOOKS like JSON but fails to match the output schema → hook marked failed (not silently injected).\n\nSource-level: both event parsers append accepted context into `additional_contexts_for_model`. `SessionStart` has a test literally named `plain_stdout_becomes_model_context`. The runtime path is `DeveloperInstructions::new(additional_context).into()` per upstream issue #16486 — confirming developer-role injection, not system-prompt replacement and not prompt substitution.\n\n**Placement (verified empirically on 0.122.0)**:\n- `SessionStart` context: recorded as a `role: \"developer\"` transcript item BEFORE the user prompt.\n- `UserPromptSubmit` context: recorded AFTER the user message, as an additional developer-role transcript item visible to the model on the same turn.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [session_start.rs parser](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/session_start.rs)\n- Source: [user_prompt_submit.rs parser](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/user_prompt_submit.rs)\n- Source: [hook_runtime.rs (runtime injection path)](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/core/src/hook_runtime.rs)\n- Schema: [user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json)\n- Upstream context: [issue #16486](https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/16486)\n- Empirical: 0.122.0 probe — stdout observed as `role: \"developer\"` in transcript items.\n\n### F3. Exit codes — `2` is the UserPromptSubmit blocker signal\n\n- **Exit 0**: success. stdout (plain or JSON additionalContext) is injected.\n- **Exit 2** + non-empty stderr: `UserPromptSubmit`-specific blocker path. Source sets `status: Blocked`, `should_stop: true`, captures stderr as reason. Turn aborts. Empirically confirmed: a probe registered with `exit 2` on UserPromptSubmit caused Codex to emit `turn.completed` with zero model tokens and the user prompt was not recorded as a user response item before completion.\n- **Other non-zero** (e.g., exit 1): hook marked failed with \"hook exited with code N\" but turn continues (fail-open). Empirically verified on 0.122.0.\n\n`PermissionRequest` has its own parallel decision contract (JSON `{\"decision\":\"deny\", \"reason\":\"...\"}` or exit 2; any deny wins over allow). `PreToolUse` has an `updatedInput` field but it's parsed-but-unsupported and fails open.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [user_prompt_submit.rs](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/user_prompt_submit.rs)\n- Source: [permission_request.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/permission_request.rs)\n- Source: [output_parser.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/output_parser.rs)\n- Empirical: 0.122.0 probe transcripts.\n\n### F4. No prompt transformation for UserPromptSubmit\n\nThe `UserPromptSubmit` output schema has `additionalProperties: false` and includes only: common fields, legacy `decision`/`reason`, `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`. There is NO `updatedInput` or equivalent prompt-rewrite field. The generated schema rejects such fields. Hooks can INJECT additional developer context alongside the user's prompt, BLOCK the prompt, but cannot REPLACE the prompt.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Schema: [user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/user-prompt-submit.command.output.schema.json)\n- Source: [user_prompt_submit.rs](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/events/user_prompt_submit.rs)\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n\n### F5. Timeout: per-hook `timeout`/`timeoutSec` in seconds, default 600s, fail-open on timeout\n\nSource: `timeout_sec.unwrap_or(600).max(1)`. On timeout, `tokio::time::timeout` returns error text `hook timed out after Ns`. Command runner produces no exit code, empty stdout/stderr. Event parsers treat that as failed-hook-run unless the event has already produced a supported decision. Empirically confirmed on 0.122.0: a `UserPromptSubmit` hook with `\"timeout\": 1` sleeping 5s caused Codex to record the user message after ~timeout and continue to the model request; the hook's post-sleep stdout was not injected.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [discovery.rs (timeout default)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/discovery.rs)\n- Source: [command_runner.rs (timeout implementation)](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/command_runner.rs)\n- Empirical: 0.122.0 timeout probe.\n\n### F6. Multiple matching hooks run CONCURRENTLY (not serially by registration order)\n\nSource: `execute_handlers` uses `futures::future::join_all(...)` to launch all matching handlers at once. Result aggregation preserves selected handler order for folding, but execution is parallel. Implication: when the user's existing Superset `notify.sh` and a new Spec Kit Memory hook are both registered for the same event, they run in parallel. Neither can prevent the other from starting. For this spec: Spec Kit Memory's hook must be idempotent and must not depend on notify.sh having already completed.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [dispatcher.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/dispatcher.rs)\n\n### F7. Event taxonomy (6 events, schema-backed, docs-matched)\n\n| Event | Can inject stdout? | Can block? | Reserved/unsupported fields |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `SessionStart` | ✓ (plain or JSON additionalContext) | — | — |\n| `UserPromptSubmit` | ✓ (plain or JSON additionalContext) | ✓ (exit 2 + stderr, or JSON decision:block) | `updatedInput` absent from schema |\n| `PreToolUse` | — | ✓ (via decision surface) | `updatedInput` parsed-but-unsupported (fail-open) |\n| `PermissionRequest` | — | ✓ (JSON decision:deny, or exit 2 + stderr) | `updatedInput`, `updatedPermissions`, `interrupt` reserved (fail-closed) |\n| `PostToolUse` | — | — | — |\n| `Stop` | — | — | — |\n\nDiscovery source enumerates exactly these six events. No undocumented events found.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Source: [discovery.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/codex/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/discovery.rs)\n- Schemas: [generated/](https://github.com/openai/codex/tree/main/codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated)\n\n### F8. Environment inherited from parent process\n\nCommand runner sets `current_dir(cwd)`, pipes stdio, and sets `kill_on_drop(true)`. It does NOT call `env_clear`, `env`, `envs`, or `env_remove`. Per Rust stdlib docs, absent those methods, `Command` inherits the current process environment. Codex prepends temporary arg0 directories to `PATH`.\n\nEmpirically confirmed on 0.122.0: probe hooks saw inherited `HOME`, `PATH` (with Codex augmentation), `OPENAI_API_KEY`, and a custom `CODEX_PROBE_CUSTOM_ENV`.\n\nNote: Codex's MODEL-REQUESTED SHELL TOOL subprocess path is different (uses `env_clear()` + explicit env map via `spawn.rs`). The `shell_environment_policy.*` config governs shell tools, NOT hooks. So hooks get the un-sanitized parent env; shell tools get the sanitized per-policy env.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Source: [command_runner.rs](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/command_runner.rs)\n- Source (contrast): [spawn.rs (shell tool env policy)](https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/codex-rs/core/src/spawn.rs)\n- Rust stdlib: [std::process::Command docs](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/process/struct.Command.html)\n- Docs: [Codex config reference](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference)\n- Empirical: 0.122.0 env probe.\n\n### F9. Feature flag: hooks require `[features] codex_hooks = true`\n\nEmpirically confirmed: on 0.122.0, hooks do NOT fire unless `[features] codex_hooks = true` is present in the effective `config.toml`. The user's current `~/.codex/config.toml` has `[features] multi_agent=true, memories=false, code_mode=true` — but `codex_hooks` is absent. That means the existing Superset `notify.sh` registrations in `~/.codex/hooks.json` are currently INERT (they don't fire). Adding `codex_hooks = true` will activate BOTH the existing notify.sh entries AND any new Spec Kit Memory entries added alongside.\n\nImplication for Phase 2: enabling the flag will start firing the Superset notify.sh for every SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/Stop event — a side-effect the user may or may not want. Confirm with the user before flipping the flag.\n\n**Sources**:\n- Docs: [Codex hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks)\n- Empirical: 0.122.0 probe — hooks silent without flag; fire correctly with flag.\n- Local config inspection: `~/.codex/config.toml` (feature flag absent 2026-04-22).\n\n---\n\n## 3. ANSWERS TO KEY QUESTIONS (from spec.md §7)\n\n| ID | Question | Answer | Finding |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Q1 | stdin payload format? | JSON object. 7-field schema for SessionStart (+ `source` enum: startup/resume/clear); 8-field schema for UserPromptSubmit (+ `turn_id`, `prompt`). `additionalProperties: false`. Serialized via `serde_json::to_string` in upstream source. | F1 |\n| Q2 | stdout → model context? | **YES.** Plain stdout OR JSON `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` becomes a `role: \"developer\"` message. SessionStart context before user prompt; UserPromptSubmit context after. | F2 |\n| Q3 | Exit-code semantics? | `0` = success + inject. `2` + stderr = UserPromptSubmit blocker (status: Blocked, should_stop: true). Other non-zero = fail-open (hook marked failed, turn continues). | F3 |\n| Q4 | Timeout behavior? | Per-hook `timeout` or `timeoutSec` field (seconds). Default 600s. On timeout: empty result, turn continues (fail-open for the hook). | F5 |\n| Q5 | Multi-hook ordering? | Concurrent via `join_all`, NOT serial. Aggregation preserves handler order for folding but execution is parallel. | F6 |\n| Q6 | Full event taxonomy? | 6 events: `SessionStart`, `PreToolUse`, `PermissionRequest`, `PostToolUse`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop`. Schema-backed. | F7 |\n| Q7 | Prompt transformation? | **No.** `UserPromptSubmit` output schema has no `updatedInput` field. Can inject additionalContext alongside prompt or block the prompt, but not replace it. | F4 |\n| Q8 | Env propagation? | Inherited from parent (no env_clear). `HOME`, `PATH` (Codex-augmented), `OPENAI_API_KEY`, custom env vars all visible. `shell_environment_policy` config governs shell tools, NOT hooks. | F8 |\n\n**Plus one un-questioned discovery**: **F9 — feature flag `codex_hooks = true` required.** Not in the original questions but material to Phase 2 implementation.\n\n---\n\n## 4. OUTCOME CLASSIFICATION — A (Full hook parity)\n\n**Classification**: **A** per `plan.md` §2 Phase 1 exit criteria.\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Transport exists ✓ (hooks.json registration model).\n- Schema known and stable ✓ (generated schemas in openai/codex repo).\n- Context injection into model is confirmed ✓ (developer-role message via `additional_contexts_for_model`; empirically observed).\n- Blocker pattern available ✓ (exit 2 for UserPromptSubmit).\n- Empirically reproducible on 0.122.0 ✓ (probe confirmed).\n\nThe only blocker is operational: the feature flag must be enabled. No code limitation, no API stability concern (docs mark hooks as experimental but all schemas are stable for 0.122.0 per the probed run).\n\n---\n\n## 5. DECISION MATRIX — Implementation Paths\n\n| # | Path | Feasibility | Eng. cost | Runtime cost | Coverage | Recommended |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| 1 | **Full native hook port**: mirror `hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.ts` to `hooks/codex/`, handle Codex-specific stdin/stdout schema, register in `~/.codex/hooks.json` alongside notify.sh, enable `codex_hooks = true` in `~/.codex/config.toml` | **HIGH** (contract proven) | Low-Medium (~2-3 days: port + test + register + user-facing docs) | Negligible per-event (one subprocess per hook invocation) | Full parity: SessionStart startup-context + UserPromptSubmit advisor brief, both injected as developer-role messages | **Yes — primary** |\n| 2 | Additional SessionStart hook for startup context (separate file from UserPromptSubmit) | High | Low (~1 day after option 1) | Negligible | Covers SessionStart startup payload | **Yes — part of option 1** |\n| 3 | File-based workaround via `AGENTS.md` (parallel to sibling phase 007's fallback) | High | Low (already exists via spec 046's voice content) | None | Static startup context only; doesn't solve per-prompt advisor brief | No — option 1 beats it cleanly for this runtime |\n| 4 | Shell wrapper around `codex exec -p` with prepended brief | Medium | Low | Per-call | Programmatic mode only | No — option 1 covers interactive TUI too |\n| 5 | Accept limitation, document only | Zero | Zero | None | Zero parity | No — Codex has real support, no reason to retreat |\n\n---\n\n## 6. RECOMMENDED IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (Phase 2)\n\n### Required environmental change (user-approved before Phase 2)\n\nAdd to `~/.codex/config.toml`:\n\n```toml\n[features]\ncodex_hooks = true\n```\n\n**Warning**: this will also activate the existing Superset `notify.sh` hook, which is currently inert. If the user doesn't want the notification side-effects from notify.sh, either (a) remove the Superset entries from `hooks.json` first, (b) modify notify.sh to no-op on Codex events, or (c) leave as-is if the notifications are desired.\n\n### Step-by-step\n\n1. **Verify feature flag decision**: confirm with user whether enabling `codex_hooks` is acceptable given notify.sh side-effects.\n2. **Create `hooks/codex/` directory** mirroring `hooks/claude/` structure.\n3. **Port `user-prompt-submit.ts`**:\n   - Read JSON from stdin (fields: `cwd`, `hook_event_name`, `model`, `permission_mode`, `prompt`, `session_id`, `transcript_path`, `turn_id`).\n   - Compute advisor brief (reuse existing Claude-side logic).\n   - Emit brief to stdout as plain text OR JSON `{hookSpecificOutput: {additionalContext: \"<brief>\"}}`.\n   - Exit 0.\n4. **Create `session-start.ts`**:\n   - Read JSON from stdin (fields: `cwd`, `hook_event_name`, `model`, `permission_mode`, `session_id`, `source`, `transcript_path`).\n   - Compute code-graph metrics (files, nodes, edges, freshness).\n   - Emit as plain text or `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`.\n   - Exit 0.\n5. **Register hooks** in `~/.codex/hooks.json` alongside notify.sh (back up first):\n   ```json\n   {\"hooks\": {\n     \"SessionStart\": [\n       {\"hooks\": [{\"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"/Users/.../notify.sh\"}]},\n       {\"hooks\": [{\"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"node /absolute/path/to/hooks/codex/session-start.js\"}]}\n     ],\n     \"UserPromptSubmit\": [\n       {\"hooks\": [{\"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"/Users/.../notify.sh\"}]},\n       {\"hooks\": [{\"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"node /absolute/path/to/hooks/codex/user-prompt-submit.js\"}]}\n     ],\n     \"Stop\": [\n       {\"hooks\": [{\"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"/Users/.../notify.sh\"}]}\n     ]\n   }}\n   ```\n6. **Write parity tests**: `tests/codex-user-prompt-submit-hook.vitest.ts` mirroring Claude's coverage.\n7. **Smoke test**: `codex` session, run the two empirical questions (\"what repository state was injected into your startup context?\" + \"what skill did the advisor recommend?\"). Expect non-empty answers mirroring Claude Code.\n8. **Verify Claude regression** (REQ-004): `vitest run claude-user-prompt-submit-hook.vitest.ts`.\n9. **Document in `cli-codex/SKILL.md`** + `README.md`: new section announcing hook parity shipped, reference this research.md for contract details.\n10. **Update parent `handover.md`** with phase outcome.\n\n### Estimated effort: 2-3 days\n\n---\n\n## 7. IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS\n\n### Idempotency (because hooks run concurrently)\n\nSince `notify.sh` and the new Spec Kit hook will run in parallel for the same event, the Spec Kit hook must:\n- Not race on shared files with notify.sh (different paths).\n- Be stdout-only (no side-effects like writing to disk that could conflict).\n- Complete fast enough to fit within the default 600s timeout with margin. Advisor brief generation is already sub-second for the Claude hook, so this is not a concern.\n\n### Timeout budget\n\nDefault 600s is generous. For the advisor-brief case (sub-second) we can leave default. For SessionStart (which might touch the code graph) set an explicit timeout of 30s as a safety net.\n\n### Env propagation\n\nHook scripts will inherit all parent env vars including `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `HOME`, `PATH`. That's fine — the Spec Kit hook needs `HOME` to find its own config and doesn't exfiltrate any secrets.\n\n### Blocker semantics\n\nThe UserPromptSubmit hook should NOT use exit 2 to block prompts (that's user-destructive). Always exit 0 with stdout; if the advisor has no recommendation to make, emit nothing or a single-line \"no recommendation\" string.\n\n### Unknown `additionalProperties`\n\nBecause `additionalProperties: false` in the stdin schemas, we cannot add custom fields to the input — Codex would reject on parse. The hook only has access to the documented fields (`prompt`, `session_id`, etc.). That's sufficient for advisor brief generation.\n\n---\n\n## 8. WHAT THIS DOES NOT SOLVE\n\n- **Pre-flag state**: users who haven't enabled `codex_hooks = true` get no hook behavior. Documented workaround: enable the flag.\n- **Docs flag as experimental**: Codex docs mark hooks experimental. Schema-level breakage could require future re-testing. Current 0.122.0 behavior is stable.\n- **UserPromptSubmit blocker could be abused**: we commit to never using exit 2 from the Spec Kit hook so we don't accidentally block the user's prompt on advisor failure.\n\n---\n\n## 9. METHODOLOGY NOTES (for future CLI-parity efforts)\n\nThe research methodology that worked — reusable for Gemini CLI or other runtimes:\n\n1. **Documentation layer**: `developers.openai.com/codex/hooks` as starting point.\n2. **Schema layer**: generated JSON schemas in the upstream repo (`codex-rs/hooks/schema/generated/`). Authoritative on wire format.\n3. **Source layer**: Rust source files (`command_runner.rs`, `dispatcher.rs`, event parsers). Authoritative on runtime behavior.\n4. **Empirical layer**: isolated probe with a throwaway `CODEX_HOME`. Verifies actual behavior on the installed binary version, catches doc/source drift.\n\nKey trick for this research: the codex agent itself running with `--sandbox workspace-write` could actually perform the empirical probe (iter 2: wrote probe scripts, captured stdin/stdout/env, validated against docs). That's a capability copilot couldn't match because copilot research was limited to documentation/repo search — no sandbox-safe probe execution.\n\n---\n\n## 10. CONVERGENCE REPORT\n\n- **Total iterations**: 10 (hit max; rolling avg at stop = 0.3833, still above 0.05 — but all 8 questions answered with primary-source citations, so additional iterations would diminish marginally)\n- **Stop reason**: max_iterations_reached (functional convergence reached by iter 8; iters 9-10 consolidated)\n- **Iteration ratio trajectory**: 0.90 → 0.70 → 0.45 → 0.35 → 0.55 → 0.20 → 0.15 → 0.35 → 0.45 → 0.35\n- **Status distribution**: 10 × `evidence` (vs copilot run's 9 × `insight`, 1 × `evidence` — Codex iterations treated every finding as verified from primary sources)\n- **Total findings**: 93 across 10 iterations (deduplicated into ~30 unique claims in this synthesis)\n- **Primary-source URLs**: 40+ distinct URLs across developers.openai.com/codex, openai/codex GitHub repo source files, generated schemas, upstream issues, Rust stdlib docs\n- **Empirical probe**: isolated `CODEX_HOME` probe on 0.122.0 verified stdin format, stdout injection, exit-code semantics, timeout behavior, and env propagation — without touching the real `~/.codex/hooks.json`\n\n---\n\n## 11. RELATED DOCUMENTS\n\n- **Spec**: `../../007-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/spec.md`\n- **Plan**: `../../007-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/plan.md`\n- **Tasks**: `../../007-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/tasks.md`\n- **Decisions**: `../../007-hook-parity/003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/decision-record.md` — ADR-003 populated from §5 above\n- **Sibling phase**: `../../007-hook-parity/002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/` — Copilot has NO dynamic hook-based injection (outcome B); this is the contrast case\n- **Reference implementation (Claude-side)**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.ts` — the implementation Phase 2 ports to `hooks/codex/`\n- **User's existing config**: `~/.codex/hooks.json` (notify.sh registered), `~/.codex/config.toml` (feature flag ABSENT — must be added in Phase 2)",
