.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:11054:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-003.out:7976:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:2788:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/gpt55/iterations/iteration-001.md:107:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:11196:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs:119, The salvage path writes recovered stdout and increments `salvaged` without calling `validateIterationOutputs` from .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/post-dispatch-validate.ts:515. That skips the declared post-dispatch checks for canonical JSONL, delta presence, executor provenance, and v2 review schema after recovery. Fix: run the validator before/after salvage and only count recovery as salvaged when the recovered artifact passes.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:11207:[{"id":"f-p123-i4-01","severity":"P0","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":106,"issue":"Salvage writes unpadded iteration filenames, while the command surface expects iteration-{NNN}.md, so recovered files are written to paths downstream validation and merge do not consume.","fix":"Format iteration ids with the shared three-digit NNN resolver or pass the resolved iteration path into salvage."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-02","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":119,"issue":"Recovered stdout is written and counted as salvaged without reusing validateIterationOutputs, skipping canonical JSONL, delta, executor provenance, and v2 schema checks after recovery.","fix":"Run post-dispatch validation before and after salvage; increment salvaged only after the recovered artifact validates."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":103,"issue":"Recovered text is extracted once from whole-lineage stdout and reused for every missing iteration, so multiple missing files can receive the same transcript instead of per-iteration output.","fix":"Recover from per-iteration logs or split stdout by iteration before writing each iteration artifact."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-04","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":48,"issue":"JSON text parts and raw stdout are accepted/truncated without a substantive-content or truncation check, allowing whitespace-only or partial markdown to become a non-empty successful artifact.","fix":"Require trimmed substantive content, surface truncation as degraded/failure, and make validation reject salvage-failure or truncated placeholders."}]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:11219:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs:119, The salvage path writes recovered stdout and increments `salvaged` without calling `validateIterationOutputs` from .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/post-dispatch-validate.ts:515. That skips the declared post-dispatch checks for canonical JSONL, delta presence, executor provenance, and v2 review schema after recovery. Fix: run the validator before/after salvage and only count recovery as salvaged when the recovered artifact passes.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:11230:[{"id":"f-p123-i4-01","severity":"P0","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":106,"issue":"Salvage writes unpadded iteration filenames, while the command surface expects iteration-{NNN}.md, so recovered files are written to paths downstream validation and merge do not consume.","fix":"Format iteration ids with the shared three-digit NNN resolver or pass the resolved iteration path into salvage."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-02","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":119,"issue":"Recovered stdout is written and counted as salvaged without reusing validateIterationOutputs, skipping canonical JSONL, delta, executor provenance, and v2 schema checks after recovery.","fix":"Run post-dispatch validation before and after salvage; increment salvaged only after the recovered artifact validates."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":103,"issue":"Recovered text is extracted once from whole-lineage stdout and reused for every missing iteration, so multiple missing files can receive the same transcript instead of per-iteration output.","fix":"Recover from per-iteration logs or split stdout by iteration before writing each iteration artifact."},{"id":"f-p123-i4-04","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","line":48,"issue":"JSON text parts and raw stdout are accepted/truncated without a substantive-content or truncation check, allowing whitespace-only or partial markdown to become a non-empty successful artifact.","fix":"Require trimmed substantive content, surface truncation as degraded/failure, and make validation reject salvage-failure or truncated placeholders."}]

     1	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Lock
     2	
     3	import { closeSync, existsSync, fsyncSync, openSync, readFileSync, renameSync, rmSync, unlinkSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
     4	import { dirname } from 'node:path';
     5	
     6	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
     7	
     8	import type { ExecutorKind } from './executor-config.js';
     9	
    10	export interface LoopLockData {
    11	  ownerPid: number;
    12	  startedAtIso: string;
    13	  ttlMs: number;
    14	  lastHeartbeatIso: string;
    15	  packetId: string;
    16	  runtimeKind: ExecutorKind | 'main';
    17	}
    18	
    19	export type LoopLockAcquireResult =
    20	  | { acquired: true; lock: LoopLockData; reclaimed?: LoopLockData }
    21	  | { acquired: false; holder: LoopLockData };
    22	
    23	// ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
    24	
    25	export class LoopLockHeldError extends Error {
    26	  holder: LoopLockData;
    27	
    28	  constructor(holder: LoopLockData) {
    29	    super(`Deep-loop lock is held by pid ${holder.ownerPid} for packet ${holder.packetId}`);
    30	    this.name = 'LoopLockHeldError';
    31	    this.holder = holder;
    32	  }
    33	}
    34	
    35	// ───── HELPERS ─────
    36	
    37	type SerializedLoopLockData = {
    38	  owner_pid: number;
    39	  started_at_iso: string;
    40	  ttl_ms: number;
    41	  last_heartbeat_iso: string;
    42	  packet_id: string;
    43	  runtime_kind: ExecutorKind | 'main';
    44	};
    45	
    46	function fsyncPath(path: string): void {
    47	  let fd: number | undefined;
    48	  try {
    49	    fd = openSync(path, 'r');
    50	    fsyncSync(fd);
    51	  } finally {
    52	    if (typeof fd === 'number') {
    53	      closeSync(fd);
    54	    }
    55	  }
    56	}
    57	
    58	function makeTempPath(targetPath: string): string {
    59	  return `${targetPath}.tmp.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}.${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
    60	}
    61	
    62	function serializeLock(data: LoopLockData): SerializedLoopLockData {
    63	  return {
    64	    owner_pid: data.ownerPid,
    65	    started_at_iso: data.startedAtIso,
    66	    ttl_ms: data.ttlMs,
    67	    last_heartbeat_iso: data.lastHeartbeatIso,
    68	    packet_id: data.packetId,
    69	    runtime_kind: data.runtimeKind,
    70	  };
    71	}
    72	
    73	function deserializeLock(raw: unknown): LoopLockData | null {
    74	  if (raw === null || typeof raw !== 'object' || Array.isArray(raw)) {
    75	    return null;
    76	  }
    77	
    78	  const candidate = raw as Partial<SerializedLoopLockData>;
    79	  if (
    80	    !Number.isInteger(candidate.owner_pid) ||
    81	    typeof candidate.started_at_iso !== 'string' ||
    82	    !Number.isInteger(candidate.ttl_ms) ||
    83	    typeof candidate.last_heartbeat_iso !== 'string' ||
    84	    typeof candidate.packet_id !== 'string' ||
    85	    typeof candidate.runtime_kind !== 'string'
    86	  ) {
    87	    return null;
    88	  }
    89	
    90	  const ownerPid = candidate.owner_pid as number;
    91	  const ttlMs = candidate.ttl_ms as number;
    92	
    93	  return {
    94	    ownerPid,
    95	    startedAtIso: candidate.started_at_iso,
    96	    ttlMs,
    97	    lastHeartbeatIso: candidate.last_heartbeat_iso,
    98	    packetId: candidate.packet_id,
    99	    runtimeKind: candidate.runtime_kind as ExecutorKind | 'main',
   100	  };
   101	}
   102	
   103	function readLoopLock(lockPath: string): LoopLockData | null {
   104	  try {
   105	    return deserializeLock(JSON.parse(readFileSync(lockPath, 'utf8')));
   106	  } catch {
   107	    return null;
   108	  }
   109	}
   110	
   111	function writeLoopLockAtomic(lockPath: string, data: LoopLockData): void {
   112	  const tempPath = makeTempPath(lockPath);
   113	  try {
   114	    writeFileSync(tempPath, `${JSON.stringify(serializeLock(data), null, 2)}\n`, { encoding: 'utf8', flag: 'wx' });
   115	    fsyncPath(tempPath);
   116	    renameSync(tempPath, lockPath);
   117	    try {
   118	      fsyncPath(dirname(lockPath));
   119	    } catch {
   120	    }
   121	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   122	    rmSync(tempPath, { force: true });
   123	    throw error;
   124	  }
   125	}
   126	
   127	function writeLoopLockExclusive(lockPath: string, data: LoopLockData): boolean {
   128	  let fd: number | undefined;
   129	  try {
   130	    fd = openSync(lockPath, 'wx');
   131	    writeFileSync(fd, `${JSON.stringify(serializeLock(data), null, 2)}\n`, { encoding: 'utf8' });
   132	    fsyncSync(fd);
   133	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   134	    const code = error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error ? (error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code : undefined;
   135	    if (code === 'EEXIST') {
   136	      return false;
   137	    }
   138	    throw error;
   139	  } finally {
   140	    if (typeof fd === 'number') {
   141	      closeSync(fd);
   142	    }
   143	  }
   144	
   145	  try {
   146	    fsyncPath(dirname(lockPath));
   147	  } catch {
   148	  }
   149	  return true;
   150	}
   151	
   152	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   153	
   154	/**
   155	 * Check whether a process is still alive.
   156	 *
   157	 * Uses kill(pid, 0) as a POSIX signal check. Returns true for
   158	 * processes the current user has permission to signal.
   159	 *
   160	 * @param pid - Process ID to check.
   161	 * @returns True if the process exists and is reachable.
   162	 */
   163	export function processAlive(pid: number): boolean {
   164	  if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) {
   165	    return false;
   166	  }
   167	
   168	  try {
   169	    process.kill(pid, 0);
   170	    return true;
   171	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   172	    if (error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error) {
   173	      if (error.code === 'ESRCH') return false;
   174	      if (error.code === 'EPERM') return true;
   175	    }
   176	    return true;
   177	  }
   178	}
   179	
   180	/**
   181	 * Determine whether a loop lock has expired or the owner process is dead.
   182	 *
   183	 * A lock is stale if the owner PID no longer exists or the heartbeat
   184	 * has exceeded twice the TTL.
   185	 *
   186	 * @param data - The loop lock data to check.
   187	 * @param now - Reference timestamp (defaults to now).
   188	 * @returns True if the lock is stale and can be reclaimed.
   189	 */
   190	export function isStaleLoopLock(data: LoopLockData, now: Date = new Date()): boolean {
   191	  const heartbeatMs = Date.parse(data.lastHeartbeatIso);
   192	  const ttlMs = Number.isFinite(data.ttlMs) ? data.ttlMs : 0;
   193	  const expired = !Number.isFinite(heartbeatMs) || now.getTime() - heartbeatMs > ttlMs * 2;
   194	
   195	  return expired || !processAlive(data.ownerPid);
   196	}
   197	
   198	/**
   199	 * Acquire the deep-loop lock, reclaiming stale locks automatically.
   200	 *
   201	 * @param lockPath - File path for the lock file.
   202	 * @param data - Lock metadata for the acquiring process.
   203	 * @returns Result indicating whether the lock was acquired and by whom.
   204	 */
   205	export function acquireLoopLock(lockPath: string, data: LoopLockData): LoopLockAcquireResult {
   206	  const holder = existsSync(lockPath) ? readLoopLock(lockPath) : null;
   207	  if (holder && !isStaleLoopLock(holder)) {
   208	    return { acquired: false, holder };
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  if (!holder) {
   212	    if (writeLoopLockExclusive(lockPath, data)) {
   213	      return { acquired: true, lock: data };
   214	    }
   215	    const current = readLoopLock(lockPath);
   216	    return { acquired: false, holder: current ?? data };
   217	  }
   218	
   219	  writeLoopLockAtomic(lockPath, data);
   220	  return holder ? { acquired: true, lock: data, reclaimed: holder } : { acquired: true, lock: data };
   221	}
   222	
   223	/**
   224	 * Refresh the loop lock heartbeat for the owning process.
   225	 *
   226	 * @param lockPath - File path for the lock file.
   227	 * @param ownerPid - PID of the owning process (must match current owner).
   228	 * @param now - Reference timestamp (defaults to now).
   229	 * @returns True if the heartbeat was successfully refreshed.
   230	 */
   231	export function refreshLoopLock(lockPath: string, ownerPid: number, now: Date = new Date()): boolean {
   232	  const holder = existsSync(lockPath) ? readLoopLock(lockPath) : null;
   233	  if (!holder || holder.ownerPid !== ownerPid) {
   234	    return false;
   235	  }
   236	
   237	  writeLoopLockAtomic(lockPath, {
   238	    ...holder,
   239	    lastHeartbeatIso: now.toISOString(),
   240	  });
   241	  return true;
   242	}
   243	
   244	/**
   245	 * Release the loop lock for the owning process.
   246	 *
   247	 * @param lockPath - File path for the lock file.
   248	 * @param ownerPid - PID of the owning process (must match current owner).
   249	 * @returns True if the lock was successfully released.
   250	 */
   251	export function releaseLoopLock(lockPath: string, ownerPid: number): boolean {
   252	  const holder = existsSync(lockPath) ? readLoopLock(lockPath) : null;
   253	  if (!holder || holder.ownerPid !== ownerPid) {
   254	    return false;
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  unlinkSync(lockPath);
   258	  return true;
   259	}

codex
## Focus
tests + security

## Findings
P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:341, `spawnSync` runs inside the pool worker, so each "async" worker blocks the event loop before the next lineage can be admitted; the fan-out pool tests use a cooperative async worker and do not prove production CLI lineages overlap. Fix: replace this with async `spawn`/`execFile` and add a fanout-run overlap test with two sleeping stubs.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:341, The direct `spawnSync` path bypasses the audited async supervisor's detached process-group timeout handling, so a timed-out lineage can leave child/grandchild processes behind. Fix: route fanout CLI execution through the existing audited async runner or duplicate its detached process-group kill/escalation behavior.

P1, .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:150, `{config.fanout_json}` is interpolated into a shell command inside single quotes before `fanout-run.cjs` can validate it; executor model strings are not quote-restricted, so a single quote in JSON can break out into shell syntax. Fix: pass fanout config via a temp JSON file, base64/stdin, or an argv-safe Node wrapper instead of shell interpolation.

P1, .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:121, `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` is bound directly as `artifact_dir` with no normalization or containment check, so a malformed override can escape the intended packet lineage tree. Fix: resolve the override and reject any path outside the expected `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}` root.

## Verdict
Core fan-out behavior and security hardening need fixes before this dimension can pass.
Review verdict: FAIL
